w2660.en: Crowds began to **loot** the shops during the chaos of the riot.
w2660.zh: 在暴動的混亂中，群眾開始搶劫商店。

w2661.en: Our **loquacious** guide chatted cheerfully throughout the entire museum tour.
w2661.zh: 我們健談的導遊在整趟博物館導覽中都愉快地閒聊著。

w2662.en: The arrogant young **lordling** demanded respect he had done nothing to earn.
w2662.zh: 那個傲慢的小貴族要求得到他絲毫未付出努力贏取的尊敬。

w2663.en: Fishermen returned at dusk across the calm waters of the **lough**.
w2663.zh: 漁夫們在黃昏時分越過湖泊平靜的水面返航。

w2664.en: The nurse explained how to treat a single **louse** found in the child's hair.
w2664.zh: 護士說明了如何處理在孩子頭髮中發現的一隻蝨子。

w2665.en: Their new puppy was such a **lovable** little creature that everyone adored it.
w2665.zh: 他們的新小狗是個如此可愛的小傢伙，人人都喜歡牠。

w2666.en: After failing the exam, she felt **low-spirited** for several quiet days.
w2666.zh: 考試不及格後，她有好幾天都默默地感到消沉。

w2667.en: He started in a **lowly** position but eventually rose to become director.
w2667.zh: 他從一個卑微的職位起步，最終晉升為主管。

w2668.en: The professor gave a remarkably **lucid** explanation of a very difficult concept.
w2668.zh: 教授對一個非常困難的概念給出了異常清晰的解釋。

w2669.en: Online tutoring has become a surprisingly **lucrative** business for many graduates.
w2669.zh: 線上家教對許多畢業生來說已成為一門出人意料地賺錢的生意。

w2670.en: It seemed utterly **ludicrous** to drive two hours for a single cup of coffee.
w2670.zh: 為了一杯咖啡開兩小時的車，這似乎完全愚蠢可笑。

w2671.en: She is regarded as a leading **luminary** of contemporary medical research.
w2671.zh: 她被視為當代醫學研究的一位頂尖知名人士。

w2672.en: Deep-sea creatures often have **luminescent** organs that glow in total darkness.
w2672.zh: 深海生物常擁有能在完全黑暗中發光的發光器官。

w2673.en: The faint **luminescence** of the fungi lit the forest floor at night.
w2673.zh: 真菌微弱的冷光在夜裡照亮了森林的地面。

w2674.en: Astronomers measured the **luminosity** of the distant star with great precision.
w2674.zh: 天文學家以極高的精確度測量了那顆遙遠恆星的光度。

w2675.en: The watch has **luminous** hands that remain clearly visible in the dark.
w2675.zh: 這支手錶有著夜光指針，在黑暗中仍清晰可見。

w2676.en: Spending the entire savings on lottery tickets was sheer **lunacy**.
w2676.zh: 把全部積蓄花在彩券上純粹是愚蠢的行為。

w2677.en: Many ancient festivals were timed according to the **lunar** calendar.
w2677.zh: 許多古老的節慶都是依照陰曆來訂定時間的。

w2678.en: He drove like a complete **lunatic**, terrifying everyone in the car.
w2678.zh: 他開車像個十足的瘋子，把車裡的每個人都嚇壞了。

w2679.en: The pastry chef cut the dough into a delicate **lune** shape.
w2679.zh: 糕點師傅把麵團切成精緻的月牙形。

w2680.en: The tabloid published **lurid** details of the scandal to attract readers.
w2680.zh: 那家小報刊登了這樁醜聞聳人聽聞的細節以吸引讀者。

w2681.en: We enjoyed a bowl of **luscious** ripe peaches fresh from the orchard.
w2681.zh: 我們享用了一碗剛從果園採摘、香甜多汁的成熟桃子。

w2682.en: Her **lustrous** dark hair shone beautifully under the bright stage lights.
w2682.zh: 她那閃亮的黑髮在明亮的舞台燈光下美麗地閃耀著。

w2683.en: The garden was famous for the **luxuriance** of its summer roses.
w2683.zh: 這座花園以其夏日玫瑰的繁茂而聞名。

w2684.en: Thick, **luxuriant** vegetation covered every slope of the tropical valley.
w2684.zh: 濃密繁茂的植被覆蓋了這片熱帶山谷的每一道斜坡。

w2685.en: After the exams, she planned to **luxuriate** in a long, warm bath.
w2685.zh: 考試結束後，她計畫盡情享受一場悠長溫暖的泡澡。

w2686.en: The detective soon exposed the witness's repeated **lying** about that evening.
w2686.zh: 偵探很快就揭穿了證人關於那晚一再說謊的事。

w2687.en: The musician gently plucked the ancient **lyre** during the quiet ceremony.
w2687.zh: 樂師在這場寧靜的儀式中輕柔地撥動著古老的里拉琴。

w2688.en: The poet was admired for the gentle **lyric** quality of her verse.
w2688.zh: 這位詩人因其詩句溫柔的抒情特質而備受讚賞。

w2689.en: Workers were hired to **macadamize** the rough country road last summer.
w2689.zh: 去年夏天，工人們被雇來在這條崎嶇的鄉間道路上鋪設碎石。

w2690.en: The factory installed expensive new **machinery** to increase its production speed.
w2690.zh: 這家工廠安裝了昂貴的新機器以提升其生產速度。

w2691.en: A skilled **machinist** carefully shaped each metal part by hand.
w2691.zh: 一位技術熟練的機械師用手仔細地塑造每一個金屬零件。

w2692.en: Philosophers once viewed the human body as a tiny **macrocosm** of the universe.
w2692.zh: 哲學家曾將人體視為宇宙的一個微小的宏觀世界。

w2693.en: Constant interruptions during the meeting began to **madden** the impatient director.
w2693.zh: 會議中不斷的打斷開始激怒了那位沒耐性的主管。

w2694.en: The chapel contained a beautiful painting of the **Madonna** holding her child.
w2694.zh: 這間小禮拜堂裡有一幅美麗的聖母瑪利亞抱著聖嬰的畫作。

w2695.en: The clever **magician** made the entire audience gasp with his final trick.
w2695.zh: 這位聰明的魔術師用他最後的戲法讓全場觀眾倒抽一口氣。

w2696.en: The senior judge spoke in a calm, **magisterial** tone that silenced the room.
w2696.zh: 資深法官以一種沉著、有絕對權威的語氣說話，使全場為之肅靜。

w2697.en: He was appointed to the local **magistracy** after years of legal service.
w2697.zh: 在多年的法律工作之後，他被任命擔任當地的法官職位。

w2698.en: The victorious general was **magnanimous** towards the soldiers he had defeated.
w2698.zh: 這位獲勝的將軍對他所擊敗的士兵展現了寬宏大量。

w2699.en: The shipping **magnate** owned a fleet of vessels across three continents.
w2699.zh: 這位航運大亨擁有一支橫跨三大洲的船隊。

w2700.en: The child used a small **magnet** to pick up the scattered iron nails.
w2700.zh: 那個孩子用一塊小磁鐵把散落的鐵釘吸了起來。

w2701.en: You can **magnetize** a needle by rubbing it against a strong magnet.
w2701.zh: 你可以把針在強磁鐵上摩擦來使它磁化。

w2702.en: Visitors were overwhelmed by the sheer **magnificence** of the marble palace.
w2702.zh: 訪客們為這座大理石宮殿的壯麗而深深折服。

w2703.en: From the summit we enjoyed a truly **magnificent** view of the valley.
w2703.zh: 從山頂上，我們欣賞到山谷真正壯麗的景色。

w2704.en: Few people fully understood the **magnitude** of the approaching financial crisis.
w2704.zh: 很少有人完全理解這場逼近的金融危機的嚴重性。

w2705.en: The palace was once home to a wealthy Indian **maharaja**.
w2705.zh: 這座宮殿曾是一位富有的印度土邦主的居所。

w2706.en: In that era, a woman's **maidenhood** ended only with her marriage.
w2706.zh: 在那個時代，女子的處女時代只有在結婚時才會結束。

w2707.en: Regular exercise helps people **maintain** good health well into old age.
w2707.zh: 規律的運動幫助人們直到年老都維持良好的健康。

w2708.en: The bridge requires constant **maintenance** to remain safe for daily traffic.
w2708.zh: 這座橋需要持續的維護才能保持對日常交通的安全。

w2709.en: Farmers across the region planted vast fields of golden **maize**.
w2709.zh: 這個地區的農民種植了大片金黃色的玉米田。

w2710.en: She rarely wears any **makeup**, preferring a simple and natural look.
w2710.zh: 她很少化妝，偏好簡單自然的造型。

w2711.en: The doctors struggled to identify the rare **malady** affecting the young patient.
w2711.zh: 醫生們努力辨識出影響這名年輕病人的罕見疾病。

w2712.en: Travellers to tropical regions should take precautions against **malaria**.
w2712.zh: 前往熱帶地區的旅客應採取預防瘧疾的措施。

w2713.en: A small group of **malcontents** stirred up trouble among the otherwise content workers.
w2713.zh: 一小群不滿者在原本心滿意足的工人之間挑起了事端。

w2714.en: The old witch was said to mutter a terrible **malediction** against her enemies.
w2714.zh: 據說那個老巫婆會對她的敵人喃喃唸出可怕的詛咒。

w2715.en: The court sentenced the **malefactor** to ten years of hard labour.
w2715.zh: 法院判處這名作惡者十年的苦役。

w2716.en: The villagers feared the **maleficent** spirit said to haunt the dark woods.
w2716.zh: 村民們害怕據說在幽暗森林中作祟的那個作惡的鬼魂。

w2717.en: His eyes were full of **malevolence** as he plotted his cruel revenge.
w2717.zh: 當他策劃他殘忍的復仇時，他的雙眼充滿了惡意。

w2718.en: The novel's **malevolent** villain delighted in causing misery to others.
w2718.zh: 小說中那個惡毒的反派以給他人帶來痛苦為樂。

w2719.en: Rivals tried to **malign** her reputation by spreading damaging untrue stories.
w2719.zh: 競爭對手試圖透過散布有害的不實傳言來中傷她的名聲。

w2720.en: Doctors removed the **malignant** tumour before it could spread further.
w2720.zh: 醫生在惡性腫瘤進一步擴散之前將它切除了。

w2721.en: Hot metal becomes soft and **malleable**, allowing the smith to shape it.
w2721.zh: 高溫的金屬會變得柔軟而具延展性，讓鐵匠得以塑造它。

w2722.en: He used a wooden **mallet** to tap the tent pegs into the ground.
w2722.zh: 他用一支木製大頭槌把帳篷釘輕敲進地裡。

w2723.en: Laws exist to protect animals from owners who **maltreat** them.
w2723.zh: 法律的存在是為了保護動物免受虐待牠們的飼主所害。

w2724.en: The unstable cliff path was a genuine **man-trap** for unwary hikers.
w2724.zh: 那條不穩固的懸崖小徑對粗心的登山者來說是個真正的危險場所。

w2725.en: The new council received a clear **mandate** from voters to reform the schools.
w2725.zh: 新的議會從選民那裡獲得了改革學校的明確授權。

w2726.en: Wearing a seatbelt is **mandatory** for all passengers in this country.
w2726.zh: 在這個國家，所有乘客繫安全帶都是強制性的。

w2727.en: The wild horse shook its thick black **mane** as it galloped away.
w2727.zh: 那匹野馬一邊疾馳而去，一邊甩動著牠濃密的黑色鬃毛。

w2728.en: Local legends warned sailors about a fierce **man-eater** lurking in the deep waters.
w2728.zh: 當地的傳說警告水手們提防潛伏在深水中的兇猛吃人動物。

w2729.en: The pilot skilfully **maneuvered** the small plane through the narrow valley.
w2729.zh: 飛行員巧妙地操縱著這架小飛機穿越狹窄的山谷。

w2730.en: His sudden **mania** for cycling led him to buy three expensive bikes.
w2730.zh: 他對騎自行車突如其來的狂熱促使他買了三輛昂貴的自行車。

w2731.en: A devoted film **maniac**, he watches at least four movies every week.
w2731.zh: 作為一個狂熱的電影迷，他每週至少看四部電影。

w2732.en: The party published its election **manifesto** outlining its main policies.
w2732.zh: 該政黨發表了概述其主要政策的競選宣言。

w2733.en: The statue depicted a tall, **manlike** figure carved from solid granite.
w2733.zh: 這座雕像描繪了一個由堅實花崗岩雕成、高大而有男子氣概的人像。

w2734.en: In that culture, courage was seen as the truest sign of **manliness**.
w2734.zh: 在那種文化中，勇氣被視為男子氣概最真實的標誌。

w2735.en: Tapping his pen was a nervous **mannerism** he displayed during interviews.
w2735.zh: 輕敲他的筆是他在面試時表現出的一種緊張的習慣性動作。

w2736.en: The wealthy family lived in a grand **manor** surrounded by ancient oak trees.
w2736.zh: 這個富裕的家庭住在一座被古老橡樹環繞的宏偉莊園宅第裡。

w2737.en: Old family photographs stood proudly along the dusty wooden **mantel**.
w2737.zh: 舊的家族照片驕傲地排列在積滿灰塵的木製壁爐架上。

w2738.en: Upon her retirement, the **mantle** of leadership passed to her young deputy.
w2738.zh: 在她退休後，領導的衣缽傳給了她年輕的副手。

w2739.en: The car **manufacturer** recalled thousands of vehicles to fix a safety fault.
w2739.zh: 這家汽車製造商召回了數千輛車以修正一項安全瑕疵。

w2740.en: The new law granted **manumission** to slaves who had served for many years.
w2740.zh: 這項新法給予了已服役多年的奴隸解放。

w2741.en: The kind master decided to **manumit** the loyal servant after long service.
w2741.zh: 這位仁慈的主人決定在僕人長期服役後解放這名忠誠的奴隸。

w2742.en: Pollution poses a serious threat to fragile **marine** ecosystems worldwide.
w2742.zh: 污染對全球脆弱的海洋生態系構成嚴重威脅。

w2743.en: The nation's wealth long depended on its busy **maritime** trade routes.
w2743.zh: 這個國家的財富長久以來都仰賴其繁忙的海運貿易路線。

w2744.en: A sudden storm left the sailors **marooned** on a tiny deserted island.
w2744.zh: 一場突如其來的風暴將水手們困在了一座無人的小島上。

w2745.en: The general declared **martial** law after violence broke out in the capital.
w2745.zh: 在首都爆發暴力事件後，將軍宣布實施戒嚴（軍事管制）。

w2746.en: The film featured friendly **Martian** visitors who came to study planet Earth.
w2746.zh: 這部電影描述了友善的火星訪客，他們前來研究地球。

w2747.en: The saint's **martyrdom** inspired countless followers throughout the following centuries.
w2747.zh: 這位聖人的殉難在隨後的數個世紀裡啟發了無數追隨者。

w2748.en: Visitors **marvel** at the intricate carvings covering the ancient temple walls.
w2748.zh: 訪客們對覆蓋在古廟牆上的精細雕刻感到驚歎。

w2749.en: The cathedral's solid stone **masonry** has survived for over six centuries.
w2749.zh: 這座大教堂堅固的石造砌築已歷經六個多世紀而屹立不搖。

w2750.en: His friendly smile was merely a **masquerade** hiding his true intentions.
w2750.zh: 他友善的微笑只不過是掩飾他真實意圖的偽裝。

w2751.en: Historians still debate the causes of the brutal **massacre** that shocked the entire region.
w2751.zh: 歷史學家至今仍爭論那場震驚整個地區的殘酷**大屠殺**的起因。

w2752.en: The new stadium is a **massive** structure that can hold over eighty thousand spectators.
w2752.zh: 這座新體育場是一棟**巨大的**建築，可以容納超過八萬名觀眾。

w2753.en: Critics regard this novel as a **masterpiece** of twentieth-century literature.
w2753.zh: 評論家將這部小說視為二十世紀文學的**傑作**。

w2754.en: Her complete **mastery** of three languages impressed every interviewer on the panel.
w2754.zh: 她對三種語言的完全**精通**令面試小組的每位面試官印象深刻。

w2755.en: The factory imports raw **material** from several countries to make its products.
w2755.zh: 這家工廠從幾個國家進口原**料**來製造產品。

w2756.en: The promised funding never **materialized**, so the community project was eventually cancelled.
w2756.zh: 承諾的資金始終沒有**實現**，因此社區計畫最終被取消了。

w2757.en: Doctors stress the importance of good **maternal** health during the early months of pregnancy.
w2757.zh: 醫生強調懷孕初期幾個月良好**母親**健康的重要性。

w2758.en: We bought cheaper tickets for the Saturday **matinee** rather than the evening show.
w2758.zh: 我們買了較便宜的週六**日場**門票，而不是晚場的票。

w2759.en: The ancient tragedy explores the guilt of a son who commits **matricide**.
w2759.zh: 這齣古老的悲劇探討了一個犯下**弒母罪**的兒子的罪惡感。

w2760.en: In that era, **matrimony** was often arranged by families rather than chosen freely.
w2760.zh: 在那個年代，**婚姻**往往由家庭安排，而非自由選擇。

w2761.en: A supportive school environment can be the **matrix** in which young talent develops.
w2761.zh: 一個支持性的學校環境可以成為年輕才能得以發展的**條件**。

w2762.en: As a **matter of fact**, the deadline had already passed before we even started.
w2762.zh: **事實上**，在我們開始之前期限就已經過了。

w2763.en: After a few drinks he became **maudlin** and started weeping about his lost youth.
w2763.zh: 喝了幾杯後，他變得**感情脆弱**，開始為逝去的青春哭泣。

w2764.en: Tourists queued for hours to enter the marble **mausoleum** of the ancient emperor.
w2764.zh: 遊客排了好幾個小時的隊，只為進入那位古代皇帝的大理石**陵墓**。

w2765.en: The film's **mawkish** ending felt forced and left many viewers rolling their eyes.
w2765.zh: 這部電影**多愁善感的**結局顯得做作，讓許多觀眾翻白眼。

w2766.en: My grandmother often repeated the **maxim** that honesty is always the best policy.
w2766.zh: 我祖母常重複那句**格言**：誠實永遠是上策。

w2767.en: The old town is a confusing **maze** of narrow streets and hidden courtyards.
w2767.zh: 這座古鎮是由狹窄街道和隱蔽庭院構成的令人困惑的**迷宮**。

w2768.en: At the medieval festival, visitors could sample honey **mead** brewed the traditional way.
w2768.zh: 在中世紀慶典上，遊客可以品嚐用傳統方法釀造的蜂蜜**酒**。

w2769.en: The refugees survived for weeks on a **meager** supply of rice and water.
w2769.zh: 難民們靠著**很少的**米和水的供給撐過了好幾週。

w2770.en: Voters are tired of **mealy-mouthed** politicians who never give a straight answer.
w2770.zh: 選民厭倦了那些從不直接回答的**說話拐彎抹角的**政客。

w2771.en: The river **meanders** gently through the green valley before reaching the sea.
w2771.zh: 這條河流平緩地**蜿蜒**穿過綠色山谷，最後流入大海。

w2772.en: The **mechanic** quickly diagnosed the strange noise coming from the car engine.
w2772.zh: 那位**技師**很快診斷出汽車引擎發出的怪聲。

w2773.en: The athlete proudly wore the gold **medallion** she had won at the championship.
w2773.zh: 這位運動員自豪地戴著她在錦標賽贏得的金**獎牌**。

w2774.en: Their **meddlesome** neighbour constantly offers unwanted advice about how to raise their children.
w2774.zh: 他們那位**愛管閒事的**鄰居不斷給出關於如何教養孩子的多餘建議。

w2775.en: The surgeon made a small incision on the **medial** side of the knee.
w2775.zh: 外科醫生在膝蓋的**內側**做了一個小切口。

w2776.en: The United Nations agreed to **mediate** between the two warring nations.
w2776.zh: 聯合國同意在兩個交戰國之間進行**調解**。

w2777.en: She studied **medicine** for six years before becoming a qualified doctor.
w2777.zh: 她學了六年**醫學**才成為合格的醫生。

w2778.en: The cathedral is a stunning example of **medieval** architecture from the twelfth century.
w2778.zh: 這座大教堂是十二世紀**中世紀**建築的驚人範例。

w2779.en: The restaurant's food was disappointingly **mediocre**, considering its expensive reputation.
w2779.zh: 考慮到它昂貴的名聲，這家餐廳的食物令人失望地**平庸**。

w2780.en: Daily **meditation** helps her stay calm and focused throughout her busy workday.
w2780.zh: 每日的**冥想**幫助她在忙碌的工作日中保持平靜與專注。

w2781.en: The band ended their concert with a lively **medley** of their greatest hits.
w2781.zh: 樂團以一首歡快的暢銷金曲**集成曲**結束了他們的演唱會。

w2782.en: The new policy was designed to **meliorate** the harsh living conditions of the poor.
w2782.zh: 這項新政策旨在**改善**窮人惡劣的生活條件。

w2783.en: The singer's **mellifluous** voice filled the hall and moved the audience to tears.
w2783.zh: 歌手**甜美圓潤的**嗓音充滿了大廳，感動得觀眾落淚。

w2784.en: A **melodious** tune drifted from the open window of the music room.
w2784.zh: 一段**旋律優美的**曲調從音樂教室敞開的窗戶飄了出來。

w2785.en: The play turned into an absurd **melodrama** full of exaggerated emotions and sudden twists.
w2785.zh: 這齣戲變成了一場荒謬的**情節劇**，充滿誇張的情感和突然的轉折。

w2786.en: She kept the seashell as a **memento** of their summer by the sea.
w2786.zh: 她把那枚貝殼留作他們海邊夏日的**紀念品**。

w2787.en: Our trip to the mountains was a truly **memorable** experience for the whole family.
w2787.zh: 我們的山區之旅對全家人來說是一次真正**難忘的**經歷。

w2788.en: Reckless drivers are a serious **menace** to pedestrians on these busy roads.
w2788.zh: 魯莽的駕駛者對這些繁忙道路上的行人構成嚴重的**威脅**。

w2789.en: The eccentric collector kept a private **menagerie** of exotic birds and reptiles.
w2789.zh: 這位古怪的收藏家飼養了一群私人的異國鳥類和爬蟲類**野生動物**。

w2790.en: The court rejected his **mendacious** testimony after finding clear contradictions in it.
w2790.zh: 法庭在發現明顯矛盾後，駁回了他**虛假的**證詞。

w2791.en: A ragged **mendicant** sat outside the temple, quietly asking passers-by for coins.
w2791.zh: 一個衣衫襤褸的**乞丐**坐在寺廟外，靜靜地向路人討錢。

w2792.en: The competitive **mentality** of the team drove everyone to train harder each day.
w2792.zh: 球隊好勝的**心態**驅使每個人每天更努力訓練。

w2793.en: A good **mentor** can guide young employees through the early challenges of their careers.
w2793.zh: 一位好的**導師**能引導年輕員工度過職業生涯早期的挑戰。

w2794.en: The city grew rich during the medieval period thanks to its busy **mercantile** trade.
w2794.zh: 這座城市在中世紀時期因其繁忙的**商業**貿易而變得富裕。

w2795.en: His decisions seemed purely **mercenary**, motivated only by personal profit.
w2795.zh: 他的決定似乎純粹是**唯利是圖的**，只受個人利益驅動。

w2796.en: The judge was **merciful** and gave the young offender a second chance.
w2796.zh: 法官很**寬大**，給了這名年輕罪犯第二次機會。

w2797.en: The critics were **merciless** in their reviews of the poorly written novel.
w2797.zh: 評論家對這部寫得很差的小說的評論毫不**留情**。

w2798.en: Beneath its **meretricious** decorations, the hotel was actually shabby and poorly maintained.
w2798.zh: 在**華而不實的**裝飾之下，這家旅館其實破舊且維護不佳。

w2799.en: The magician's clever tricks completely **mesmerized** the children in the front row.
w2799.zh: 魔術師巧妙的把戲完全**迷住**了前排的孩子們。

w2800.en: The waiter greeted the gentlemen politely, saying, "This way, **messieurs**."
w2800.zh: 侍者禮貌地向那些紳士打招呼，說：「這邊請，**各位先生**。」

w2801.en: Recycling **metal** uses far less energy than extracting it from raw ore.
w2801.zh: 回收**金屬**所耗費的能源遠少於從原礦中提煉它。

w2802.en: She specialised in **metallurgy** and studied how different alloys behave under extreme heat.
w2802.zh: 她專攻**冶金學**，研究不同合金在極端高溫下的表現。

w2803.en: The caterpillar undergoes a remarkable **metamorphosis** before emerging as a butterfly.
w2803.zh: 毛毛蟲在化為蝴蝶之前會經歷一場驚人的**蛻變**。

w2804.en: The writer used a clever **metaphor** to compare life to a long journey.
w2804.zh: 作家用一個巧妙的**隱喻**將人生比作一段漫長的旅程。

w2805.en: The lecture raised deep **metaphysical** questions about the nature of reality and existence.
w2805.zh: 這場講座提出了關於現實與存在本質的深刻**形而上學**問題。

w2806.en: As a respected **metaphysician**, he spent decades pondering the meaning of being.
w2806.zh: 身為一位受人尊敬的**形而上學者**，他花了數十年思索存在的意義。

w2807.en: Students often find **metaphysics** challenging because it deals with abstract, intangible ideas.
w2807.zh: 學生常覺得**形而上學**很難，因為它探討抽象而無形的概念。

w2808.en: The strict headmaster would **mete** out punishment to anyone who broke the rules.
w2808.zh: 嚴厲的校長會對任何違反規定的人**施予**懲罰。

w2809.en: Some ancient religions taught that the soul experiences **metempsychosis** after death.
w2809.zh: 一些古代宗教教導說，靈魂在死後會經歷**輪迴轉生**。

w2810.en: The accountant kept **meticulous** records of every single transaction throughout the year.
w2810.zh: 會計師對全年每一筆交易都保持**一絲不苟的**記錄。

w2811.en: In the phrase "the crown decided," the word "crown" is an example of **metonymy**.
w2811.zh: 在「王室做出決定」這句話中，「王室」一詞是**轉喻**的例子。

w2812.en: Most scientists worldwide use the **metric** system for measuring length and weight.
w2812.zh: 全球大多數科學家使用**公制**系統來測量長度和重量。

w2813.en: The piano teacher set the **metronome** to help her student keep a steady rhythm.
w2813.zh: 鋼琴老師設定了**節拍器**，幫助學生保持穩定的節奏。

w2814.en: Millions of people move to the **metropolis** each year in search of work.
w2814.zh: 每年都有數百萬人遷往這座**大都會**尋找工作。

w2815.en: The survey covered the entire **metropolitan** area, including the city and its suburbs.
w2815.zh: 這項調查涵蓋了整個**大都市**地區，包括城市及其郊區。

w2816.en: The final exam truly tested the students' **mettle** under intense time pressure.
w2816.zh: 期末考在巨大的時間壓力下真正考驗了學生的**勇氣與才能**。

w2817.en: The young **mettlesome** recruits volunteered eagerly for the most difficult assignments.
w2817.zh: 那些年輕**精神抖擻的**新兵踴躍地自願承擔最艱難的任務。

w2818.en: This small village is a **microcosm** of the larger problems facing the whole nation.
w2818.zh: 這個小村莊是整個國家所面臨的更大問題的**縮影**。

w2819.en: The speaker tapped the **microphone** to check whether the sound system was working.
w2819.zh: 講者輕敲**麥克風**，確認音響系統是否正常運作。

w2820.en: Under the **microscope**, the cell's internal structures became clearly visible.
w2820.zh: 在**顯微鏡**下，細胞的內部結構變得清晰可見。

w2821.en: The water sample contained countless **microscopic** organisms invisible to the naked eye.
w2821.zh: 這份水樣含有無數肉眼看不見的**極微小的**生物。

w2822.en: Advances in **microscopy** have allowed biologists to observe living cells in extraordinary detail.
w2822.zh: **顯微術**的進步讓生物學家能以非凡的細節觀察活細胞。

w2823.en: In Sweden, families gather to celebrate the long, bright days of **midsummer**.
w2823.zh: 在瑞典，家庭會聚在一起慶祝**仲夏**漫長而明亮的日子。

w2824.en: The experienced **midwife** calmly guided the mother through a long and difficult labour.
w2824.zh: 經驗豐富的**助產士**冷靜地引導母親度過漫長而艱難的分娩。

w2825.en: The old professor entered the room with a calm and dignified **mien**.
w2825.zh: 老教授帶著沉著而莊重的**神態**走進房間。

w2826.en: Many **migrant** workers travel long distances each season to find seasonal jobs.
w2826.zh: 許多**遷移的**勞工每個季節都長途跋涉去尋找季節性工作。

w2827.en: Thousands of birds **migrate** south every autumn to escape the harsh winter.
w2827.zh: 每年秋天，成千上萬隻鳥**遷徙**到南方以躲避嚴冬。

w2828.en: Scientists tracked the **migratory** patterns of the geese using tiny electronic tags.
w2828.zh: 科學家用微型電子標籤追蹤這些鵝的**遷徙**模式。

w2829.en: The salesman boasted about the impressive **mileage** his new car could achieve.
w2829.zh: 推銷員誇耀他的新車能達到的驚人**行車里程**。

w2830.en: A small but **militant** group demanded immediate and radical change to the law.
w2830.zh: 一個人數不多但**激進的**團體要求立即對法律進行根本性的改變。

w2831.en: Historians warn that rising **militarism** often precedes destructive international conflicts.
w2831.zh: 歷史學家警告，**軍國主義**的興起往往是毀滅性國際衝突的前兆。

w2832.en: Several factors **militate** against the success of the proposed business venture.
w2832.zh: 有幾個因素對這項擬議的商業投資的成功**起著不利作用**。

w2833.en: A local **militia** was formed to defend the town during the unrest.
w2833.zh: 當地組建了一支**民兵組織**，以在動亂期間保衛城鎮。

w2834.en: On a clear night far from the city, the **Milky Way** stretches across the sky.
w2834.zh: 在遠離城市的晴朗夜晚，**銀河**橫跨整個天空。

w2835.en: Farmers in the dry region grow **millet** because it needs very little water.
w2835.zh: 乾旱地區的農民種植**小米**，因為它需要的水很少。

w2836.en: The talented child could **mimic** the voices of several famous cartoon characters.
w2836.zh: 這個有天分的孩子能**模仿**好幾個著名卡通角色的聲音。

w2837.en: The museum displays a **miniature** model of the entire ancient city.
w2837.zh: 博物館展出整座古城的**微型**模型。

w2838.en: The company installed new software to **minimize** the risk of data loss.
w2838.zh: 公司安裝了新軟體，以**使**資料遺失的風險**降到最低**。

w2839.en: The powerful minister surrounded himself with loyal **minions** who never questioned his orders.
w2839.zh: 這位權勢薰天的部長身邊圍繞著從不質疑其命令的忠實**奴才**。

w2840.en: The sick villagers were grateful for the doctor's tireless **ministration**.
w2840.zh: 生病的村民對醫生不知疲倦的**服侍**心懷感激。

w2841.en: The new policy was announced by the **Ministry** of Education yesterday afternoon.
w2841.zh: 這項新政策由教育**部**於昨天下午宣布。

w2842.en: Only a small **minority** of the students opposed the proposed schedule change.
w2842.zh: 只有一**小**部分學生反對擬議的時間表變更。

w2843.en: The scientist examined the **minute** cracks in the rock under a powerful lens.
w2843.zh: 科學家在強力鏡頭下檢查岩石中那些**極小的**裂縫。

w2844.en: A good editor pays attention to every **minutia** of grammar and punctuation.
w2844.zh: 優秀的編輯會留意文法和標點的每一個**細節**。

w2845.en: Exhausted travellers in the desert sometimes mistake a **mirage** for a real oasis.
w2845.zh: 沙漠中精疲力竭的旅人有時會把**海市蜃樓**誤認為真正的綠洲。

w2846.en: The coroner ruled that the climber's death was a tragic **misadventure**.
w2846.zh: 驗屍官裁定那名登山者的死亡是一場悲慘的**不幸事故**。

w2847.en: Years of betrayal left him bitter and increasingly **misanthropic** towards everyone.
w2847.zh: 多年的背叛使他變得尖刻，並對所有人愈發**厭惡人類**。

w2848.en: His deep **misanthropy** led him to live alone in a remote mountain cabin.
w2848.zh: 他深切的**厭惡人類**使他獨自住在偏遠的山中小屋裡。

w2849.en: Please read the instructions carefully so that you do not **misapprehend** the rules.
w2849.zh: 請仔細閱讀說明，以免你**誤解**規則。

w2850.en: The teacher warned the boys not to **misbehave** during the school assembly.
w2850.zh: 老師警告男孩們在學校朝會期間不要**舉止失禮**。

w2851.en: Repeated **misbehavior** in class finally led to his suspension from school.
w2851.zh: 課堂上一再的**惡劣行為**最終導致他被學校停學。

w2852.en: The **mischievous** kitten knocked the pen off the desk just to watch it fall.
w2852.zh: 那隻**頑皮的**小貓把筆從桌上撥下來，只為看著它掉落。

w2853.en: Officials had to **miscount** the votes again after spotting a clear error.
w2853.zh: 在發現明顯錯誤後，官員們不得不重新清點，避免再次**數錯**選票。

w2854.en: The sheriff vowed to track down the **miscreant** who had robbed the bank.
w2854.zh: 警長發誓要追捕那名搶劫銀行的**惡棍**。

w2855.en: The corrupt official was eventually punished for his many financial **misdeeds**.
w2855.zh: 這名貪腐的官員最終因其諸多財務**不法行為**而受到懲罰。

w2856.en: In that country, shoplifting is treated as a **misdemeanor** rather than a serious crime.
w2856.zh: 在那個國家，商店行竊被視為**輕罪**而非嚴重犯罪。

w2857.en: The old **miser** refused to spend money even on basic comforts for himself.
w2857.zh: 這個老**守財奴**甚至連自己的基本舒適花費都不願支出。

w2858.en: Apart from one minor **mishap**, the entire expedition went remarkably smoothly.
w2858.zh: 除了一次小小的**意外**之外，整趟探險進行得異常順利。

w2859.en: Reporters must be careful not to **misinterpret** the politician's carefully chosen words.
w2859.zh: 記者必須小心，不要**誤解**這位政客精心挑選的措辭。

w2860.en: I always **mislay** my keys whenever I am in a hurry to leave.
w2860.zh: 每當我急著出門時，總會**一時找不到**我的鑰匙。

w2861.en: The directors were accused of badly **mismanaging** the company's limited funds.
w2861.zh: 董事們被指控嚴重**管理不善**公司有限的資金。

w2862.en: Calling that tiny pond a lake is really something of a **misnomer**.
w2862.zh: 把那個小池塘稱作湖，實在算是一種**名稱誤用**。

w2863.en: His lifelong **misogamy** meant he never seriously considered settling down.
w2863.zh: 他一生的**厭惡結婚**意味著他從未認真考慮安定下來。

w2864.en: The campaign aimed to challenge the **misogyny** still present in many workplaces.
w2864.zh: 這項運動旨在挑戰許多職場中仍然存在的**女性貶抑**。

w2865.en: Be careful not to **misplace** your passport while travelling through the airport.
w2865.zh: 在機場通行時，小心別**亂放**你的護照。

w2866.en: The article was criticised for **misrepresenting** the scientist's original findings.
w2866.zh: 這篇文章因**歪曲**那位科學家的原始發現而受到批評。

w2867.en: Historians blame the famine partly on years of **misrule** by a corrupt regime.
w2867.zh: 歷史學家將這場飢荒部分歸咎於腐敗政權多年的**治理不當**。

w2868.en: The elderly worshipper carried a worn **missal** to the morning service.
w2868.zh: 這位年長的信徒帶著一本破舊的**彌撒用書**去參加晨間禮拜。

w2869.en: The army successfully intercepted the incoming **missile** before it reached the city.
w2869.zh: 軍方在來襲的**飛彈**抵達城市前成功將其攔截。

w2870.en: She received an official **missive** informing her of the committee's final decision.
w2870.zh: 她收到一封官方**公函**，通知她委員會的最終決定。

w2871.en: Many investors **mistrust** companies that refuse to publish clear financial reports.
w2871.zh: 許多投資者**不信任**那些拒絕公布清楚財務報告的公司。

w2872.en: The early morning hills were **misty**, hiding the valley below from view.
w2872.zh: 清晨的山丘**多霧**，將下方的山谷遮蔽住了。

w2873.en: Please explain your point again, because I completely **misunderstand** what you mean.
w2873.zh: 請再解釋一次你的觀點，因為我完全**誤解**了你的意思。

w2874.en: Employees were warned never to **misuse** company resources for personal purposes.
w2874.zh: 員工被警告絕不可將公司資源**濫用**於私人目的。

w2875.en: Tiny dust **mites** living in mattresses can trigger allergies in sensitive people.
w2875.zh: 生活在床墊裡的微小塵**蟎**會引發敏感人群的過敏。

w2876.en: The carpenter carefully cut a neat **miter** to join the two wooden frames.
w2876.zh: 木匠仔細地切出一個整齊的**斜接頭**，以接合兩個木框。

w2877.en: Planting more trees can help **mitigate** the effects of rising urban temperatures.
w2877.zh: 多種樹木有助於**減輕**都市氣溫上升所帶來的影響。

w2878.en: Students often use **mnemonics** to remember long lists of historical dates.
w2878.zh: 學生常使用**助記符號**來記住一長串的歷史日期。

w2879.en: A deep **moat** once surrounded the castle to keep attackers out.
w2879.zh: 一條深深的**護城河**曾環繞著城堡，以阻擋攻擊者。

w2880.en: The historian warned that unchecked anger can quickly slide into violent **mobocracy**.
w2880.zh: 歷史學家警告，不受約束的憤怒可能迅速滑向暴力的**暴民統治**。

w2881.en: She slipped on a pair of soft leather **moccasins** before relaxing by the fire.
w2881.zh: 她在火爐邊放鬆前，穿上了一雙柔軟的皮製**莫卡辛軟皮鞋**。

w2882.en: He endured the constant **mockery** of his classmates with surprising patience.
w2882.zh: 他以驚人的耐心忍受著同學們不斷的**嘲笑**。

w2883.en: Doctors advise enjoying sweets only in **moderation** to maintain good health.
w2883.zh: 醫生建議**適度**享用甜食以維持良好健康。

w2884.en: The **moderator** kept the heated debate fair by giving each speaker equal time.
w2884.zh: **主持人**給予每位發言者相同的時間，使這場激烈的辯論保持公平。

w2885.en: The exhibition explored how artists responded to the rapid arrival of **modernity**.
w2885.zh: 這場展覽探討了藝術家如何回應**現代性**的迅速到來。

w2886.en: The government plans to **modernize** the country's ageing railway network.
w2886.zh: 政府計畫**現代化**該國老化的鐵路網絡。

w2887.en: After several **modifications**, the design finally met all the safety requirements.
w2887.zh: 經過幾次**修改**後，這個設計終於符合所有安全要求。

w2888.en: Engineers had to **modify** the original plan to reduce the overall cost.
w2888.zh: 工程師必須**修改**原始計畫以降低整體成本。

w2889.en: She always wears **modish** clothes that reflect the very latest fashion trends.
w2889.zh: 她總是穿著反映最新時尚潮流的**時髦**衣服。

w2890.en: A skilled speaker learns to **modulate** her voice to hold the audience's attention.
w2890.zh: 熟練的講者懂得**調節**自己的聲音以抓住聽眾的注意力。

w2891.en: He offered a sincere apology to **mollify** his angry colleague.
w2891.zh: 他真誠地道歉以**撫慰**他憤怒的同事。

w2892.en: Snakes regularly **molt**, shedding their old skin as they grow larger.
w2892.zh: 蛇會定期**褪皮**，在長大時脫去舊皮。

w2893.en: There was only a **momentary** pause before the orchestra began to play again.
w2893.zh: 管弦樂團只停頓了**片刻**便又開始演奏。

w2894.en: The signing of the treaty was a **momentous** event in the nation's history.
w2894.zh: 這項條約的簽署是這個國家歷史上一件**重大的**事件。

w2895.en: The campaign gained **momentum** as more volunteers joined it every single week.
w2895.zh: 隨著每週有更多志工加入，這場運動獲得了**動力**。

w2896.en: Britain remains a constitutional **monarchy** with a king as its head of state.
w2896.zh: 英國仍是一個以國王為國家元首的君主立憲**君主制**國家。

w2897.en: The ancient **monastery** on the hill once housed dozens of devoted monks.
w2897.zh: 山上那座古老的**修道院**曾經容納了數十位虔誠的僧侶。

w2898.en: The central bank adjusted its **monetary** policy to control rising inflation.
w2898.zh: 中央銀行調整其**貨幣**政策以控制不斷上升的通貨膨脹。

w2899.en: The friendly **mongrel** they adopted from the shelter quickly became part of the family.
w2899.zh: 他們從收容所領養的那隻友善**雜種狗**很快就成了家庭的一員。

w2900.en: The strange dream felt like a **monition** of the trouble that lay ahead.
w2900.zh: 那個奇怪的夢感覺像是對前方麻煩的一種**警告**。

w2901.en: The bishop sent a stern **monitory** to the clergy about their conduct.
w2901.zh: 主教向神職人員發出一封嚴厲的**告誡書**，告誡他們的行為。

w2902.en: Under the harsh **monocracy**, no citizen dared to criticise the ruler openly.
w2902.zh: 在嚴酷的**獨裁政治**下，沒有公民敢公開批評統治者。

w2903.en: In many cultures, **monogamy** is considered the traditional basis for marriage.
w2903.zh: 在許多文化中，**一夫一妻制**被視為婚姻的傳統基礎。

w2904.en: His shirt pocket was embroidered with an elegant **monogram** of his initials.
w2904.zh: 他的襯衫口袋上繡著一個由他姓名首字母組成的優雅**交織字母**。

w2905.en: The scholar published a detailed **monograph** on medieval trade routes.
w2905.zh: 這位學者發表了一篇關於中世紀貿易路線的詳細**專論**。

w2906.en: The ancient **monolith** stood alone in the field, weathered by centuries of wind.
w2906.zh: 那塊古老的**獨塊巨石**孤立在田野中，經歷了數世紀風霜的侵蝕。

w2907.en: The actor delivered a powerful **monologue** that silenced the entire theatre.
w2907.zh: 那位演員發表了一段震撼人心的**獨白**，讓整個劇院鴉雀無聲。

w2908.en: His obsession with chess grew into a kind of **monomania** that consumed his days.
w2908.zh: 他對西洋棋的著迷發展成一種佔據他每日生活的**偏狂**。

w2909.en: The company held a near **monopoly** of the regional electricity supply for decades.
w2909.zh: 這家公司數十年來幾乎**壟斷**了該地區的電力供應。

w2910.en: The word "cat" is a simple **monosyllable** that young children learn early.
w2910.zh: 「cat」這個字是幼兒很早就學會的簡單**單音節詞**。

w2911.en: He read the report aloud in a dull **monotone** that made everyone drowsy.
w2911.zh: 他用一種乏味的**單音調**朗讀報告，讓所有人都昏昏欲睡。

w2912.en: The long, **monotonous** journey across the flat plain left the passengers bored.
w2912.zh: 穿越平坦平原的漫長而**單調乏味的**旅程讓乘客感到無聊。

w2913.en: To escape the **monotony** of office life, she took up painting on weekends.
w2913.zh: 為了逃離辦公室生活的**單調乏味**，她在週末開始畫畫。

w2914.en: The hotel concierge bowed and said, "Good evening, **monsieur**."
w2914.zh: 飯店門房鞠了一躬，說：「晚安，**先生**。」

w2915.en: Many residents complained that the new tower was an architectural **monstrosity**.
w2915.zh: 許多居民抱怨那座新高樓是一個建築上的**巨大而醜陋的東西**。

w2916.en: A single **moonbeam** slipped through the curtains and lit up the quiet room.
w2916.zh: 一束**月光**穿過窗簾，照亮了寂靜的房間。

w2917.en: The coach's encouraging speech greatly boosted the team's **morale** before the final.
w2917.zh: 教練鼓舞人心的演說在決賽前大大提升了球隊的**士氣**。

w2918.en: As a strict **moralist**, he judged every action by rigid ethical standards.
w2918.zh: 身為一位嚴格的**道德家**，他用嚴苛的倫理標準評判每一個行為。

w2919.en: Philosophers have long debated the foundations of human **morality** and right conduct.
w2919.zh: 哲學家長期以來爭論著人類**道德**與正當行為的基礎。

w2920.en: Nobody enjoys listening to a friend who constantly **moralizes** about their choices.
w2920.zh: 沒有人喜歡聽一個不斷對他們的選擇**說教**的朋友。

w2921.en: The government declared a temporary **moratorium** on all new building permits.
w2921.zh: 政府宣布**暫停**核發所有新的建築許可。

w2922.en: He developed a **morbid** fascination with stories about famous disasters.
w2922.zh: 他對著名災難的故事產生了一種**病態的**著迷。

w2923.en: The reviewer's **mordacious** comments left the young author feeling deeply discouraged.
w2923.zh: 評論者**尖刻的**評論讓這位年輕作家感到非常氣餒。

w2924.en: Her **mordant** wit made the meeting entertaining but occasionally rather cruel.
w2924.zh: 她**尖刻而風趣的**機智使會議變得有趣，但偶爾相當刻薄。

w2925.en: Without fresh investment, the once-thriving company became completely **moribund**.
w2925.zh: 沒有新的投資，這家曾經興旺的公司變得完全**停滯不前**。

w2926.en: He grew increasingly **morose** after losing both his job and his savings.
w2926.zh: 在失去工作和積蓄後，他變得越來越**陰鬱**。

w2927.en: Linguists study **morphology** to understand how words are formed from smaller parts.
w2927.zh: 語言學家研究**詞法學**，以了解詞彙是如何由更小的部分構成的。

w2928.en: A **motley** crowd of tourists, students, and merchants filled the busy square.
w2928.zh: 一群由遊客、學生和商人組成的**混雜的**人群擠滿了繁忙的廣場。

w2929.en: The school's **motto** encourages every pupil to work hard and stay honest.
w2929.zh: 這所學校的**校訓**鼓勵每位學生努力學習並保持誠實。

w2930.en: An experienced **mountaineer** led the group safely to the snowy summit.
w2930.zh: 一位經驗豐富的**登山運動員**帶領隊伍安全抵達白雪覆蓋的山頂。

w2931.en: The road wound slowly through the wild and **mountainous** northern region.
w2931.zh: 道路緩緩蜿蜒穿過荒野而**多山的**北部地區。

w2932.en: She took a quick **mouthful** of water before continuing her long speech.
w2932.zh: 她在繼續她漫長的演說前，迅速喝了一**口**水。

w2933.en: Thick curtains helped to **muffle** the noise from the busy street outside.
w2933.zh: 厚重的窗簾有助於**減弱**外面繁忙街道的噪音。

w2934.en: The historical novel features a **mulatto** heroine navigating a deeply divided society.
w2934.zh: 這部歷史小說以一位**混血兒**女主角在嚴重分裂的社會中周旋為主軸。

w2935.en: The weary **muleteer** led his loaded animals along the steep mountain trail.
w2935.zh: 疲憊的**趕騾人**牽著馱滿貨物的牲口沿著陡峭的山徑前行。

w2936.en: The exhibition displayed pottery of **multiform** shapes from many different ancient cultures.
w2936.zh: 這場展覽展出了來自許多不同古代文化、形狀**多樣的**陶器。

w2937.en: The committee faced a **multiplicity** of opinions before reaching a final agreement.
w2937.zh: 委員會在達成最終協議前面對了**多種多樣的**意見。

w2938.en: He longed to escape the **mundane** routine of his ordinary office job.
w2938.zh: 他渴望逃離他平凡辦公室工作那**單調乏味的**例行公事。

w2939.en: The new park was funded entirely by the **municipal** government.
w2939.zh: 這座新公園完全由**市政**府出資興建。

w2940.en: The **municipality** is responsible for collecting rubbish and maintaining local roads.
w2940.zh: **市政當局**負責收集垃圾和維護當地道路。

w2941.en: The hospital was rebuilt thanks to the remarkable **munificence** of a single donor.
w2941.zh: 這家醫院得以重建，全靠一位捐贈者非凡的**慷慨解囊**。

w2942.en: The **munificent** businessman donated millions to fund scholarships for poor students.
w2942.zh: 這位**慷慨的**商人捐出數百萬資助貧困學生的獎學金。

w2943.en: The general ordered a **muster** of all troops at dawn the next morning.
w2943.zh: 將軍下令所有部隊於次日黎明**集結**。

w2944.en: A random genetic **mutation** can sometimes give an organism a survival advantage.
w2944.zh: 隨機的基因**突變**有時能賦予生物體生存優勢。

w2945.en: Vandals had badly **mutilated** the ancient statue, breaking off both its arms.
w2945.zh: 破壞者嚴重**毀壞**了這座古老雕像，折斷了它的雙臂。

w2946.en: The exhausted sailors finally rose in **mutiny** against their cruel captain.
w2946.zh: 精疲力竭的水手們終於起來**叛變**，反抗他們殘忍的船長。

w2947.en: The night sky revealed a **myriad** of stars far from the city lights.
w2947.zh: 遠離城市燈光的夜空展現出**無數**的星星。

w2948.en: The ancient **mystic** claimed to receive visions while meditating in the desert.
w2948.zh: 這位古代**神秘主義者**聲稱在沙漠中冥想時得到了異象。

w2949.en: The detective's strange behaviour caused great **mystification** among his colleagues.
w2949.zh: 偵探的怪異行為在他的同事間引起了極大的**迷惑不解**。

w2950.en: It is a common **myth** that people only use ten percent of their brains.
w2950.zh: 認為人們只使用大腦百分之十的說法是個常見的**錯誤觀念**。

w2951.en: Students of Greek **mythology** learn about gods, heroes, and ancient legends.
w2951.zh: 研究希臘**神話**的學生會學習關於神祇、英雄與古老傳說的故事。

w2952.en: The poem was written by a **nameless** author whose identity remains unknown today.
w2952.zh: 這首詩由一位**無名的**作者所寫，其身分至今仍不為人知。

w2953.en: Workers used **naphtha** as a solvent to clean the greasy machine parts.
w2953.zh: 工人用**揮發油**作為溶劑來清潔油膩的機器零件。

w2954.en: Every spring, the garden fills with bright yellow **narcissus** flowers.
w2954.zh: 每年春天，花園裡都開滿了鮮黃色的**水仙花**。

w2955.en: The old fisherman loved to **narrate** thrilling tales of his sea voyages.
w2955.zh: 老漁夫喜歡**敘述**他海上航行的驚險故事。

w2956.en: The documentary's calm **narration** guided viewers through the complex historical events.
w2956.zh: 這部紀錄片平靜的**旁白**引導觀眾了解複雜的歷史事件。

w2957.en: The film presents a gripping **narrative** about a family's struggle to survive.
w2957.zh: 這部電影呈現了一個關於一家人掙扎求生的扣人心弦的**故事**。

w2958.en: The unseen **narrator** explained the characters' thoughts throughout the entire novel.
w2958.zh: 看不見的**敘述者**在整部小說中解釋著角色的想法。

w2959.en: His **narrow-minded** attitude prevented him from accepting any unfamiliar ideas.
w2959.zh: 他**心胸狹窄的**態度使他無法接受任何陌生的想法。

w2960.en: A bad cold left her with a heavy, **nasal** voice for several days.
w2960.zh: 重感冒讓她有好幾天聲音沉重且帶有**鼻音**。

w2961.en: Nurses carefully recorded the baby's weight and other **natal** details after the birth.
w2961.zh: 護士在嬰兒**出生**後仔細記錄了他的體重和其他出生細節。

w2962.en: Applicants must provide proof of their **nationality** before the interview.
w2962.zh: 申請人必須在面試前提供其**國籍**證明。

w2963.en: Some people are **naturally** talented at music and learn instruments very quickly.
w2963.zh: 有些人**天生**就有音樂天賦，學習樂器非常快。

w2964.en: The rocking boat soon filled the passengers with an unpleasant feeling of **nausea**.
w2964.zh: 搖晃的船很快讓乘客充滿了令人不適的**噁心**感。

w2965.en: The thick, greasy smell from the kitchen began to **nauseate** the diners.
w2965.zh: 廚房傳來濃重油膩的氣味開始讓用餐者感到**噁心**。

w2966.en: The strong smell of paint made several workers feel dizzy and **nauseous**.
w2966.zh: 濃烈的油漆味讓好幾名工人感到頭暈和**噁心**。

w2967.en: The museum displays many **nautical** instruments once used by ancient sailors.
w2967.zh: 博物館展出許多古代水手曾使用的**航海**儀器。

w2968.en: The country invested heavily in expanding its **naval** fleet during the war.
w2968.zh: 這個國家在戰爭期間大量投資於擴充其**海軍**艦隊。

w2969.en: The doctor gently examined the baby's **navel** during the routine checkup.
w2969.zh: 醫生在例行檢查時輕輕檢查了嬰兒的**肚臍**。

w2970.en: The wide, calm river is easily **navigable** by large cargo ships.
w2970.zh: 這條寬闊平靜的河流大型貨船很容易**通航**。

w2971.en: Sailors once used the stars to **navigate** safely across the open ocean.
w2971.zh: 水手們曾用星星在廣闊的海洋上安全**導航**。

w2972.en: Through the telescope, astronomers observed a glowing **nebula** light-years away.
w2972.zh: 透過望遠鏡，天文學家觀測到一團數光年外發光的**星雲**。

w2973.en: Bringing warm clothing is absolutely **necessary** for a trip to the mountains.
w2973.zh: 前往山區的旅行絕對**有必要**攜帶保暖衣物。

w2974.en: The sudden storm will **necessitate** a complete change to our travel plans.
w2974.zh: 這場突如其來的暴風雨將**使**我們的旅行計畫**必須**徹底改變。

w2975.en: Clean water is a basic **necessity** that every community urgently needs.
w2975.zh: 乾淨的水是每個社區都迫切需要的基本**必需品**。

w2976.en: The newspaper published a long **necrology** listing those who had died that year.
w2976.zh: 報紙刊登了一份長長的**逝者名單**，列出那年去世的人。

w2977.en: In the legend, a powerful **necromancer** summoned spirits to reveal hidden secrets.
w2977.zh: 在傳說中，一位強大的**招魂占卜師**召喚亡靈來揭示隱藏的秘密。

w2978.en: Archaeologists discovered an ancient **necropolis** filled with elaborately decorated tombs.
w2978.zh: 考古學家發現了一座古老的**古代墳場**，裡面滿是裝飾精美的墓穴。

w2979.en: If blood flow is blocked, the surrounding tissue may eventually suffer **necrosis**.
w2979.zh: 如果血流受阻，周圍的組織最終可能會發生**壞死**。

w2980.en: Bees gather sweet **nectar** from flowers to produce their golden honey.
w2980.zh: 蜜蜂從花朵中採集甜美的**花蜜**來製造金黃色的蜂蜜。

w2981.en: She bit into the ripe **nectarine**, enjoying its smooth skin and sweet juice.
w2981.zh: 她咬了一口成熟的**油桃**，享受著它光滑的果皮和甜美的汁液。

w2982.en: My grandmother spent quiet evenings doing delicate **needlework** by the fire.
w2982.zh: 我祖母在火爐邊度過寧靜的夜晚，做著精緻的**針線活**。

w2983.en: The charity provides warm meals to **needy** families throughout the cold winter.
w2983.zh: 這個慈善機構在整個寒冬為**貧窮的**家庭提供溫熱的餐食。

w2984.en: Police uncovered a **nefarious** scheme to defraud elderly investors of their savings.
w2984.zh: 警方揭發了一個詐騙年長投資者積蓄的**邪惡**計謀。

w2985.en: A single careless mistake can completely **negate** months of hard work.
w2985.zh: 一個粗心的錯誤就能完全**抵消**數月的辛勤努力。

w2986.en: His flat **negation** of the accusation surprised everyone present at the hearing.
w2986.zh: 他對指控的斷然**否認**讓在場聽證會的每個人都感到驚訝。

w2987.en: The report criticised managers who were **neglectful** of basic safety procedures.
w2987.zh: 報告批評了那些**疏忽**基本安全程序的管理者。

w2988.en: She slipped a silk **negligee** over her shoulders before going to bed.
w2988.zh: 她在就寢前將一件絲質**女式晨衣**披在肩上。

w2989.en: The accident was caused by the driver's clear **negligence** at the crossing.
w2989.zh: 這起事故是由駕駛在路口明顯的**疏忽**所造成的。

w2990.en: The company was found **negligent** for failing to maintain its old machinery.
w2990.zh: 這家公司因未能維護其老舊機器而被判定為**失職**。

w2991.en: The difference between the two prices was so small as to be **negligible**.
w2991.zh: 這兩個價格之間的差異小到可說是**微不足道的**。

w2992.en: The final salary offer is still **negotiable**, so feel free to discuss it.
w2992.zh: 最終的薪資提議仍可**協商**，所以歡迎進一步討論。

w2993.en: After years of rivalry, she finally defeated her old **nemesis** in the final.
w2993.zh: 經過多年的較量，她終於在決賽中擊敗了她的老**勁敵**。

w2994.en: The philosopher imagined a peaceful **neocracy** where no one held power over others.
w2994.zh: 這位哲學家想像出一個沒有人能支配他人的和平**無支配體制**。

w2995.en: The course examined how **neo-Darwinism** combines genetics with the theory of evolution.
w2995.zh: 這門課程探討**新達爾文主義**如何將遺傳學與演化論結合。

w2996.en: Scholars sometimes wrote scientific works in **neo-Latin** long after the Roman era.
w2996.zh: 在羅馬時代結束很久之後，學者有時仍以**新拉丁語**撰寫科學著作。

w2997.en: The festival celebrated **neopaganism** with rituals inspired by ancient nature worship.
w2997.zh: 這場慶典以受古代自然崇拜啟發的儀式來歌頌**新異教**。

w2998.en: Archaeologists found **neolithic** tools and pottery buried beneath the ancient settlement.
w2998.zh: 考古學家在這座古老聚落下方發現了**新石器時代的**工具和陶器。

w2999.en: Every generation adds new **neology** to the language as technology and culture change.
w2999.zh: 隨著科技與文化的變遷，每一代都會為語言增添新的**新詞**。

w3000.en: As a complete **neophyte**, she felt nervous on her very first day at the studio.
w3000.zh: 身為一個徹底的**生手**，她在工作室的第一天感到緊張。

w3001.en: The small village seems to **nestle** between two green hills near the river.
w3001.zh: 這座小村莊彷彿依偎在河邊兩座青翠的山丘之間。

w3002.en: The parent birds tirelessly carried insects back to feed the hungry **nestlings**.
w3002.zh: 親鳥不知疲倦地把昆蟲叼回巢中，餵養飢餓的雛鳥。

w3003.en: His rude and dismissive comments clearly **nettled** several members of the committee.
w3003.zh: 他粗魯又輕蔑的言論顯然激怒了委員會的好幾位成員。

w3004.en: The new railway **network** connects all the major cities across the entire region.
w3004.zh: 新的鐵路網路連接了整個地區的所有主要城市。

w3005.en: Scientists study how **neural** signals travel rapidly through the human brain and body.
w3005.zh: 科學家研究神經訊號如何在人類的大腦和身體中快速傳遞。

w3006.en: She specialised in **neurology** after years of studying disorders of the nervous system.
w3006.zh: 在多年研究神經系統疾病後，她專攻神經學。

w3007.en: The vet advised us to **neuter** the cat to prevent unwanted litters.
w3007.zh: 獸醫建議我們為這隻貓結紮，以避免不想要的幼貓出生。

w3008.en: During the dispute, the mediator remained completely **neutral** and favoured neither side.
w3008.zh: 在爭端中，調解人保持完全中立，不偏袒任何一方。

w3009.en: The plan was risky; **nevertheless**, the team decided to go ahead with it.
w3009.zh: 這個計劃有風險；儘管如此，團隊仍決定繼續推行。

w3010.en: The bridge was designed using classic **Newtonian** principles of force and motion.
w3010.zh: 這座橋是依據經典的牛頓力學原理設計的。

w3011.en: The charity received only a **niggardly** donation that barely covered its costs.
w3011.zh: 這家慈善機構只收到一筆微薄的捐款，幾乎無法支應其開銷。

w3012.en: As a committed **nihilist**, he argued that life had no inherent meaning at all.
w3012.zh: 作為一個堅定的虛無主義者，他主張生命根本沒有任何固有的意義。

w3013.en: After the final whistle, the score remained at one goal to **nil**.
w3013.zh: 終場哨聲響起後，比分仍停留在一比零。

w3014.en: The **nimble** dancer leapt across the stage with remarkable speed and grace.
w3014.zh: 那位身手靈活的舞者以驚人的速度與優雅躍過舞台。

w3015.en: He dismissed the careless intern as a complete **nit** who never learned anything.
w3015.zh: 他把那個粗心的實習生貶為一個從來學不會東西的十足傻瓜。

w3016.en: Owls are **nocturnal** creatures that hunt for prey during the dark hours.
w3016.zh: 貓頭鷹是夜行性動物，會在黑暗的時段獵捕獵物。

w3017.en: The new electric motor was almost completely **noiseless** even at full speed.
w3017.zh: 這款新型電動馬達即使在全速運轉時也幾乎完全無聲。

w3018.en: A **noisome** smell from the drains made the whole kitchen unbearable.
w3018.zh: 排水管傳來的一股惡臭讓整個廚房令人無法忍受。

w3019.en: The classroom was far too **noisy** for the students to concentrate properly.
w3019.zh: 教室實在太吵鬧了，學生們無法好好專心。

w3020.en: The desert **nomads** moved their tents whenever the grazing land ran out.
w3020.zh: 沙漠中的遊牧民每當牧草耗盡時就會遷移他們的帳篷。

w3021.en: In everyday speech the word carries a **nomic**, widely accepted sense.
w3021.zh: 在日常用語中，這個詞帶有一種慣用且廣為接受的意義。

w3022.en: He is only the **nominal** head; his deputy makes all the real decisions.
w3022.zh: 他只是名義上的領導，所有實際決策都由他的副手做出。

w3023.en: The party agreed to **nominate** her as their candidate for the upcoming election.
w3023.zh: 該黨同意提名她為即將到來選舉的候選人。

w3024.en: Her **nomination** for the directorship surprised many of her longtime colleagues.
w3024.zh: 她獲得董事職位的提名讓許多她的老同事感到驚訝。

w3025.en: Each **nominee** was interviewed carefully before the final appointment was announced.
w3025.zh: 在最終任命公佈之前，每位被提名者都接受了仔細的面試。

w3026.en: Public transport in that remote village is virtually **non-existent** these days.
w3026.zh: 如今那個偏遠村莊的公共交通幾乎是不存在的。

w3027.en: The college offers special evening classes for its **non-resident** students.
w3027.zh: 這所學院為其不寄宿的學生開設了特別的夜間課程。

w3028.en: She received the shocking news with surprising **nonchalance** and a calm smile.
w3028.zh: 她以出人意料的若無其事和一抹平靜的微笑接受了這個驚人的消息。

w3029.en: As a **non-combatant**, the medic was forbidden to carry any offensive weapon.
w3029.zh: 作為非戰鬥人員，這名醫護兵被禁止攜帶任何攻擊性武器。

w3030.en: He wore a **nondescript** grey coat that nobody in the crowd would notice.
w3030.zh: 他穿著一件平淡無奇的灰色外套，人群中沒有人會注意到。

w3031.en: Despite his loud boasting, his colleagues regarded him as a complete **nonentity**.
w3031.zh: 儘管他大聲吹噓，同事們仍把他視為一個無足輕重的人。

w3032.en: As a violinist she is a **nonpareil**, admired by audiences around the world.
w3032.zh: 作為小提琴家，她無可匹敵，受到世界各地觀眾的讚賞。

w3033.en: Working long hours has unfortunately become the **norm** in many modern offices.
w3033.zh: 長時間工作不幸地已成為許多現代辦公室的常態。

w3034.en: After months of disruption, the town slowly returned to a sense of **normalcy**.
w3034.zh: 在數月的混亂之後，這座城鎮慢慢恢復到一種正常狀態。

w3035.en: The old church features thick walls in the classic **Norman** architectural style.
w3035.zh: 這座古老的教堂以典型諾曼式建築風格的厚牆為特色。

w3036.en: The salesman promoted his strange tonic as a **nostrum** for every illness.
w3036.zh: 那名推銷員把他古怪的補藥吹捧成能治百病的萬應靈藥。

w3037.en: There has been a **noticeable** improvement in air quality since the factory closed.
w3037.zh: 自從工廠關閉以來，空氣品質有了顯著的改善。

w3038.en: That stretch of road is **notorious** for serious accidents during heavy rain.
w3038.zh: 那段路因大雨時嚴重車禍而臭名昭著。

w3039.en: She wrote a charming **novelette** that readers could finish in one quiet afternoon.
w3039.zh: 她寫了一篇迷人的短篇小說，讀者可以在一個寧靜的下午讀完。

w3040.en: As a complete **novice**, he struggled to keep up with the experienced climbers.
w3040.zh: 作為一個徹底的新手，他費力地想跟上那些經驗豐富的登山者。

w3041.en: **Nowadays** most people rely on their phones to find directions and information.
w3041.zh: 如今大多數人依賴手機來尋找方向和資訊。

w3042.en: After hours of searching, the lost keys were simply **nowhere** to be found.
w3042.zh: 找了好幾個小時，遺失的鑰匙就是哪裡都找不到。

w3043.en: The factory released **noxious** fumes that endangered the health of nearby residents.
w3043.zh: 那家工廠排放有毒的煙氣，危及附近居民的健康。

w3044.en: A skilled translator must capture every subtle **nuance** of the original meaning.
w3044.zh: 一位熟練的譯者必須捕捉原意的每一個細微差別。

w3045.en: The **nucleus** of an atom contains both protons and tightly packed neutrons.
w3045.zh: 原子核中含有質子以及緊密堆積的中子。

w3046.en: The art class spent the afternoon sketching a **nude** figure in charcoal.
w3046.zh: 美術課花了一下午用炭筆素描一個裸體人物。

w3047.en: The committee dismissed his proposal as a **nugatory** waste of everyone's time.
w3047.zh: 委員會把他的提案斥為毫無價值、浪費眾人時間的東西。

w3048.en: The constant dripping tap became a real **nuisance** throughout the long night.
w3048.zh: 那個一直在滴水的水龍頭整個漫長的夜晚成了一個真正惱人的麻煩。

w3049.en: Children learn basic **numeration** before they can begin to add and subtract.
w3049.zh: 孩子們在能開始加減之前會先學習基本的計數。

w3050.en: The report presented the survey results in clear **numerical** form for comparison.
w3050.zh: 該報告以清晰的數字形式呈現調查結果以供比較。

w3051.en: The young woman entered the **nunnery** and devoted her life to quiet prayer.
w3051.zh: 這位年輕女子進入女修道院，將一生奉獻於寧靜的祈禱。

w3052.en: The couple exchanged their **nuptial** vows in a small chapel by the sea.
w3052.zh: 這對伴侶在海邊一座小教堂交換了他們的婚姻誓言。

w3053.en: Good teachers **nurture** young talent patiently until it grows into real skill.
w3053.zh: 好老師耐心地培育年輕的才能，直到它成長為真正的技能。

w3054.en: Fresh vegetables provide essential **nutriment** that the growing body badly needs.
w3054.zh: 新鮮蔬菜提供成長中的身體所迫切需要的必要營養物。

w3055.en: Doctors recommend a **nutritive** diet rich in vitamins, minerals, and protein.
w3055.zh: 醫生建議採用富含維生素、礦物質和蛋白質的營養飲食。

w3056.en: The grand hall featured a heavy **oaken** door carved with delicate patterns.
w3056.zh: 這座宏偉的大廳有一扇雕刻著精緻花紋的厚重橡木門。

w3057.en: Sailors once packed **oakum** tightly between the planks to keep the deck watertight.
w3057.zh: 水手們從前會把填絮緊緊塞進木板之間，以保持甲板不漏水。

w3058.en: The minister remained **obdurate** on the issue, refusing every reasonable compromise.
w3058.zh: 那位部長在這個議題上頑固不化，拒絕一切合理的妥協。

w3059.en: A tall stone **obelisk** stands in the square to honour the fallen soldiers.
w3059.zh: 廣場上矗立著一座高大的石製方尖紀念碑，以紀念陣亡的士兵。

w3060.en: Eating too much fatty food can make a person seriously **obese** over time.
w3060.zh: 長期攝取過多油膩食物會使人嚴重肥胖。

w3061.en: Doctors warn that childhood **obesity** has been rising steadily in recent decades.
w3061.zh: 醫生警告說，兒童肥胖在近幾十年來持續上升。

w3062.en: The newspaper published a moving **obituary** celebrating the writer's remarkable life.
w3062.zh: 報紙刊登了一篇感人的訃告，頌揚這位作家非凡的一生。

w3063.en: A good journalist should report the facts in a fair and **objective** manner.
w3063.zh: 一位優秀的記者應該以公正客觀的方式報導事實。

w3064.en: As a conscientious **objector**, he refused to take part in the war.
w3064.zh: 作為一個基於良心的反對者，他拒絕參與這場戰爭。

w3065.en: The contract **obligates** the company to repair any faults free of charge.
w3065.zh: 這份合約責成公司免費修復任何瑕疵。

w3066.en: Wearing a seatbelt is **obligatory** for every passenger travelling in the car.
w3066.zh: 車內每位乘客都必須繫上安全帶。

w3067.en: He gave only an **oblique** answer, never addressing the question directly.
w3067.zh: 他只給出一個拐彎抹角的答案，從未直接回應這個問題。

w3068.en: The massive flood **obliterated** every trace of the small riverside village.
w3068.zh: 那場大洪水徹底抹去了這個河邊小村莊的一切痕跡。

w3069.en: After the scandal, the once-famous actor faded quietly into complete **oblivion**.
w3069.zh: 醜聞之後，這位曾經聞名的演員悄然淡出，被徹底遺忘。

w3070.en: The gardener planted bright flowers along the edge of the **oblong** lawn.
w3070.zh: 園丁沿著那塊矩形草坪的邊緣種了鮮豔的花朵。

w3071.en: His loud and **obnoxious** behaviour quickly annoyed everyone at the dinner party.
w3071.zh: 他大聲又令人討厭的行為很快惹惱了晚宴上的每個人。

w3072.en: The family gathered quietly to attend the solemn **obsequies** of their grandfather.
w3072.zh: 家人安靜地聚集在一起，參加祖父莊嚴的葬禮儀式。

w3073.en: The waiter was so **obsequious** that his constant flattery became quite embarrassing.
w3073.zh: 那名服務生諂媚到不斷的奉承都讓人相當尷尬。

w3074.en: Strict **observance** of the safety rules is expected from all the workers.
w3074.zh: 所有工人都應嚴格遵守安全規則。

w3075.en: A truly **observant** detective notices tiny details that others usually overlook.
w3075.zh: 一位真正善於觀察的偵探會注意到別人通常忽略的微小細節。

w3076.en: Astronomers gathered at the mountain **observatory** to study the distant galaxies.
w3076.zh: 天文學家聚集在那座山上的天文臺研究遙遠的星系。

w3077.en: The rapid **obsolescence** of technology forces companies to upgrade their equipment often.
w3077.zh: 科技的快速荒廢迫使公司經常升級他們的設備。

w3078.en: Many older mobile phones are already **obsolescent** and will soon be unsupported.
w3078.zh: 許多較舊的手機已經逐漸過時，很快將不再受到支援。

w3079.en: Typewriters became almost completely **obsolete** once personal computers entered every office.
w3079.zh: 一旦個人電腦進入每間辦公室，打字機就幾乎完全被淘汰了。

w3080.en: An experienced **obstetrician** carefully monitored the mother throughout her difficult pregnancy.
w3080.zh: 一位經驗豐富的產科醫生在這位母親艱難的孕期中仔細監測她的狀況。

w3081.en: She studied **obstetrics** because she wanted to help women through childbirth safely.
w3081.zh: 她研讀產科學，因為她想幫助婦女安全地度過分娩。

w3082.en: His stubborn **obstinacy** made it almost impossible to reach any agreement.
w3082.zh: 他頑固的固執使得幾乎不可能達成任何協議。

w3083.en: The teacher struggled to control the **obstreperous** children during the noisy assembly.
w3083.zh: 老師在吵鬧的集會中費力地控制那些喧鬧不服管束的孩子。

w3084.en: A fallen tree completely **obstructed** the narrow road into the village.
w3084.zh: 一棵倒下的樹完全阻塞了通往村莊的狹窄道路。

w3085.en: Workers cleared the **obstruction** so that traffic could finally flow again.
w3085.zh: 工人清除了障礙物，交通終於得以再次順暢。

w3086.en: He hated to **obtrude** upon their private conversation, but the news was urgent.
w3086.zh: 他不願打擾他們的私人談話，但這個消息很緊急。

w3087.en: The large advertising sign was far too **obtrusive** for the quiet old street.
w3087.zh: 那塊大型廣告招牌對這條安靜的老街來說太過刺眼了。

w3088.en: In logic class we learned to **obvert** a statement into its opposite quality.
w3088.zh: 在邏輯課上我們學會了把一個陳述換質成相反的性質。

w3089.en: Installing better software should **obviate** the need for constant manual corrections.
w3089.zh: 安裝更好的軟體應該能消除不斷手動修正的必要。

w3090.en: On one memorable **occasion**, the whole family gathered to celebrate her graduation.
w3090.zh: 在一個難忘的時刻，全家人聚在一起慶祝她畢業。

w3091.en: Trade routes once linked the distant **Occident** with the markets of Asia.
w3091.zh: 古代的貿易路線曾將遙遠的西方與亞洲的市場連結起來。

w3092.en: Thick fat can gradually **occlude** an artery and restrict the blood flow.
w3092.zh: 厚厚的脂肪會逐漸阻塞動脈並限制血液流動。

w3093.en: The old library held many rare books on **occult** rituals and hidden symbols.
w3093.zh: 那座古老的圖書館收藏許多關於神秘儀式和隱藏符號的珍稀書籍。

w3094.en: The new **occupant** of the flat painted every room a cheerful colour.
w3094.zh: 這間公寓的新住戶把每個房間都漆成了愉悅的顏色。

w3095.en: A solar eclipse is a rare **occurrence** that always fascinates curious observers.
w3095.zh: 日蝕是一種罕見的事件，總是讓好奇的觀察者著迷。

w3096.en: The children learned to draw an **octagon** with eight equal straight sides.
w3096.zh: 孩子們學會畫出有八條等長直邊的八邊形。

w3097.en: The singer effortlessly reached a note a full **octave** above the others.
w3097.zh: 那位歌手輕鬆地唱到比其他人高出整整一個八度的音。

w3098.en: The rare poems were printed in a small **octavo** volume bound in leather.
w3098.zh: 這些珍稀的詩作以一本皮革裝訂的小型八開本印製。

w3099.en: The lively **octogenarian** still swims every morning before eating his breakfast.
w3099.zh: 這位活力充沛的八旬老人每天早上吃早餐前仍會游泳。

w3100.en: The doctor used a special lamp to examine the patient's **ocular** muscles.
w3100.zh: 醫生用一盞特殊的燈檢查病人的眼部肌肉。

w3101.en: My grandmother visited the **oculist** regularly to check her failing eyesight.
w3101.zh: 我祖母定期去看眼科醫師，檢查她日漸衰退的視力。

w3102.en: The clock that runs backwards is a real **oddity** in the antique shop.
w3102.zh: 那座倒著走的時鐘是這家古董店裡一件真正的怪事。

w3103.en: The poet wrote a heartfelt **ode** to the beauty of the autumn forest.
w3103.zh: 這位詩人寫了一首發自內心的頌詩，讚美秋日森林的美麗。

w3104.en: Spreading such cruel rumours about a friend is truly an **odious** act.
w3104.zh: 散佈關於朋友如此殘忍的謠言實在是一種可憎的行為。

w3105.en: The dishonest official earned the public **odium** after the corruption was exposed.
w3105.zh: 貪腐被揭發後，這名不誠實的官員招致了大眾的憎恨。

w3106.en: The **odoriferous** blossoms in the garden filled the warm evening air with sweetness.
w3106.zh: 花園裡芬芳的花朵讓溫暖的夜晚空氣充滿了甜香。

w3107.en: A faint **odorous** smell from the bin lingered in the small kitchen.
w3107.zh: 垃圾桶傳來的一股淡淡難聞氣味在小廚房裡久久不散。

w3108.en: The outdoor concert was **off** because of the sudden and heavy rain.
w3108.zh: 由於突如其來的大雨，這場戶外音樂會取消了。

w3109.en: When asked the price, she gave a rough estimate quite **offhand**.
w3109.zh: 被問到價格時，她不假思索地給出了一個粗略的估計。

w3110.en: A respected local judge agreed to **officiate** at the couple's wedding ceremony.
w3110.zh: 一位受人敬重的當地法官同意主持這對伴侶的婚禮。

w3111.en: The **officious** clerk kept interfering with tasks that were none of his business.
w3111.zh: 那名愛指手畫腳的職員不斷干涉與他無關的事務。

w3112.en: This small company is an **offshoot** of a much larger international firm.
w3112.zh: 這家小公司是一家規模大得多的國際公司的分支。

w3113.en: In the fairy tale, a fearsome **ogre** guarded the bridge to the castle.
w3113.zh: 在這則童話中，一個可怕的食人怪物守衛著通往城堡的橋。

w3114.en: The nurse gently applied a soothing **ointment** to the child's grazed knee.
w3114.zh: 護士輕輕地在孩子擦傷的膝蓋上塗抹舒緩的藥膏。

w3115.en: Dogs possess remarkable **olfactory** abilities that humans simply cannot match.
w3115.zh: 狗擁有人類根本無法比擬的卓越嗅覺能力。

w3116.en: The two rivals finally extended an **olive-branch** and agreed to work together.
w3116.zh: 這兩個對手終於伸出橄欖枝，同意攜手合作。

w3117.en: Dark clouds gathered overhead, casting an **ominous** shadow across the silent valley.
w3117.zh: 烏雲在頭頂聚集，在寂靜的山谷投下不祥的陰影。

w3118.en: A single **omission** in the contract caused months of confusion and legal disputes.
w3118.zh: 合約中的一處疏漏造成了數月的混亂和法律糾紛。

w3119.en: Many religions describe their god with a quality of complete **omnipotence**.
w3119.zh: 許多宗教以全能的特質來描述他們的神。

w3120.en: Believers trust that an **Omnipotent** creator shaped every star in the universe.
w3120.zh: 信徒相信一位全能的造物主塑造了宇宙中的每一顆星星。

w3121.en: The novel imagines a god whose **omniscience** reveals every secret thought.
w3121.zh: 這部小說想像出一位全知能洞悉每個秘密念頭的神。

w3122.en: The story is told by an **omniscient** narrator who knows everyone's hidden feelings.
w3122.zh: 這個故事由一位無所不知的敘述者講述，他知道每個人隱藏的感受。

w3123.en: Bears are **omnivorous** animals, eating both ripe berries and small fish.
w3123.zh: 熊是雜食性動物，既吃成熟的漿果，也吃小魚。

w3124.en: Caring for three young children alone proved an **onerous** daily responsibility.
w3124.zh: 獨自照顧三個年幼的孩子被證明是一項繁重的日常責任。

w3125.en: The sudden **onrush** of cold water swept the small boat onto the rocks.
w3125.zh: 一股突如其來的冷水奔流把那艘小船沖上了岩石。

w3126.en: Doctors monitored the patient closely after the sudden **onset** of the fever.
w3126.zh: 在發燒突然發病後，醫生密切監測這名病人。

w3127.en: The town could not withstand the fierce **onslaught** of the advancing army.
w3127.zh: 這座城鎮無法抵擋進攻軍隊的猛烈攻擊。

w3128.en: The polished stone showed a soft **opalescence** as it caught the morning light.
w3128.zh: 這塊拋光的石頭在迎著晨光時呈現出柔和的乳光。

w3129.en: The bathroom window was made of **opaque** glass to keep out prying eyes.
w3129.zh: 浴室的窗戶是用不透明的玻璃做的，以擋住窺探的目光。

w3130.en: The new machine can **operate** continuously for twelve hours without overheating.
w3130.zh: 這台新機器可以連續運轉十二小時而不會過熱。

w3131.en: The old regulation is no longer **operative**, having been repealed last year.
w3131.zh: 那條舊規定已不再有效，去年就被廢除了。

w3132.en: The crane **operator** carefully lifted the heavy beam onto the building's roof.
w3132.zh: 起重機操作員小心地把那根沉重的橫樑吊到大樓的屋頂上。

w3133.en: The audience enjoyed the light, humorous **operetta** performed at the town theatre.
w3133.zh: 觀眾欣賞了在市立劇院上演的輕鬆幽默的輕歌劇。

w3134.en: In my honest **opinion**, the second proposal is far more practical than the first.
w3134.zh: 依我誠實的看法，第二個提案遠比第一個來得實際。

w3135.en: She studied her **opponent** carefully before the crucial chess match began.
w3135.zh: 在關鍵的西洋棋對局開始前，她仔細地研究了她的對手。

w3136.en: He waited for an **opportune** moment to raise the delicate subject with his boss.
w3136.zh: 他等待一個合適的時機向他的老闆提起這個微妙的話題。

w3137.en: As a shrewd **opportunist**, he seized every chance that promised quick profit.
w3137.zh: 作為一個精明的機會主義者，他抓住每一個能帶來快速利潤的機會。

w3138.en: Studying abroad gave her a rare **opportunity** to learn a new language.
w3138.zh: 出國留學給了她一個學習新語言的難得機會。

w3139.en: My quiet sister has the exact **opposite** personality to my noisy brother.
w3139.zh: 我文靜的妹妹和我吵鬧的弟弟有著截然相反的個性。

w3140.en: The disgraced minister faced public **opprobrium** after the scandal became widely known.
w3140.zh: 醜聞廣為人知後，這位名譽掃地的部長面臨大眾的譴責。

w3141.en: The surgeon carefully avoided damaging the delicate **optic** nerve during the operation.
w3141.zh: 外科醫生在手術中小心避免損傷脆弱的視神經。

w3142.en: The **optician** measured my eyes and recommended a stronger pair of glasses.
w3142.zh: 驗光師量了我的眼睛，並建議配一副度數更高的眼鏡。

w3143.en: He studied **optics** to understand how lenses bend and focus rays of light.
w3143.zh: 他研讀光學，以了解透鏡如何使光線彎曲和聚焦。

w3144.en: Despite the setback, her natural **optimism** kept the whole team hopeful.
w3144.zh: 儘管遭遇挫折，她與生俱來的樂觀讓整個團隊保持希望。

w3145.en: Walking to work is a healthier **option** than sitting in traffic every morning.
w3145.zh: 步行上班是比每天早上塞在車陣中更健康的選擇。

w3146.en: She trained in **optometry** so she could test patients' vision accurately.
w3146.zh: 她接受驗光的訓練，以便能準確地檢測病人的視力。

w3147.en: The palace impressed every visitor with its sheer **opulence** and golden decorations.
w3147.zh: 這座宮殿以其十足的奢華和金色裝飾令每位訪客印象深刻。

w3148.en: The wealthy banker lived in an **opulent** mansion filled with rare antiques.
w3148.zh: 這位富有的銀行家住在一座擺滿珍稀古董的奢華豪宅裡。

w3149.en: The students sat an **oral** examination to test their spoken French.
w3149.zh: 學生們參加了一場口試以測試他們的法語口語。

w3150.en: The senator began to **orate** passionately about the need for fairer laws.
w3150.zh: 那位參議員開始慷慨激昂地演講，闡述對更公平法律的需求。

w3151.en: The president delivered a moving **oration** at the soldiers' memorial service.
w3151.zh: 總統在士兵的追悼會上發表了一篇感人的演說。

w3152.en: As a gifted **orator**, he could hold a vast audience spellbound for hours.
w3152.zh: 作為一位天才的演說家，他能讓廣大的聽眾入迷數小時。

w3153.en: The choir performed a magnificent **oratorio** based on an ancient biblical story.
w3153.zh: 合唱團演出了一齣根據古老聖經故事改編的壯麗神劇。

w3154.en: His brilliant **oratory** persuaded even the most doubtful members of the crowd.
w3154.zh: 他出色的雄辯說服了人群中連最存疑的成員。

w3155.en: Crossing the frozen mountains alone was a terrible **ordeal** for the explorers.
w3155.zh: 獨自穿越冰封的群山對探險者來說是一場可怕的磨難。

w3156.en: In English, words like first and second are examples of an **ordinal**.
w3156.zh: 在英語中，像 first 和 second 這樣的詞就是序數詞的例子。

w3157.en: After years of study, the young man finally received his **ordination** as a priest.
w3157.zh: 經過多年的學習，這位年輕人終於被授予聖職成為神父。

w3158.en: The army stored its heavy **ordnance** safely in a fortified underground bunker.
w3158.zh: 軍隊把重型火炮安全地存放在一個加固的地下碉堡裡。

w3159.en: The wild **orgy** of celebration lasted late into the warm summer night.
w3159.zh: 這場狂歡的慶祝活動一直持續到溫暖夏夜的深夜。

w3160.en: Scholars still debate the exact **origin** of this ancient and mysterious custom.
w3160.zh: 學者們仍在爭論這項古老而神秘習俗的確切起源。

w3161.en: The museum proudly displays the **original** painting rather than a printed copy.
w3161.zh: 博物館自豪地展出這幅原作，而非印刷的複製品。

w3162.en: Many common English words actually **originate** from older Latin and Greek roots.
w3162.zh: 許多常見的英語詞彙其實源自更古老的拉丁文和希臘文詞根。

w3163.en: The cathedral ceiling was covered with **ornate** carvings of leaves and angels.
w3163.zh: 大教堂的天花板上佈滿了葉子和天使的華麗雕刻。

w3164.en: Many people still hold **orthodox** views about how families should be organised.
w3164.zh: 許多人對於家庭應如何組織仍持有正統的觀點。

w3165.en: The young scientist boldly challenged the prevailing **orthodoxy** of her field.
w3165.zh: 這位年輕科學家大膽挑戰她領域中盛行的正統觀念。

w3166.en: In geometry, two **orthogonal** lines meet to form a perfect right angle.
w3166.zh: 在幾何學中，兩條正交的線相交形成一個完美的直角。

w3167.en: The injured athlete was fitted with a special **orthopedic** brace for support.
w3167.zh: 這位受傷的運動員被裝上了一個提供支撐的特製矯形護具。

w3168.en: The **orthopedist** recommended surgery to repair the runner's badly damaged knee.
w3168.zh: 矯形外科醫師建議動手術修復這位跑者嚴重受損的膝蓋。

w3169.en: The compass needle began to **oscillate** wildly near the powerful magnet.
w3169.zh: 指南針的指針在那塊強力磁鐵附近開始劇烈地來回擺動。

w3170.en: The shy couple paused to **osculate** gently beneath the old garden archway.
w3170.zh: 這對害羞的伴侶在老花園的拱門下停下來輕輕地接吻。

w3171.en: Rigid traditions can **ossify** an organisation and prevent any fresh thinking.
w3171.zh: 僵硬的傳統會使一個組織僵化，並阻礙任何新的思維。

w3172.en: His expensive car was bought purely for show and shameless **ostentation**.
w3172.zh: 他那輛昂貴的車純粹是為了炫耀和無恥的賣弄而買的。

w3173.en: The whistle-blower faced years of cruel **ostracism** from his former colleagues.
w3173.zh: 這名告密者多年來面臨前同事殘酷的排擠。

w3174.en: The other children began to **ostracize** the newcomer for no real reason.
w3174.zh: 其他孩子毫無真正理由地開始排擠這個新來的人。

w3175.en: You really **ought** to apologise for the careless mistake you made yesterday.
w3175.zh: 你真的應該為昨天犯下的粗心錯誤道歉。

w3176.en: The angry citizens voted to **oust** the corrupt mayor from his office.
w3176.zh: 憤怒的市民投票將這名貪腐的市長趕下臺。

w3177.en: The critic dismissed the film as an **out-and-out** failure from start to finish.
w3177.zh: 影評人把這部電影貶為從頭到尾徹頭徹尾的失敗之作。

w3178.en: The sudden **outbreak** of flu forced the school to close for a week.
w3178.zh: 流感的突然爆發迫使學校停課一週。

w3179.en: His angry **outburst** of frustration surprised the normally calm colleagues.
w3179.zh: 他憤怒的挫折情緒爆發讓平時冷靜的同事們感到驚訝。

w3180.en: The lonely **outcast** wandered from town to town, never finding a real home.
w3180.zh: 這個孤獨的受排斥者從一個城鎮流浪到另一個城鎮，從未找到一個真正的家。

w3181.en: The factory closure sparked a furious public **outcry** across the whole region.
w3181.zh: 工廠關閉在整個地區引發了憤怒的公眾抗議。

w3182.en: The twins constantly tried to **outdo** each other in every school competition.
w3182.zh: 這對雙胞胎在每場校內比賽中都不斷試圖勝過對方。

w3183.en: The artist wore an **outlandish** costume covered in feathers and tiny mirrors.
w3183.zh: 這位藝術家穿著一套佈滿羽毛和小鏡子的稀奇古怪服裝。

w3184.en: These sturdy old boots will surely **outlast** any cheaper modern pair.
w3184.zh: 這雙堅固的舊靴子肯定會比任何更便宜的現代靴子更耐穿。

w3185.en: The government voted to **outlaw** the dangerous practice across the entire country.
w3185.zh: 政府投票在全國範圍內取締這項危險的做法。

w3186.en: Many tortoises **outlive** their owners by several remarkable decades.
w3186.zh: 許多陸龜比牠們的主人多活了好幾個驚人的十年。

w3187.en: They spent their honeymoon in a quiet, **out-of-the-way** village in the mountains.
w3187.zh: 他們在山中一個寧靜偏僻的村莊度過了蜜月。

w3188.en: The soldiers manned a lonely **outpost** far beyond the edge of the empire.
w3188.zh: 士兵們駐守在遠超出帝國邊緣的一個孤立前哨基地。

w3189.en: The cruel decision provoked widespread **outrage** among ordinary citizens everywhere.
w3189.zh: 這項殘酷的決定在各地的普通市民中激起了廣泛的憤慨。

w3190.en: Charging such **outrageous** prices for basic food is simply unacceptable.
w3190.zh: 對基本食物收取如此駭人的價格根本令人無法接受。

w3191.en: The clinic runs programmes to **outreach** medical care to remote rural communities.
w3191.zh: 這家診所推行計劃，把醫療照護的服務範圍擴大到偏遠的鄉村社區。

w3192.en: They went for a pleasant **outride** through the green hills on horseback.
w3192.zh: 他們騎著馬穿越青翠的山丘，享受了一趟愉快的騎馬外出。

w3193.en: The canoe had a wooden **outrigger** to keep it steady in rough waves.
w3193.zh: 這艘獨木舟裝有木製的舷外浮材，以在洶湧的波浪中保持平穩。

w3194.en: The committee rejected the flawed proposal **outright** without any further discussion.
w3194.zh: 委員會徹底駁回了這個有缺陷的提案，沒有任何進一步的討論。

w3195.en: The swimmer **outstretched** her arms to reach the edge of the pool.
w3195.zh: 那位游泳者伸出雙臂去搆泳池的邊緣。

w3196.en: Demand for the new phone quickly began to **outstrip** the available supply.
w3196.zh: 對這款新手機的需求很快開始超過可用的供應量。

w3197.en: The clear benefits of the plan greatly **outweigh** its small possible risks.
w3197.zh: 這項計劃明顯的好處遠大於它微小的可能風險。

w3198.en: Try not to **overdo** the exercise, or your tired muscles will ache tomorrow.
w3198.zh: 運動不要做得過頭，否則你疲累的肌肉明天會痠痛。

w3199.en: The patient was rushed to hospital after taking a dangerous **overdose** of pills.
w3199.zh: 這名病人在服用了危險的過量藥丸後被緊急送往醫院。

w3200.en: It is easy to **overeat** when delicious food is constantly placed before you.
w3200.zh: 當美味的食物不斷擺在你面前時，很容易就會吃得太多。

w3201.en: Climbers sheltered beneath a rocky **overhang** during the sudden mountain storm.
w3201.zh: 登山者在突如其來的山中暴風雨中躲在一處岩石突出物下避難。

w3202.en: In his haste, the editor managed to **overleap** an obvious spelling error.
w3202.zh: 匆忙之中，這位編輯竟忽略了一個明顯的拼寫錯誤。

w3203.en: The cruel **overlord** demanded heavy taxes from every village under his control.
w3203.zh: 這位殘暴的領主向他控制下的每個村莊徵收沉重的稅賦。

w3204.en: Drivers can use the new **overpass** to avoid the busy junction below.
w3204.zh: 駕駛人可以利用新的天橋來避開下方繁忙的路口。

w3205.en: The company tends to **overpay** its executives while underpaying ordinary staff.
w3205.zh: 這家公司傾向於給主管過高的報酬，卻給普通員工過低的薪水。

w3206.en: Three officers struggled to **overpower** the violent and desperate intruder.
w3206.zh: 三名警員費力地制服了那名兇暴而絕望的闖入者。

w3207.en: Chronic **overproduction** of goods caused prices to collapse across the market.
w3207.zh: 商品的長期生產過剩導致整個市場價格崩跌。

w3208.en: Do not **overreach** yourself by promising more than you can possibly deliver.
w3208.zh: 不要因為承諾超出你所能交付的而讓自己伸得過遠。

w3209.en: After the harvest, hungry rats began to **overrun** the entire grain store.
w3209.zh: 收成過後，飢餓的老鼠開始在整座糧倉裡橫行肆虐。

w3210.en: A senior manager was hired to **oversee** the whole construction project.
w3210.zh: 公司聘請了一位資深經理來監督整個建設專案。

w3211.en: The strict **overseer** watched the labourers closely throughout the long working day.
w3211.zh: 嚴厲的監工在漫長的工作日中密切注視著工人們。

w3212.en: Her brilliant sister tended to **overshadow** her at every family gathering.
w3212.zh: 她才華洋溢的姊姊在每次家庭聚會中總讓她相形見絀。

w3213.en: The eager runner tried to **overstride** the leader on the final bend.
w3213.zh: 這位急切的跑者試圖在最後的彎道超過領先者。

w3214.en: The rebels plotted for years to **overthrow** the harsh military government.
w3214.zh: 叛軍密謀多年要推翻這個殘酷的軍政府。

w3215.en: His polite words carried a faint **overtone** of warning and quiet threat.
w3215.zh: 他禮貌的言辭帶有一絲警告和無聲威脅的弦外之音。

w3216.en: The orchestra played a stirring **overture** before the opera's first act began.
w3216.zh: 在歌劇第一幕開始之前，樂團演奏了一段激動人心的序曲。

w3217.en: The doctor warned that being slightly **overweight** could still strain the heart.
w3217.zh: 醫生警告說，即使稍微超重仍可能對心臟造成負擔。

w3218.en: The nanny tried to **pacify** the crying baby with a gentle lullaby.
w3218.zh: 保姆試著用輕柔的搖籃曲安撫哭泣的嬰兒。

w3219.en: She tore open a fresh **packet** of biscuits and offered one to everyone.
w3219.zh: 她撕開一包新的餅乾，分給每個人一塊。

w3220.en: The two nations signed a historic peace **pact** to end the long war.
w3220.zh: 這兩個國家簽署了一項歷史性的和平條約，以結束長久的戰爭。

w3221.en: In ancient times, the **pagan** villagers worshipped many gods of nature.
w3221.zh: 在古代，這些異教徒村民崇拜許多自然之神。

w3222.en: The town held a colourful historical **pageant** to mark its five hundredth anniversary.
w3222.zh: 這座城鎮舉辦了一場色彩繽紛的歷史劇來慶祝它的五百週年。

w3223.en: The spicy curry was far too hot for her delicate young **palate**.
w3223.zh: 這道辛辣的咖哩對她細膩的年輕味覺來說實在太辣了。

w3224.en: The wealthy family lived in a **palatial** home with marble floors throughout.
w3224.zh: 這個富裕的家庭住在一棟全屋鋪著大理石地板的宮殿般豪宅裡。

w3225.en: She studies **paleontology** to learn how dinosaurs lived millions of years ago.
w3225.zh: 她研讀古生物學，以了解恐龍在數百萬年前是如何生活的。

w3226.en: The artist mixed bright colours on her wooden **palette** before painting.
w3226.zh: 這位藝術家在作畫前先在她的木製調色板上調出鮮豔的顏色。

w3227.en: The poet later wrote a **palinode** retracting the harsh words of his earlier verse.
w3227.zh: 這位詩人後來寫了一首翻案詩，收回他早期詩作中嚴厲的言辭。

w3228.en: Even the most exciting hobby can eventually **pall** after too much repetition.
w3228.zh: 即使是最令人興奮的嗜好，在過度重複之後最終也會變得乏味。

w3229.en: These tablets only **palliate** the pain; they cannot cure the disease itself.
w3229.zh: 這些藥片只能緩解疼痛，無法治癒疾病本身。

w3230.en: The frightened boy turned **pallid** when he heard the strange noise upstairs.
w3230.zh: 那個受驚的男孩聽到樓上奇怪的聲響時臉色變得蒼白。

w3231.en: There was a **palpable** sense of tension in the silent waiting room.
w3231.zh: 寂靜的候診室裡瀰漫著一種可感知的緊張氣氛。

w3232.en: A severe stroke left the elderly man with **palsy** down one side.
w3232.zh: 一次嚴重的中風使這位年長男子的一側身體癱瘓。

w3233.en: After his long illness, his face still looked thin and slightly **paly**.
w3233.zh: 在久病之後，他的臉看起來仍然消瘦且略顯蒼白。

w3234.en: The tour guide handed each visitor a small **pamphlet** about the castle's history.
w3234.zh: 導遊發給每位訪客一本關於城堡歷史的小冊子。

w3235.en: The reformer began to **pamphleteer** tirelessly against the unfair new taxes.
w3235.zh: 這位改革者開始不知疲倦地撰寫小冊子，抨擊不公平的新稅。

w3236.en: There is no single **panacea** that will solve all of society's complex problems.
w3236.zh: 沒有任何單一的萬靈丹能解決社會所有複雜的問題。

w3237.en: The trade agreement aimed to strengthen **Pan-American** cooperation across the whole continent.
w3237.zh: 這項貿易協定旨在加強整個美洲大陸的泛美合作。

w3238.en: Health officials worked desperately to contain the spreading **pandemic** before it worsened.
w3238.zh: 衛生官員拼命努力，想在疫情惡化之前控制這場蔓延的大規模流行病。

w3239.en: When the result was announced, sheer **pandemonium** broke out among the fans.
w3239.zh: 當結果宣佈時，球迷之間爆發了一片騷動。

w3240.en: The biographer wrote a glowing **panegyric** on the late leader's many achievements.
w3240.zh: 這位傳記作家為已故領袖的諸多成就寫了一篇熱情洋溢的頌詞。

w3241.en: A **panel** of experts gathered to assess the safety of the new bridge.
w3241.zh: 一個專家小組聚集起來評估這座新橋的安全性。

w3242.en: Do not **panic** if the lights go out; the backup generator starts automatically.
w3242.zh: 如果燈熄了，不要驚慌，備用發電機會自動啟動。

w3243.en: The exhibition displayed a dazzling **panoply** of costumes from many different cultures.
w3243.zh: 這場展覽展示了來自許多不同文化、琳琅滿目的服飾。

w3244.en: From the summit we admired a breathtaking **panorama** of the snowy valley below.
w3244.zh: 從山頂我們欣賞著下方雪谷令人屏息的全景。

w3245.en: Ancient **pantheism** taught that a divine spirit dwelt within all living things.
w3245.zh: 古代的泛神論教導說，一種神聖的靈寓於所有生物之中。

w3246.en: That young pianist now belongs firmly in the **pantheon** of great musical talents.
w3246.zh: 那位年輕鋼琴家如今穩居偉大音樂才華的名流之列。

w3247.en: Every Christmas the children eagerly watched the funny **pantomime** at the local theatre.
w3247.zh: 每年聖誕節，孩子們都熱切地觀賞當地劇院滑稽的童話劇。

w3248.en: The photographer used a **pantoscope** to capture the entire sweeping mountain range.
w3248.zh: 這位攝影師用廣角照相機捕捉了整片綿延的山脈。

w3249.en: His **papacy** lasted nearly twenty years and brought many changes to the church.
w3249.zh: 他的教皇任期持續了將近二十年，為教會帶來許多變革。

w3250.en: The museum carefully preserves an ancient scroll written on fragile **papyrus**.
w3250.zh: 博物館仔細保存著一卷寫在脆弱紙莎草紙上的古老卷軸。

w3251.en: The teacher used a famous **parable** to explain the value of kindness to strangers.
w3251.zh: 老師用一個著名的寓言故事，向學生說明善待陌生人的價值。

w3252.en: It is a strange **paradox** that the more we have, the less satisfied we feel.
w3252.zh: 這是個奇怪的悖論：我們擁有得越多，反而感到越不滿足。

w3253.en: She was regarded as a **paragon** of patience throughout the long and difficult negotiations.
w3253.zh: 在漫長而艱難的談判中，她被視為耐心的典範。

w3254.en: His rapid rise in the company **paralleled** the firm's own remarkable growth that decade.
w3254.zh: 他在公司的迅速晉升，與該公司那十年間驚人的成長同步發生。

w3255.en: The poet used **parallelism** to give the verses a strong and memorable rhythm.
w3255.zh: 這位詩人運用排比，使詩句帶有強烈而令人難忘的節奏。

w3256.en: Years of indecision led to a complete **paralysis** in the government's economic policy.
w3256.zh: 多年的猶豫不決，導致政府的經濟政策完全陷入癱瘓。

w3257.en: A sudden power failure can **paralyze** the entire transport network within just a few minutes.
w3257.zh: 突然停電可能在短短幾分鐘內使整個交通網絡陷入癱瘓。

w3258.en: For the surgeon, the patient's safety remains of **paramount** importance during every operation.
w3258.zh: 對這位外科醫生來說，病人的安全在每次手術中都是至上的考量。

w3259.en: The novel tells the tragic story of a queen and her secret **paramour**.
w3259.zh: 這部小說講述了一位王后與她秘密情人的悲劇故事。

w3260.en: He arrived at the lake carrying all the **paraphernalia** needed for a day of fishing.
w3260.zh: 他帶著垂釣一整天所需的全套用具來到湖邊。

w3261.en: Could you **paraphrase** that complicated paragraph so the younger students can understand it?
w3261.zh: 你能把那段複雜的文字改述一下，好讓年紀較小的學生看懂嗎？

w3262.en: She began to **pare** the apples carefully before adding them to the pie.
w3262.zh: 她小心地削蘋果皮，然後再把蘋果放進派裡。

w3263.en: Little is known about the artist's **parentage**, since the records were lost long ago.
w3263.zh: 關於這位藝術家的家世人們所知甚少，因為相關紀錄早已遺失。

w3264.en: After the scandal, the once-respected banker became a social **pariah** overnight.
w3264.zh: 醜聞爆發後，這位曾受敬重的銀行家一夜之間成了被社會拋棄的人。

w3265.en: The old church serves a quiet rural **parish** of only a few hundred families.
w3265.zh: 這座古老的教堂服務著一個只有數百戶人家的寧靜鄉村教區。

w3266.en: The café offered a relaxed, distinctly **Parisian** atmosphere right in the heart of London.
w3266.zh: 這家咖啡館在倫敦市中心提供了一種輕鬆而明顯帶有巴黎風情的氛圍。

w3267.en: The union demanded **parity** with workers doing identical jobs in other regional factories.
w3267.zh: 工會要求與其他地區工廠中從事相同工作的工人享有同等待遇。

w3268.en: In legal **parlance**, this kind of agreement is usually called a binding contract.
w3268.zh: 用法律術語來說，這類協議通常被稱為具約束力的合約。

w3269.en: The two generals agreed to **parley** with each other before the fighting resumed.
w3269.zh: 兩位將軍同意在戰事重啟前先彼此會談。

w3270.en: The new law was finally approved after a long debate in **parliament**.
w3270.zh: 經過議會中的長時間辯論，這項新法律終於獲得通過。

w3271.en: On Sunday afternoons the family would gather for tea in the cosy front **parlor**.
w3271.zh: 每逢週日下午，全家人會聚在舒適的前廳裡喝茶。

w3272.en: The comedy show cleverly **parodied** several well-known politicians and their speaking styles.
w3272.zh: 這個喜劇節目巧妙地滑稽模仿了幾位知名政治人物及其說話風格。

w3273.en: Linguists note that "flour" and "flower" are **paronymous**, sounding alike but spelt differently.
w3273.zh: 語言學家指出，flour 與 flower 是同音而拼法不同的字。

w3274.en: In a sudden **paroxysm** of rage, he swept all the papers off the desk.
w3274.zh: 在一陣突發的暴怒中，他把桌上所有的文件都掃落在地。

w3275.en: In ancient drama, **parricide** was considered among the most terrible of all possible crimes.
w3275.zh: 在古代戲劇中，弒親被視為所有罪行中最為可怕的一種。

w3276.en: Students learn to **parse** each sentence to identify its subject, verb, and object.
w3276.zh: 學生學習對每個句子作文法分析，以辨識其主詞、動詞與受詞。

w3277.en: Their **parsimonious** manager refused to spend money even on basic office supplies.
w3277.zh: 他們那位吝嗇的經理連基本的辦公用品都不肯花錢買。

w3278.en: Under the old custom, the family land was **partible** among all the surviving sons.
w3278.zh: 依照舊習俗，這份家族土地可在所有在世的兒子之間分配。

w3279.en: Every **participant** in the study received a small payment for their valuable time.
w3279.zh: 這項研究的每位參與者都因其寶貴的時間而獲得一筆小額報酬。

w3280.en: All employees are strongly encouraged to **participate** in the company's annual safety training.
w3280.zh: 公司強烈鼓勵所有員工參加一年一度的安全訓練。

w3281.en: A thin glass **partition** separated the manager's office from the main working area.
w3281.zh: 一道薄玻璃隔板把經理辦公室與主要工作區隔開。

w3282.en: The newspaper was criticised for its openly **partisan** coverage of the election.
w3282.zh: 這份報紙因對選舉作出公然偏袒的報導而受到批評。

w3283.en: As a deeply **passible** child, she was easily moved to tears by sad stories.
w3283.zh: 身為一個極為易感動的孩子，她很容易因悲傷的故事而落淚。

w3284.en: He took a **passive** role in the discussion, rarely offering opinions of his own.
w3284.zh: 他在討論中扮演被動的角色，很少提出自己的意見。

w3285.en: The poem paints a peaceful **pastoral** scene of shepherds resting beside a stream.
w3285.zh: 這首詩描繪了牧羊人在溪邊休息的寧靜鄉村景象。

w3286.en: He inherited his calm temperament from his **paternal** grandfather, a quiet country doctor.
w3286.zh: 他從擔任鄉村醫生的祖父那裡，繼承了沉著的性情。

w3287.en: A simple blood test was used to establish the child's **paternity** beyond doubt.
w3287.zh: 一項簡單的血液檢測被用來毫無疑問地確認這孩子的父親身分。

w3288.en: The film's final scene carries a deep **pathos** that stays with the audience.
w3288.zh: 這部電影的最後一幕帶有深沉的感染力，久久縈繞在觀眾心中。

w3289.en: As the family **patriarch**, the old man made every major decision for the household.
w3289.zh: 身為家族的族長，這位老人替全家做出每一個重大決定。

w3290.en: Her elegant manners and refined accent revealed her clearly **patrician** upbringing.
