w3906.en: Queen Victoria came to **reign** over a vast and powerful empire.
w3906.zh: 維多利亞女王開始統治一個幅員遼闊而強盛的帝國。

w3907.en: The company agreed to **reimburse** employees for travel expenses incurred on business.
w3907.zh: 公司同意償還員工因公出差所產生的旅費。

w3908.en: The rider pulled gently on the **rein** to slow the trotting horse.
w3908.zh: 騎手輕輕拉動繮繩，讓小跑的馬慢下來。

w3909.en: The court ordered the company to **reinstate** the unfairly dismissed worker.
w3909.zh: 法院命令公司讓那名被不公平解僱的工人復職。

w3910.en: The teacher had to **reiterate** the instructions several times before everyone understood.
w3910.zh: 老師不得不把指示重申好幾遍，大家才聽懂。

w3911.en: After the short break, the delegates **rejoined** their colleagues in the main hall.
w3911.zh: 短暫休息後，代表們在主會堂與同事重新會合。

w3912.en: A relaxing holiday by the sea seemed to **rejuvenate** the exhausted teacher.
w3912.zh: 在海邊度過的悠閒假期似乎讓這位筋疲力盡的老師恢復了活力。

w3913.en: Ancient legends often celebrate the **rejuvenescence** of heroes through magical springs.
w3913.zh: 古老的傳說常頌揚英雄藉由神奇泉水返老還童。

w3914.en: The patient seemed better but soon began to **relapse** into illness.
w3914.zh: 病人似乎好轉了，但很快又開始舊病復發。

w3915.en: Managers tried to **relegate** the unpopular task to a junior assistant.
w3915.zh: 主管們試圖把這項不受歡迎的工作下放給一名初級助理。

w3916.en: After hours of pleading, her strict father finally began to **relent**.
w3916.zh: 在苦苦哀求數小時後，她嚴厲的父親終於開始心軟。

w3917.en: Please include only information that is directly **relevant** to the research question.
w3917.zh: 請只納入與研究問題直接相關的資訊。

w3918.en: The region's heavy **reliance** on tourism makes its economy rather fragile.
w3918.zh: 該地區對旅遊業的嚴重依賴使其經濟相當脆弱。

w3919.en: Many remote villages remain heavily **reliant** on a single export crop.
w3919.zh: 許多偏遠村莊仍嚴重依賴單一的出口作物。

w3920.en: The ageing director was reluctant to **relinquish** control of the family business.
w3920.zh: 年邁的董事不情願放棄對家族企業的控制權。

w3921.en: The cathedral displayed a jewelled **reliquary** containing a revered saint's bones.
w3921.zh: 大教堂展示了一個鑲滿珠寶、裝有一位受敬奉聖人遺骨的聖骨盒。

w3922.en: After the marathon, the runners clearly **relished** their well-earned hot meal.
w3922.zh: 馬拉松結束後，跑者們顯然享受著他們應得的熱騰騰餐點。

w3923.en: She agreed to the plan, though with obvious **reluctance** in her voice.
w3923.zh: 她同意了這個計畫，儘管聲音裡帶著明顯的不情願。

w3924.en: Many witnesses are **reluctant** to testify openly against powerful criminals.
w3924.zh: 許多證人不情願公開作證指控勢力強大的罪犯。

w3925.en: The town holds a quiet service in **remembrance** of the fallen soldiers.
w3925.zh: 小鎮舉行了一場肅穆的儀式，以紀念陣亡的士兵。

w3926.en: Over dinner, my grandfather shared fond **reminiscence** of his childhood village.
w3926.zh: 晚餐時，我祖父分享了他對童年村莊的美好追憶。

w3927.en: The melody was strangely **reminiscent** of a lullaby my mother once sang.
w3927.zh: 這段旋律出奇地令人想起我母親曾唱過的一首搖籃曲。

w3928.en: It would be **remiss** of me not to thank everyone for their help.
w3928.zh: 如果我不感謝大家的幫助，那就是我的疏忽了。

w3929.en: Doctors were delighted that the cancer had finally gone into **remission**.
w3929.zh: 醫生們很高興癌症終於進入了緩解期。

w3930.en: The owners plan to **remodel** the old kitchen with modern fittings.
w3930.zh: 屋主計畫用現代化的配件改造這間舊廚房。

w3931.en: Despite loud **remonstrance** from residents, the council approved the new motorway.
w3931.zh: 儘管居民大聲抗議，議會仍批准了新的高速公路。

w3932.en: The **remonstrant** villagers gathered outside the town hall to voice their anger.
w3932.zh: 抗議的村民聚集在市政廳外表達他們的憤怒。

w3933.en: Several parents **remonstrated** with the principal about the sudden fee increase.
w3933.zh: 幾位家長就突然漲學費一事向校長提出抗議。

w3934.en: The firm agreed to **remunerate** the consultant generously for her extra work.
w3934.zh: 公司同意為這位顧問的額外工作給予豐厚的酬勞。

w3935.en: Staff expect fair **remuneration** for the long hours they work each week.
w3935.zh: 員工期望他們每週長時間的工作能得到公平的報酬。

w3936.en: The city became a vibrant centre of art during the Italian **Renaissance**.
w3936.zh: 在義大利文藝復興時期，這座城市成為充滿活力的藝術中心。

w3937.en: The spies arranged a secret **rendezvous** in a quiet café downtown.
w3937.zh: 間諜們在市中心一家僻靜的咖啡館安排了一次秘密會面。

w3938.en: The singer's heartfelt **rendition** of the old ballad moved the entire audience.
w3938.zh: 歌手對這首古老民謠真摯的詮釋打動了全場觀眾。

w3939.en: The owners spent a fortune to **renovate** the crumbling Victorian townhouse.
w3939.zh: 屋主花了一大筆錢翻新這棟搖搖欲墜的維多利亞式聯排房屋。

w3940.en: His public **renunciation** of violence surprised both his allies and enemies.
w3940.zh: 他公開聲明放棄暴力，令他的盟友與敵人都感到驚訝。

w3941.en: Management decided to **reorganize** the department to improve overall efficiency.
w3941.zh: 管理層決定改組這個部門以提升整體效率。

w3942.en: Fortunately the damage to the painting was minor and easily **reparable**.
w3942.zh: 幸好這幅畫的損壞輕微，很容易修復。

w3943.en: The defeated nation was required to pay heavy **reparation** after the war.
w3943.zh: 戰敗國在戰後被要求支付沉重的賠償。

w3944.en: The two comedians delighted the crowd with their quick, witty **repartee**.
w3944.zh: 兩位喜劇演員以他們敏捷詼諧的應答逗樂了觀眾。

w3945.en: Parliament voted to **repeal** the outdated law later that same year.
w3945.zh: 國會在同年稍晚投票廢除了這條過時的法律。

w3946.en: A thin coating of wax helps **repel** water from the fabric surface.
w3946.zh: 一層薄薄的蠟有助於使織物表面不沾水。

w3947.en: Campers sprayed insect **repellent** to keep the mosquitoes away at dusk.
w3947.zh: 露營者噴灑驅蟲劑，在黃昏時把蚊子趕走。

w3948.en: He showed genuine **repentance** and apologised sincerely for the harm caused.
w3948.zh: 他表現出真誠的悔恨，並為所造成的傷害誠摯道歉。

w3949.en: The local theatre presents several classic plays in **repertory** each season.
w3949.zh: 當地劇院每季以保留劇目輪演的方式上演數齣經典戲劇。

w3950.en: Constant **repetition** of the new vocabulary helped the students memorise it quickly.
w3950.zh: 對新詞彙不斷的重複幫助學生迅速記住它。

w3951.en: There is little point in **repining** over chances that have already passed.
w3951.zh: 為已經錯過的機會而苦惱埋怨是沒什麼意義的。

w3952.en: We must **replenish** our supplies of food before the long journey ahead.
w3952.zh: 在前方的長途旅程之前，我們必須補充我們的食物儲備。

w3953.en: The ancient library was **replete** with manuscripts on history and philosophy.
w3953.zh: 這座古老的圖書館充滿了關於歷史與哲學的手稿。

w3954.en: The museum displayed a perfect **replica** of the famous ancient warship.
w3954.zh: 博物館展出了那艘著名古代戰艦的完美複製品。

w3955.en: The dusty old archive served as a **repository** of forgotten local history.
w3955.zh: 那個積滿灰塵的舊檔案室是被遺忘的地方歷史的寶庫。

w3956.en: Most readers would **reprehend** the politician's dishonest handling of public funds.
w3956.zh: 大多數讀者都會譴責這位政客對公款不誠實的處理方式。

w3957.en: His **reprehensible** behaviour towards the staff led to his immediate dismissal.
w3957.zh: 他對員工應受斥責的行為導致他立即被解僱。

w3958.en: The report contained sharp **reprehension** of the company's careless safety practices.
w3958.zh: 這份報告對該公司草率的安全做法提出了嚴厲的譴責。

w3959.en: He struggled to **repress** his anger during the tense board meeting.
w3959.zh: 在緊張的董事會議上，他努力壓抑自己的怒氣。

w3960.en: The early symptoms were mild and seemed entirely **repressible** with rest.
w3960.zh: 早期症狀輕微，似乎靠休息就完全可以抑制。

w3961.en: The governor decided to **reprieve** the prisoner just hours before the execution.
w3961.zh: 州長在處決前幾小時決定對這名囚犯緩期執行。

w3962.en: The teacher had to **reprimand** the pupil for repeatedly disrupting the lesson.
w3962.zh: 老師不得不為這名學生屢次擾亂課堂而訓斥他。

w3963.en: The attack was launched as a swift **reprisal** for the earlier raid.
w3963.zh: 這次攻擊是針對先前突襲所發動的迅速報復。

w3964.en: The novel's villain is a heartless **reprobate** who betrays everyone around him.
w3964.zh: 小說中的反派是一個背叛身邊每個人的無情惡棍。

w3965.en: This printer can faithfully **reproduce** detailed colour photographs at high resolution.
w3965.zh: 這台印表機能以高解析度忠實複製細緻的彩色照片。

w3966.en: The gallery sells affordable **reproduction** of several famous Impressionist paintings.
w3966.zh: 這家畫廊出售幾幅著名印象派畫作的平價複製品。

w3967.en: The manager issued a stern **reproof** for the careless mistakes in the report.
w3967.zh: 經理為報告中粗心的錯誤發出了嚴厲的責備。

w3968.en: The scientist firmly **repudiated** the false claims made about her research.
w3968.zh: 這位科學家堅決否認外界對她研究所作的不實指控。

w3969.en: She felt deep **repugnance** at the cruelty shown towards the helpless animals.
w3969.zh: 她對那些無助動物所受的殘忍對待感到深深的厭惡。

w3970.en: Most people find the idea of cheating their friends utterly **repugnant**.
w3970.zh: 大多數人都覺得欺騙朋友的念頭令人極度反感。

w3971.en: The defenders managed to **repulse** the enemy's attack along the river.
w3971.zh: 守軍設法在河邊擊退了敵人的進攻。

w3972.en: The thick smell of rotting fish was utterly **repulsive** to the visitors.
w3972.zh: 腐魚的濃烈氣味令訪客感到極為反感。

w3973.en: The old inn was widely **reputed** to be the finest in the county.
w3973.zh: 這家老旅館被廣泛認為是全郡最好的旅館。

w3974.en: The choir sang a solemn **requiem** for the victims of the disaster.
w3974.zh: 唱詩班為這場災難的罹難者唱了一首莊嚴的安魂曲。

w3975.en: A valid passport is a **requisite** document for travelling across the border.
w3975.zh: 有效的護照是跨越邊境旅行所必需的文件。

w3976.en: The villagers offered gifts in **requital** for the stranger's timely help.
w3976.zh: 村民們獻上禮物，以報答這位陌生人及時的幫助。

w3977.en: She hoped one day to **requite** the kindness her neighbours had shown her.
w3977.zh: 她希望有一天能報答鄰居們對她展現的善意。

w3978.en: The committee voted to **rescind** the controversial rule introduced last spring.
w3978.zh: 委員會投票撤銷了去年春天推出的那條有爭議的規定。

w3979.en: The carpenter offered to **reseat** the wobbly antique chairs at a fair price.
w3979.zh: 木匠提出以公道的價格為這些搖晃的古董椅重裝新座面。

w3980.en: There is a striking **resemblance** between the young actor and his famous father.
w3980.zh: 這位年輕演員與他著名的父親之間有著驚人的相似之處。

w3981.en: Many workers deeply **resent** being asked to work without any extra pay.
w3981.zh: 許多工人對被要求無償加班感到非常憤恨。

w3982.en: The city draws its drinking water from a large mountain **reservoir**.
w3982.zh: 這座城市的飲用水取自一座大型的山區水庫。

w3983.en: A sticky **residue** of sugar remained at the bottom of the cup.
w3983.zh: 杯底殘留著黏稠的糖漬殘餘物。

w3984.en: Children often show remarkable **resilience** when adjusting to difficult new circumstances.
w3984.zh: 孩子在適應艱難的新環境時，往往展現出非凡的復原力。

w3985.en: The local economy proved surprisingly **resilient** despite the global financial crisis.
w3985.zh: 儘管面臨全球金融危機，當地經濟仍展現出驚人的復原力。

w3986.en: The new vaccine greatly increases the body's **resistance** to the virus.
w3986.zh: 這種新疫苗大幅提高了人體對該病毒的抵抗力。

w3987.en: Older employees were initially quite **resistant** to the new computer system.
w3987.zh: 年長的員工起初對新的電腦系統相當抗拒。

w3988.en: This coating makes the surface highly **resistive** to scratches and corrosion.
w3988.zh: 這層塗料使表面對刮痕與腐蝕具有很強的抵抗力。

w3989.en: The flood gathered such force that it seemed almost **resistless**.
w3989.zh: 洪水匯聚了如此強大的力量，彷彿幾乎無法抵抗。

w3990.en: The singer's deep voice has a warm **resonance** that fills the hall.
w3990.zh: 歌手深沉的嗓音帶有一種充滿整個大廳的溫暖共鳴。

w3991.en: The actor's **resonant** voice carried clearly to the back of the theatre.
w3991.zh: 演員洪亮的嗓音清晰地傳到劇院的後排。

w3992.en: The poet's themes of loss still **resonate** with readers a century later.
w3992.zh: 這位詩人關於失去的主題，在一個世紀後仍能引起讀者的共鳴。

w3993.en: The country's mineral **resource** attracted investment from companies all over the world.
w3993.zh: 這個國家的礦產資源吸引了來自世界各地公司的投資。

w3994.en: The cool evening breeze offered a welcome **respite** from the day's heat.
w3994.zh: 涼爽的晚風為人們提供了從白日酷熱中得來的可喜喘息。

w3995.en: The bride looked utterly **resplendent** in her embroidered silk wedding gown.
w3995.zh: 新娘身著繡花絲質婚紗，看起來光彩奪目。

w3996.en: More than half the **respondent** group agreed that public transport needed improvement.
w3996.zh: 超過半數的回答者一致認為大眾運輸需要改善。

w3997.en: The court ordered full **restitution** of the money taken from the victims.
w3997.zh: 法院下令全額歸還從受害者那裡奪走的錢。

w3998.en: The **resumption** of peace talks gave the war-weary nation fresh hope.
w3998.zh: 和平談判的重新開始給這個飽受戰爭之苦的國家帶來了新的希望。

w3999.en: Analysts noted a **resurgent** interest in vinyl records among younger listeners.
w3999.zh: 分析師注意到黑膠唱片在年輕聽眾中再度興起的興趣。

w4000.en: The town witnessed a remarkable **resurrection** of its once-dying textile industry.
w4000.zh: 這座小鎮見證了其一度衰落的紡織業驚人的復興。

w4001.en: Paramedics worked hard to **resuscitate** the drowning child before the ambulance arrived.
w4001.zh: 在救護車抵達前，急救人員努力使溺水的孩子甦醒過來。

w4002.en: If the company lowers its prices, competitors will surely **retaliate** with deeper discounts.
w4002.zh: 如果這家公司降價，競爭對手肯定會以更大的折扣進行反擊。

w4003.en: The terrible smell from the bin made several passengers begin to **retch**.
w4003.zh: 垃圾桶傳出的難聞氣味讓好幾名乘客開始乾嘔。

w4004.en: Frequent revision greatly improves the long-term **retention** of new vocabulary.
w4004.zh: 頻繁複習能大幅提升新詞彙的長期保留率。

w4005.en: Her natural **reticence** made it difficult for colleagues to know her true opinions.
w4005.zh: 她天生的沈默寡言讓同事很難得知她真正的想法。

w4006.en: He was unusually **reticent** about his past, rarely mentioning his earlier career.
w4006.zh: 他對自己的過去異常沈默寡言，很少提及早年的職業生涯。

w4007.en: The visiting queen arrived with a large **retinue** of advisers and servants.
w4007.zh: 來訪的女王帶著一大批顧問和僕從的隨行人員抵達。

w4008.en: "That's none of your business," was her sharp **retort** to the rude question.
w4008.zh: 「這不關你的事」是她對那個無禮問題的尖銳反駁。

w4009.en: The photographer spent hours trying to **retouch** the faded wedding portrait.
w4009.zh: 攝影師花了好幾個小時試圖修飾那張褪色的婚禮肖像。

w4010.en: The lost hikers decided to **retrace** their steps back to the main trail.
w4010.zh: 迷路的登山客決定沿原路返回主要的步道。

w4011.en: Under pressure from critics, the minister was forced to **retract** his earlier statement.
w4011.zh: 在批評者的壓力下，部長被迫撤回他先前的聲明。

w4012.en: Faced with falling profits, the firm had to **retrench** by closing several branches.
w4012.zh: 面對利潤下滑，這家公司不得不藉由關閉數家分店來緊縮開支。

w4013.en: It took the engineers weeks to **retrieve** the data from the damaged hard drive.
w4013.zh: 工程師花了好幾週才從損壞的硬碟中找回那些資料。

w4014.en: The new tax law is **retroactive**, applying to income earned last year too.
w4014.zh: 這項新稅法具有追溯效力，也適用於去年所賺取的收入。

w4015.en: Critics argued the policy would **retrograde** the progress made over the past decade.
w4015.zh: 批評者認為這項政策會使過去十年取得的進步倒退。

w4016.en: Economists warned that the reforms could trigger a serious economic **retrogression**.
w4016.zh: 經濟學家警告，這些改革可能引發嚴重的經濟倒退。

w4017.en: In **retrospect**, leaving that stable job was the best decision I ever made.
w4017.zh: 回顧起來，離開那份穩定的工作是我做過最好的決定。

w4018.en: The museum is hosting a **retrospective** exhibition covering forty years of her paintings.
w4018.zh: 博物館正在舉辦一場涵蓋她四十年畫作的回顧展。

w4019.en: After years apart, the charity helped **reunite** the refugee child with her parents.
w4019.zh: 分離多年後，這個慈善機構幫助難民孩童與她的父母重聚。

w4020.en: The journalist's report contained a shocking **revelation** about the company's hidden debts.
w4020.zh: 這名記者的報導揭露了關於該公司隱藏債務的驚人內情。

w4021.en: Students across the country **revere** the old professor for his lifelong dedication.
w4021.zh: 全國各地的學生都因這位老教授畢生的奉獻而崇敬他。

w4022.en: The pilgrims stood in **reverent** silence before the ancient stone temple.
w4022.zh: 朝聖者在這座古老的石廟前肅然起敬地靜默站立。

w4023.en: Doctors observed a slow **reversion** to her former healthy weight after treatment.
w4023.zh: 醫生觀察到她在治療後緩慢回復到先前的健康體重。

w4024.en: Without regular practice, beginners often **revert** to their old bad habits.
w4024.zh: 若缺乏定期練習，初學者常會回復到他們舊有的壞習慣。

w4025.en: Online commenters began to **revile** the author for his controversial remarks.
w4025.zh: 網路評論者開始因這位作者具爭議的言論而辱罵他。

w4026.en: The editor sent the manuscript back for a thorough **revisal** before publication.
w4026.zh: 編輯在出版前把稿件退回，要求進行徹底的修訂。

w4027.en: You should **revise** the first chapter carefully before submitting your final draft.
w4027.zh: 在繳交最終稿之前，你應該仔細修訂第一章。

w4028.en: The court ordered the immediate **revocation** of the company's trading licence.
w4028.zh: 法院下令立即撤銷該公司的營業執照。

w4029.en: The authorities decided to **revoke** his passport after the visa violation.
w4029.zh: 當局在他違反簽證規定後決定撤銷他的護照。

w4030.en: The senator delivered a passionate **rhapsody** on the beauty of the wild coastline.
w4030.zh: 參議員發表了一段熱情洋溢的演講，讚頌野生海岸線的美麗。

w4031.en: The politician's speech was full of empty **rhetoric** but offered no real solutions.
w4031.zh: 這位政客的演說充滿浮誇之詞，卻沒有提出任何實際的解決方案。

w4032.en: As a skilled **rhetorician**, she could persuade almost any audience to agree.
w4032.zh: 身為一位嫻熟的演說家，她幾乎能說服任何聽眾表示贊同。

w4033.en: The comedian's **ribald** jokes made some audience members laugh and others blush.
w4033.zh: 這位喜劇演員粗俗詼諧的笑話讓部分觀眾大笑，也讓其他人臉紅。

w4034.en: Everyone felt a sense of **riddance** when the noisy machines were finally removed.
w4034.zh: 當那些吵雜的機器終於被搬走時，每個人都感到一種解脫。

w4035.en: The young inventor faced constant **ridicule** before her idea proved successful.
w4035.zh: 這位年輕發明家在她的構想證明成功之前，不斷遭到嘲笑。

w4036.en: It seems **ridiculous** to drive two hours just to buy a cup of coffee.
w4036.zh: 光是為了買一杯咖啡就開兩小時的車，似乎很荒唐。

w4037.en: Corruption was **rife** throughout the department before the new director arrived.
w4037.zh: 在新主管到任之前，這個部門裡貪腐橫行。

w4038.en: The reformer was admired for his **righteousness** and refusal to accept bribes.
w4038.zh: 這位改革者因其正直以及拒絕收賄而受人景仰。

w4039.en: After a long legal battle, she finally became the **rightful** owner of the land.
w4039.zh: 經過漫長的法律訴訟後，她終於成為這塊土地的合法所有人。

w4040.en: Renewing the licence involved an exhausting **rigmarole** of forms and interviews.
w4040.zh: 換發執照涉及一套令人疲憊不堪、繁瑣費時的表格與面談手續。

w4041.en: Few students were prepared for the academic **rigor** of the graduate programme.
w4041.zh: 很少有學生為這個研究所課程的學術嚴格程度做好準備。

w4042.en: The findings were accepted only after **rigorous** testing in several laboratories.
w4042.zh: 這些研究結果是在數間實驗室經過嚴謹的測試後才被接受。

w4043.en: A gentle breeze sent a tiny **ripplet** across the surface of the pond.
w4043.zh: 一陣輕柔的微風在池塘的水面上掀起一道小漣漪。

w4044.en: His attempt to look serious in that costume was almost **risible**.
w4044.zh: 他穿著那套服裝想裝出嚴肅的樣子，幾乎令人發笑。

w4045.en: A clear **rivulet** trickled down the hillside and joined the larger stream.
w4045.zh: 一道清澈的小溪沿著山坡涓涓流下，匯入更大的溪流。

w4046.en: The new bridge was designed to be **robust** enough to survive major earthquakes.
w4046.zh: 這座新橋的設計足夠堅固，能夠承受大型地震。

w4047.en: The pianist ended her recital with a lively **rondo** by Mozart.
w4047.zh: 這位鋼琴家以一首莫札特活潑的迴旋曲結束了她的獨奏會。

w4048.en: Naturalists climbed the cliff to study the noisy **rookery** in the tall pines.
w4048.zh: 博物學家攀上懸崖，研究高大松樹上那片吵雜的禿鼻烏鴉巢群。

w4049.en: The old printing press relied on a large **rotary** drum to feed the paper.
w4049.zh: 這台老式印刷機依靠一個巨大的旋轉式滾筒來輸送紙張。

w4050.en: The Earth takes roughly twenty-four hours to **rotate** once on its axis.
w4050.zh: 地球繞著自己的軸自轉一圈大約需要二十四小時。

w4051.en: Learning grammar purely by **rote** rarely helps students speak fluently.
w4051.zh: 純粹靠死記硬背學習文法，很少能幫助學生流利地說話。

w4052.en: The cheerful, **rotund** baker greeted every customer with a warm smile.
w4052.zh: 這位開朗、圓胖的麵包師傅以溫暖的微笑迎接每一位顧客。

w4053.en: The textbook offers only a **rudimentary** introduction to advanced economic theory.
w4053.zh: 這本教科書僅對進階經濟理論提供了粗淺的介紹。

w4054.en: Many investors came to **rue** their hasty decision once the market collapsed.
w4054.zh: 市場崩盤後，許多投資人開始對自己倉促的決定感到懊悔。

w4055.en: A gang of **ruffian** youths had been frightening shopkeepers in the area.
w4055.zh: 一幫兇惡的青少年一直在恐嚇這一帶的店主。

w4056.en: Cows and sheep are **ruminant** animals that chew their food a second time.
w4056.zh: 牛和羊是會把食物再咀嚼一次的反芻動物。

w4057.en: She liked to **ruminate** quietly over a problem before suggesting any solution.
w4057.zh: 她喜歡在提出任何解決方案之前，靜靜地對問題長時間思考。

w4058.en: The sudden increase in pressure caused the old pipe to **rupture**.
w4058.zh: 壓力突然增加導致那條老舊的管線破裂。

w4059.en: We spent the weekend in a charming **rustic** cottage beside a quiet lake.
w4059.zh: 我們在一座寧靜湖畔迷人的鄉村小屋裡度過了週末。

w4060.en: The professor analysed the loyalty shown by the heroine in **Ruth**.
w4060.zh: 教授分析了《路得記》中女主角所展現的忠誠。

w4061.en: Many parents willingly **sacrifice** their own comfort for their children's education.
w4061.zh: 許多父母心甘情願為了孩子的教育而犧牲自己的舒適。

w4062.en: Archaeologists discovered a stone altar used for **sacrificial** ceremonies.
w4062.zh: 考古學家發現了一座用於獻祭儀式的石製祭壇。

w4063.en: Damaging the ancient shrine was considered an unforgivable **sacrilege**.
w4063.zh: 破壞這座古老的神龕被視為不可饒恕的褻瀆神聖之舉。

w4064.en: To the villagers, building a shop on the holy site seemed deeply **sacrilegious**.
w4064.zh: 對村民來說，在這塊聖地上蓋商店似乎是極度褻瀆神明的。

w4065.en: New regulations were introduced to **safeguard** workers against unfair dismissal.
w4065.zh: 當局引入新規定，以保護勞工免於遭到不公平的解僱。

w4066.en: The committee relied on the **sagacious** advice of its most experienced member.
w4066.zh: 委員會仰賴其最資深成員睿智的建議。

w4067.en: The newspaper was criticised for printing **salacious** stories about celebrities.
w4067.zh: 這家報紙因刊登關於名人的淫穢報導而受到批評。

w4068.en: The report stressed the **salience** of climate change to future economic planning.
w4068.zh: 這份報告強調了氣候變遷對於未來經濟規劃的重要性。

w4069.en: The lecturer summarised the most **salient** points of the lengthy report.
w4069.zh: 講師總結了這份冗長報告中最顯著的幾個重點。

w4070.en: Crops will not grow well in the highly **saline** soil near the coast.
w4070.zh: 作物在海岸附近含鹽分極高的土壤中無法生長良好。

w4071.en: The harsh defeat had a **salutary** effect on the overconfident young team.
w4071.zh: 這場慘敗對過度自信的年輕隊伍產生了有益的影響。

w4072.en: He began his letter with a polite **salutation** to the entire committee.
w4072.zh: 他在信的開頭以一句禮貌的稱呼語向整個委員會致意。

w4073.en: The top graduate delivered the **salutatory** at the crowded ceremony.
w4073.zh: 這位頂尖畢業生在擠滿人的典禮上致了畢業歡迎辭。

w4074.en: Divers managed to **salvage** several valuable artefacts from the sunken ship.
w4074.zh: 潛水員設法從沉船中搶救出數件珍貴的文物。

w4075.en: The soldiers fired a thunderous **salvo** to mark the general's arrival.
w4075.zh: 士兵們鳴放了一陣震耳欲聾的禮炮齊射，以標誌將軍的到來。

w4076.en: His **sanctimonious** tone annoyed colleagues who knew his own faults well.
w4076.zh: 他那道貌岸然的口吻惹惱了那些深知他自身缺點的同事。

w4077.en: The board refused to **sanction** any further spending until the audit was complete.
w4077.zh: 董事會在審計完成之前拒絕批准任何進一步的支出。

w4078.en: Many cultures emphasise the **sanctity** of human life above all else.
w4078.zh: 許多文化都將人類生命的神聖置於一切之上。

w4079.en: Historians described the battle as one of the most **sanguinary** of the war.
w4079.zh: 歷史學家把這場戰役描述為這場戰爭中最血腥的戰役之一。

w4080.en: Despite the setbacks, the manager remained **sanguine** about reaching the annual target.
w4080.zh: 儘管遭遇挫折，經理對達成年度目標仍然保持樂觀。

w4081.en: The wound left a deep **sanguineous** stain on the white bandage.
w4081.zh: 這道傷口在白色繃帶上留下了一塊深紅的血漬。

w4082.en: The chef added fresh herbs to make the simple broth more **sapid**.
w4082.zh: 主廚加入新鮮香草，讓這道簡單的高湯更有味道。

w4083.en: His pretended **sapience** quickly faded once the experts asked detailed questions.
w4083.zh: 當專家們問起細節問題時，他裝出來的聰明很快就露了餡。

w4084.en: Humans are often described as the only truly **sapient** species on Earth.
w4084.zh: 人類常被描述為地球上唯一真正具有智慧、會思考的物種。

w4085.en: The old monk shared his **sapiential** insights with the young students.
w4085.zh: 這位老僧與年輕的學生們分享他充滿智慧的見解。

w4086.en: The clay felt strangely **saponaceous**, slipping easily through her fingers.
w4086.zh: 這團黏土摸起來出奇地像肥皂，輕易地從她指間滑落。

w4087.en: There was a clear note of **sarcasm** in his supposedly polite reply.
w4087.zh: 在他那看似禮貌的回覆中，有著明顯的諷刺意味。

w4088.en: The museum displayed an ancient Egyptian **sarcophagus** carved with detailed figures.
w4088.zh: 博物館展出了一具刻著精細人物圖像的古埃及石棺。

w4089.en: He gave a **sardonic** smile when his rival's plan finally collapsed.
w4089.zh: 當對手的計畫終於失敗時，他露出了譏諷的微笑。

w4090.en: The lavish buffet was enough to **satiate** even the hungriest of guests.
w4090.zh: 這頓豐盛的自助餐足以讓即使最飢餓的賓客也吃得飽足。

w4091.en: The play is a sharp **satire** on the greed of modern corporations.
w4091.zh: 這齣戲是對現代企業貪婪的尖銳諷刺。

w4092.en: The magazine is famous for its **satiric** cartoons about leading politicians.
w4092.zh: 這本雜誌以諷刺主要政治人物的漫畫而聞名。

w4093.en: The author uses humour to **satirize** the snobbery of the upper classes.
w4093.zh: 作者運用幽默來諷刺上流階層的勢利。

w4094.en: In the painting, a mischievous **satyr** dances at the edge of the forest.
w4094.zh: 在這幅畫中，一個頑皮的薩特在森林邊緣跳舞。

w4095.en: The lone traveller was **savage**ly attacked by a wild dog on the path.
w4095.zh: 這名獨自旅行的人在小路上遭到一隻野狗兇猛地攻擊。

w4096.en: After the long hike, we sat down to **savor** a simple but delicious meal.
w4096.zh: 在漫長的健行之後，我們坐下來細細品嚐一頓簡單卻美味的餐點。

w4097.en: The knight slid his polished sword smoothly back into its leather **scabbard**.
w4097.zh: 騎士將他擦得發亮的劍順暢地滑回皮製的劍鞘中。

w4098.en: The prolonged drought led to a severe **scarcity** of clean drinking water.
w4098.zh: 長期的乾旱導致乾淨飲用水嚴重短缺。

w4099.en: Her **scholarly** article was praised for its careful use of primary sources.
w4099.zh: 她那篇學術性的文章因謹慎使用第一手資料而備受讚譽。

w4100.en: The school measures **scholastic** achievement through both exams and class projects.
w4100.zh: 這所學校透過考試和課堂專題來衡量學業成就。

w4101.en: There was not a **scintilla** of evidence to support his serious accusation.
w4101.zh: 沒有絲毫證據能支持他那項嚴重的指控。

w4102.en: Her witty remarks seemed to **scintillate** throughout the lively dinner party.
w4102.zh: 在這場熱鬧的晚宴上，她機智的話語彷彿妙語如珠。

w4103.en: The original study was narrow, leaving plenty of **scope** for further research.
w4103.zh: 最初的研究範圍狹窄，因此留下了充分進行後續研究的餘地。

w4104.en: The novel's villain is a charming **scoundrel** who cheats everyone he meets.
w4104.zh: 這部小說的反派是個迷人的惡棍，欺騙他所遇見的每一個人。

w4105.en: The toddler began to **scribble** colourful shapes all over the clean wall.
w4105.zh: 那名幼兒開始在乾淨的牆上到處亂塗五顏六色的圖案。

w4106.en: Before printing existed, a **scribe** would copy each book entirely by hand.
w4106.zh: 在印刷術出現之前，抄寫員會完全用手抄寫每一本書。

w4107.en: The director asked the writer to revise the **script** before filming began.
w4107.zh: 導演要求編劇在開拍前修改劇本。

w4108.en: The scholar examined several **Scriptural** references in the medieval manuscript.
w4108.zh: 這位學者檢視了這份中世紀手稿中數處源出聖經的引文。

w4109.en: She had no **scruple** about reporting the fraud, even to her own friends.
w4109.zh: 她毫無顧忌地舉報那起詐欺，即使對象是她自己的朋友。

w4110.en: The accountant was admired for her **scrupulous** attention to every detail.
w4110.zh: 這位會計師因對每個細節都嚴謹認真而受人讚賞。

w4111.en: The newspaper printed a **scurrilous** attack on the mayor's private life.
w4111.zh: 這家報紙刊登了一篇詆毀市長私生活的文章。

w4112.en: The frightened crabs began to **scuttle** sideways across the wet sand.
w4112.zh: 受驚的螃蟹開始在潮濕的沙地上橫著碎步快跑。

w4113.en: The old farmer used a long **scythe** to cut the tall summer grass.
w4113.zh: 那位老農夫用一把長柄大鐮刀割除高高的夏季野草。

w4114.en: In the gloomy old house, they once held a **seance** to contact spirits.
w4114.zh: 在那棟陰森的老宅裡，他們曾舉行降神會試圖與亡靈溝通。

w4115.en: The cook let the hot pan **sear** the steak quickly on both sides.
w4115.zh: 廚師讓滾燙的平底鍋將牛排兩面快速地高溫煎過。

w4116.en: Overactive **sebaceous** glands can sometimes cause oily skin in teenagers.
w4116.zh: 皮脂腺分泌過度有時會導致青少年皮膚出油。

w4117.en: In the diagram, the **secant** line crosses the circle at two distinct points.
w4117.zh: 在這張圖中，那條割線在兩個不同的點上與圓相交。

w4118.en: Several northern regions threatened to **secede** from the central government.
w4118.zh: 數個北部地區揚言要脫離中央政府。

w4119.en: The proposed **secession** from the union sparked fierce debate across the nation.
w4119.zh: 擬議中脫離聯邦的行動在全國引發了激烈的辯論。

w4120.en: The writer chose to **seclude** himself in a remote cabin to finish his novel.
w4120.zh: 這位作家選擇把自己隔離在一間偏遠的小屋裡完成他的小說。

w4121.en: After retiring, she lived in peaceful **seclusion** deep in the mountains.
w4121.zh: 退休後，她在深山裡過著寧靜的隱居生活。

w4122.en: Air pollution is a **secondary** concern compared with the immediate flood damage.
w4122.zh: 與眼前的洪災損害相比，空氣污染是次要的問題。

w4123.en: First, gather your sources; **secondly**, organise them into a clear outline.
w4123.zh: 首先，蒐集你的資料來源；其次，把它們整理成清晰的大綱。

w4124.en: The hotel looked impressive online but turned out to be rather **second-rate**.
w4124.zh: 這家飯店在網路上看起來令人印象深刻，結果卻相當二流。

w4125.en: The negotiations were conducted in complete **secrecy** to avoid public panic.
w4125.zh: 這些談判在完全保密的狀態下進行，以避免引發大眾恐慌。

w4126.en: The minister asked her **secretary** to arrange the meeting for early Monday.
w4126.zh: 部長要求她的祕書把會議安排在週一一早。

w4127.en: He was strangely **secretive** about where he had spent the weekend.
w4127.zh: 關於他週末去了哪裡，他顯得異常隱隱藏藏。

w4128.en: Despite the chaos around her, she remained remarkably calm and **sedate**.
w4128.zh: 儘管周遭一片混亂，她仍然出奇地冷靜與沉著。

w4129.en: A **sedentary** lifestyle with little exercise can seriously harm your health.
w4129.zh: 久坐不動且少運動的生活方式可能嚴重危害你的健康。

w4130.en: Over centuries, layers of **sediment** built up at the bottom of the lake.
w4130.zh: 歷經數個世紀，一層層的沉積物在湖底逐漸堆積。

w4131.en: The pamphlets were banned because the government considered them an act of **sedition**.
w4131.zh: 這些小冊子遭到查禁，因為政府視其為煽動叛亂的行為。

w4132.en: The editor was arrested for publishing **seditious** articles against the regime.
w4132.zh: 這名編輯因發表反對政權的煽動性文章而遭到逮捕。

w4133.en: Clever advertisements often try to **seduce** shoppers into buying things they don't need.
w4133.zh: 巧妙的廣告常試圖誘惑消費者去購買他們並不需要的東西。

w4134.en: Her **sedulous** efforts over many years finally earned her the prestigious award.
w4134.zh: 她多年來孜孜不倦的努力終於為她贏得了這項著名的獎項。

w4135.en: The villagers treated the old **seer** as someone who could foretell the future.
w4135.zh: 村民們把這位年邁的預言家當作能夠預知未來的人。

w4136.en: The crowd outside the courthouse began to **seethe** with anger after the verdict.
w4136.zh: 判決出爐後，法院外的群眾開始因憤怒而群情激動。

w4137.en: In the old kingdom, each **seignior** controlled a vast stretch of farmland.
w4137.zh: 在這個古老的王國裡，每位領主都掌控著一大片農地。

w4138.en: The **seismograph** recorded a series of tremors deep beneath the ocean floor.
w4138.zh: 地震儀記錄到海床深處發生的一連串震動。

w4139.en: A good entrepreneur must **seize** opportunities before competitors even notice them.
w4139.zh: 優秀的創業家必須在競爭對手注意到之前就抓住機會。

w4140.en: The committee was highly **selective**, accepting only a handful of applicants.
w4140.zh: 委員會非常有選擇性，只錄取了少數幾位申請者。

w4141.en: Losing his job badly damaged his **self-respect** for several months.
w4141.zh: 失業在好幾個月裡嚴重損害了他的自尊心。

w4142.en: The two paintings bore a striking **semblance** to each other in style.
w4142.zh: 這兩幅畫在風格上有著驚人的相似之處。

w4143.en: The explorers described the remote tribe as living in a **semicivilized** state.
w4143.zh: 探險家把這個偏遠的部落描述為處於半文明的狀態。

w4144.en: The patient was only **semiconscious** and could barely answer the nurse's questions.
w4144.zh: 這名病人只處於半清醒狀態，幾乎無法回答護士的問題。

w4145.en: The company publishes a **semiannual** report every June and December.
w4145.zh: 這家公司在每年六月和十二月發布半年期報告。

w4146.en: The children arranged their chairs in a neat **semicircle** around the storyteller.
w4146.zh: 孩子們把椅子整齊地排成半圓形，圍著說故事的人。

w4147.en: Each week the graduate students present their research at a small **seminar**.
w4147.zh: 研究生們每週在一場小型研討班上發表他們的研究。

w4148.en: He spent four years at a **seminary** before becoming a parish priest.
w4148.zh: 他在成為教區神父之前，在神學院待了四年。

w4149.en: The novel sensitively portrays an old man slowly becoming **senile**.
w4149.zh: 這部小說細膩地描繪了一位老人逐漸變得老態龍鍾。

w4150.en: After the injection, she lost all **sensation** in her lower arm.
w4150.zh: 打完針後，她下臂完全失去了知覺。

w4151.en: As soon as I entered the room, I could **sense** the tension in the air.
w4151.zh: 我一走進房間，就能感覺到空氣中的緊張氣氛。

w4152.en: The poet was praised for her unusual **sensibility** to sound and rhythm.
w4152.zh: 這位詩人因對聲音與節奏的獨特敏感而受到讚揚。

w4153.en: Newborn babies are extremely **sensitive** to sudden loud noises.
w4153.zh: 新生兒對突然的巨大聲響極為敏感。

w4154.en: The lecture described the brain's **sensorium** as the centre of all perception.
w4154.zh: 這場講座把大腦的感覺中樞描述為一切知覺的核心。

w4155.en: The film was criticised for emphasising **sensual** pleasure over genuine emotion.
w4155.zh: 這部電影因強調肉體上的享樂多過真摯的情感而受到批評。

w4156.en: The garden offered a **sensuous** mix of bright colours and sweet scents.
w4156.zh: 這座花園提供了一種訴諸美感、融合鮮豔色彩與甜美香氣的體驗。

w4157.en: Please write one complete **sentence** to explain your main argument clearly.
w4157.zh: 請寫出一個完整的句子，清楚地說明你的主要論點。

w4158.en: Philosophers still debate whether machines could ever achieve true **sentience**.
w4158.zh: 哲學家至今仍在爭論機器是否可能獲得真正的知覺能力。

w4159.en: The film imagines a future where robots become fully **sentient** beings.
w4159.zh: 這部電影想像了一個機器人成為完全有感覺力的存在的未來。

w4160.en: A lone **sentinel** stood guard at the entrance to the ancient fortress.
w4160.zh: 一名孤獨的哨兵在這座古老堡壘的入口處站崗。

w4161.en: The two issues are closely related but are nonetheless clearly **separable**.
w4161.zh: 這兩個議題密切相關，但仍然是明顯可分離的。

w4162.en: Teachers usually **separate** the noisy children before a quiet reading lesson.
w4162.zh: 老師通常會在安靜的閱讀課之前把吵鬧的孩子分開。

w4163.en: The region's **separatist** movement demanded full independence from the central state.
w4163.zh: 該地區的分離主義運動要求完全脫離中央政府獨立。

w4164.en: The committee holds a **septennial** review of the organisation's long-term goals.
w4164.zh: 委員會每七年一次檢討該組織的長期目標。

w4165.en: The knight was buried in a stone **sepulcher** beneath the cathedral floor.
w4165.zh: 這位騎士被安葬在大教堂地板下方的一座石墓中。

w4166.en: Critics dismissed his thesis as **sequacious**, merely echoing his supervisor's ideas.
w4166.zh: 評論者將他的論文斥為缺乏獨創性，只是重複他指導教授的觀點。

w4167.en: The film's **sequel** proved even more popular than the original.
w4167.zh: 這部電影的續集結果比原作更受歡迎。

w4168.en: Please arrange the historical events in the correct **sequence**.
w4168.zh: 請把這些歷史事件按照正確的順序排列。

w4169.en: Economic decline was the inevitable **sequent** of years of poor planning.
w4169.zh: 經濟衰退是多年來規劃不善的必然結果。

w4170.en: The judge ordered the court to **sequester** the jury during the high-profile trial.
w4170.zh: 法官下令在這場備受矚目的審判期間將陪審團隔離。

w4171.en: The authorities moved to **sequestrate** the assets of the bankrupt company.
w4171.zh: 當局採取行動扣押這家破產公司的資產。

w4172.en: After years of service, he was finally promoted to **sergeant**.
w4172.zh: 服役多年後，他終於晉升為中士。

w4173.en: The **sergeant-at-arms** quietly escorted the disruptive visitor out of the chamber.
w4173.zh: 議會衛士悄悄地把那名鬧事的訪客護送出議事廳。

w4174.en: The **sergeant-major** inspected every soldier's uniform before the morning parade.
w4174.zh: 軍士長在晨間檢閱前檢查了每位士兵的制服。

w4175.en: The hotel is famous for its friendly and efficient **service**.
w4175.zh: 這家飯店以親切又有效率的服務聞名。

w4176.en: These old boots are still perfectly **serviceable** for muddy mountain walks.
w4176.zh: 這雙舊靴子用來走泥濘的山路仍然完全堪用。

w4177.en: For years the captured workers lived in harsh and unpaid **servitude**.
w4177.zh: 多年來，這些被俘的工人過著嚴苛且無報酬的奴役生活。

w4178.en: The accident caused the complete **severance** of the power line.
w4178.zh: 這起事故導致電力線路完全被切斷。

w4179.en: The young driver was **severely** punished for ignoring the warning signs.
w4179.zh: 這名年輕駕駛因無視警示標誌而受到嚴厲的懲罰。

w4180.en: The choir performed a delicate **sextet** by an unfamiliar baroque composer.
w4180.zh: 合唱團演唱了一首陌生巴洛克作曲家的細膩六重唱。

w4181.en: This year's profits were nearly **sextuple** those of the struggling first year.
w4181.zh: 今年的利潤幾乎是慘澹經營的第一年的六倍。

w4182.en: The accusation was based on **sheer** speculation rather than any solid proof.
w4182.zh: 這項指控純粹建立在臆測之上，而非任何確鑿的證據。

w4183.en: His **shiftless** attitude meant projects were rarely finished on time.
w4183.zh: 他得過且過的態度意味著專案很少能準時完成。

w4184.en: The **shrewd** investor sold her shares just before the market crashed.
w4184.zh: 這位精明的投資人在市場崩盤前剛好賣掉了她的股票。

w4185.en: The children let out a delighted **shriek** as the rollercoaster plunged downward.
w4185.zh: 當雲霄飛車俯衝而下時，孩子們發出了開心的尖叫。

w4186.en: The label warns of possible **shrinkage** if the sweater is washed in hot water.
w4186.zh: 標籤警告，若用熱水清洗這件毛衣，可能會發生收縮。

w4187.en: Without rain for weeks, the young plants began to **shrivel** in the heat.
w4187.zh: 連續數週沒有降雨，這些幼苗開始在高溫中枯萎。

w4188.en: The exhausted travellers had to **shuffle** slowly through the crowded terminal.
w4188.zh: 疲憊的旅客只能拖著腳步緩緩穿過擁擠的航廈。

w4189.en: The poet used soft **sibilance** to imitate the sound of whispering waves.
w4189.zh: 這位詩人運用柔和的絲絲聲來模仿低語般的海浪聲。

w4190.en: The snake gave a long, **sibilant** hiss before slipping into the grass.
w4190.zh: 這條蛇發出一聲悠長、絲絲作響的嘶聲，隨即溜進草叢。

w4191.en: The actor learned to **sibilate** softly to make the villain sound menacing.
w4191.zh: 這名演員學會輕輕地發出齒擦音，好讓反派聽起來充滿威脅感。

w4192.en: Astronomers measure time using **sidereal** days based on the distant stars.
w4192.zh: 天文學家利用以遙遠恆星為基準的恆星日來測量時間。

w4193.en: The castle withstood a long **siege** before its defenders finally surrendered.
w4193.zh: 這座城堡經歷了漫長的圍攻，守軍才終於投降。

w4194.en: Historians still debate the true **significance** of that ancient treaty.
w4194.zh: 歷史學家至今仍在爭論那份古老條約真正的重要性。

w4195.en: The survey revealed a **significant** rise in support for renewable energy.
w4195.zh: 這項調查顯示，民眾對再生能源的支持有了顯著的上升。

w4196.en: The dictionary lists several shades of **signification** for this single word.
w4196.zh: 這本字典為這個單字列出了好幾種細微不同的意義。

w4197.en: Your situation is very **similar** to the one I faced last year.
w4197.zh: 你的處境和我去年面對的情況非常相似。

w4198.en: The poet compared her courage to a lion in a striking **simile**.
w4198.zh: 詩人在一個生動的明喻中把她的勇氣比作獅子。

w4199.en: There is a clear **similitude** between the two ancient legends.
w4199.zh: 這兩個古老傳說之間有著明顯的相似之處。

w4200.en: The teacher used diagrams to **simplify** the complicated chemical process.
w4200.zh: 老師運用圖表來簡化這個複雜的化學過程。

w4201.en: The new software can **simulate** how a building behaves during an earthquake.
w4201.zh: 這套新軟體能夠模擬建築物在地震中的反應。

w4202.en: The two events were almost **simultaneous**, beginning within seconds of each other.
w4202.zh: 這兩起事件幾乎同時發生，間隔只有幾秒鐘。

w4203.en: Critics complained the post was a **sinecure** offering a salary for no real work.
w4203.zh: 批評者抱怨這個職位是個坐領乾薪、沒有實際工作的閒職。

w4204.en: A stray spark from the fire began to **singe** the edge of the curtain.
w4204.zh: 火堆飛出的一點火星開始把窗簾的邊緣燒焦。

w4205.en: A **sinister** shadow moved slowly across the wall of the empty corridor.
w4205.zh: 一道不祥的影子緩緩地掠過空蕩走廊的牆面。

w4206.en: The river's gentle **sinuosity** made it a favourite spot for slow canoeing.
w4206.zh: 這條河平緩的彎曲使它成為慢速划獨木舟的熱門地點。

w4207.en: The narrow path followed a **sinuous** course through the dense forest.
w4207.zh: 這條狹窄的小徑沿著一條蜿蜒的路線穿過茂密的森林。

w4208.en: A blocked nasal **sinus** can cause uncomfortable pressure around the eyes.
w4208.zh: 鼻竇阻塞可能會造成眼睛周圍不適的壓迫感。

w4209.en: The ambulance's **siren** grew louder as it raced through the busy streets.
w4209.zh: 救護車的警笛聲隨著它在繁忙的街道上疾駛而越來越響。

w4210.en: A hot, dry **sirocco** swept across the coast, leaving everyone feeling exhausted.
w4210.zh: 一股炎熱乾燥的西洛可風橫掃海岸，讓每個人都感到疲憊不堪。

w4211.en: The shelter was run by a small **sisterhood** of dedicated volunteers.
w4211.zh: 這間收容所由一個由熱忱志工組成的小型婦女團體經營。

w4212.en: As a natural **skeptic**, she questioned every claim the salesman made.
w4212.zh: 身為一個天生的懷疑論者，她質疑那名推銷員的每一項說法。

w4213.en: His deep **skepticism** about the new theory delayed its wider acceptance.
w4213.zh: 他對這項新理論深切的懷疑延緩了它更廣泛地被接受。

w4214.en: They rowed a small wooden **skiff** quietly across the calm morning lake.
w4214.zh: 他們划著一艘小木舟，靜靜地橫越清晨平靜的湖面。

w4215.en: A brief **skirmish** broke out between the rival fans outside the stadium.
w4215.zh: 敵對的球迷在體育場外爆發了一場短暫的小衝突。

w4216.en: The magician amazed the crowd with the sheer **sleight** of his fingers.
w4216.zh: 這位魔術師以他純熟的手法技巧令觀眾驚嘆不已。

w4217.en: There was only a **slight** difference between the two competing designs.
w4217.zh: 這兩個相互競爭的設計之間只有微小的差異。

w4218.en: His **slothful** habits meant the garden was soon overgrown with weeds.
w4218.zh: 他懶散的習慣意味著花園很快就長滿了雜草。

w4219.en: His brother was a hopeless **sluggard** who never finished any task.
w4219.zh: 他的哥哥是個無可救藥的懶漢，從來不曾完成任何任務。

w4220.en: Being naturally **sociable**, she quickly made friends in the new office.
w4220.zh: 由於天生善於社交，她很快就在新辦公室裡交到了朋友。

w4221.en: The lecture compared different forms of **socialism** in twentieth-century Europe.
w4221.zh: 這場講座比較了二十世紀歐洲不同形式的社會主義。

w4222.en: As a committed **socialist**, she campaigned for free healthcare for all.
w4222.zh: 身為一名堅定的社會主義者，她為全民免費醫療而奔走。

w4223.en: She studied **sociology** to understand how communities respond to rapid change.
w4223.zh: 她研讀社會學，以瞭解社群如何回應快速的變遷。

w4224.en: The choir warmed up by singing the note **sol** several times.
w4224.zh: 合唱團藉由反覆唱出「唆」這個音來暖嗓。

w4225.en: After losing the match, she found some **solace** in her friends' kind words.
w4225.zh: 輸掉比賽後，她從朋友親切的話語中找到了一些安慰。

w4226.en: The new roof panels convert **solar** energy directly into household electricity.
w4226.zh: 這些新的屋頂面板將太陽能直接轉換成家用電力。

w4227.en: The technician used hot **solder** to join the two thin copper wires.
w4227.zh: 技師用熔化的焊錫把兩條細銅線接合在一起。

w4228.en: The young **soldier** wrote letters home to his family every single week.
w4228.zh: 這名年輕士兵每週都寫信回家給他的家人。

w4229.en: Forgetting the host's name was an embarrassing social **solecism**.
w4229.zh: 忘記主人的名字是個令人尷尬的社交失禮。

w4230.en: She hired a **solicitor** to handle the complicated property dispute.
w4230.zh: 她聘請了一位事務律師來處理這樁複雜的房產糾紛。

w4231.en: His constant **solicitude** for his elderly neighbours touched the whole street.
w4231.zh: 他對年長鄰居持續不斷的關心感動了整條街的人。

w4232.en: In a famous **soliloquy**, the hero questions the meaning of his own life.
w4232.zh: 在一段著名的獨白中，主角質疑著自己生命的意義。

w4233.en: The summer **solstice** marks the longest day of the entire year.
w4233.zh: 夏至標誌著一整年中白晝最長的一天。

w4234.en: Sugar is highly **soluble** and disappears quickly when stirred into warm tea.
w4234.zh: 糖極易溶解，攪進溫熱的茶裡很快就消失不見。

w4235.en: The auditors confirmed that the small firm was still financially **solvent**.
w4235.zh: 審計人員確認這家小公司在財務上仍有償付能力。

w4236.en: The funeral was held in a quiet, **somber** chapel beside the cemetery.
w4236.zh: 喪禮在墓園旁一間安靜、肅穆的小教堂裡舉行。

w4237.en: The warm room and dull lecture had a thoroughly **somniferous** effect.
w4237.zh: 溫暖的房間和沉悶的講課產生了徹底催人入睡的效果。

w4238.en: A heavy meal often brings on a pleasant feeling of **somnolence**.
w4238.zh: 豐盛的一餐常會帶來一種愉悅的睏倦感。

w4239.en: The medicine left him feeling **somnolent** for most of the afternoon.
w4239.zh: 這種藥讓他整個下午大半時間都昏昏欲睡。

w4240.en: The student practised a difficult Beethoven **sonata** for the recital.
w4240.zh: 這名學生為了獨奏會練習一首高難度的貝多芬奏鳴曲。

w4241.en: The poet wrote a tender **sonnet** celebrating his lifelong friendship.
w4241.zh: 這位詩人寫了一首溫柔的十四行詩，歌頌他畢生的友誼。

w4242.en: The announcer's deep, **sonorous** voice filled the entire concert hall.
w4242.zh: 播音員那渾厚低沉的嗓音充滿了整個音樂廳。

w4243.en: The ancient **soothsayer** warned the king of dangers lying ahead.
w4243.zh: 那位古代的預言家警告國王前方潛藏著危險。

w4244.en: His clever **sophism** sounded convincing but collapsed under careful examination.
w4244.zh: 他巧妙的詭辯聽起來很有說服力，但在仔細檢驗下便站不住腳。

w4245.en: The lawyer's **sophistical** argument failed to fool the experienced judge.
w4245.zh: 這名律師強詞奪理的論點未能矇騙過經驗豐富的法官。

w4246.en: Years of travel had helped to **sophisticate** the once naive young man.
w4246.zh: 多年的旅行使這位曾經天真的年輕人變得世故起來。

w4247.en: The debate was full of clever **sophistry** rather than honest reasoning.
w4247.zh: 這場辯論充滿了巧妙的詭辯，而非誠實的論證。

w4248.en: The young **soprano** hit the highest notes with remarkable ease.
w4248.zh: 這位年輕的女高音輕鬆自如地唱出了最高的音。

w4249.en: In the legend, the wizard used **sorcery** to summon a fierce storm.
w4249.zh: 在這則傳說中，巫師施展巫術召喚了一場猛烈的風暴。

w4250.en: The novel exposes the **sordid** dealings hidden behind the company's clean image.
w4250.zh: 這部小說揭露了隱藏在這家公司光鮮形象背後的骯髒勾當。

w4251.en: She bought a small wooden elephant as a **souvenir** from her trip to Thailand.
w4251.zh: 她從泰國旅行帶回一隻小木象作為紀念品。

w4252.en: The desert landscape was dotted with **sparse** vegetation and a few hardy shrubs.
w4252.zh: 沙漠景觀上點綴著稀疏的植被和幾叢耐旱的灌木。

w4253.en: The hikers endured **Spartan** conditions, sleeping on the ground with little food.
w4253.zh: 健行者忍受著斯巴達式的艱苦條件，睡在地上且食物匱乏。

w4254.en: His **spasmodic** efforts to study meant his grades remained frustratingly inconsistent.
w4254.zh: 他斷斷續續發作般的讀書努力使他的成績一直令人沮喪地不穩定。

w4255.en: Many graduates choose to **specialize** in fields with strong job prospects abroad.
w4255.zh: 許多畢業生選擇專攻就業前景良好的海外領域。

w4256.en: Fresh seafood pasta is the **specialty** of this small coastal restaurant.
w4256.zh: 鮮海鮮義大利麵是這家海邊小餐廳的招牌特色菜。

w4257.en: In earlier centuries, merchants often demanded payment in **specie** rather than paper notes.
w4257.zh: 在早期幾個世紀，商人常要求以硬幣而非紙鈔付款。

w4258.en: Scientists discovered a new **species** of frog deep within the rainforest.
w4258.zh: 科學家在雨林深處發現了一種新的青蛙物種。

w4259.en: The museum displayed a remarkable **specimen** of an ancient fossilized fern.
w4259.zh: 博物館展示了一件非凡的古代蕨類化石標本。

w4260.en: His argument sounded convincing but was actually **specious** and full of hidden flaws.
w4260.zh: 他的論點聽起來令人信服，實則似是而非且充滿隱藏的漏洞。

w4261.en: Thousands of excited **spectators** filled the stadium before the championship final.
w4261.zh: 成千上萬興奮的觀眾在冠軍決賽前擠滿了體育場。

w4262.en: The old castle was said to be haunted by a ghostly **specter** at midnight.
w4262.zh: 那座古堡據說在午夜會有幽靈般的鬼怪出沒。

w4263.en: A prism splits white light into the full **spectrum** of visible colors.
w4263.zh: 稜鏡將白光分解成可見光的完整光譜。

w4264.en: Historians can only **speculate** on why the ancient city was suddenly abandoned.
w4264.zh: 歷史學家只能推測那座古城為何突然被遺棄。

w4265.en: The property **speculator** bought dozens of flats hoping prices would soar.
w4265.zh: 那名房地產投機者買下數十間公寓，希望價格會飆漲。

w4266.en: The scientist measured the **sphericity** of each tiny glass bead under a microscope.
w4266.zh: 科學家在顯微鏡下測量每顆微小玻璃珠的球狀程度。

w4267.en: The Earth is not a perfect sphere but a slightly flattened **spheroid**.
w4267.zh: 地球並非完美的球體，而是一個略微扁平的橢球體。

w4268.en: The optician used a **spherometer** to measure the curvature of the lens.
w4268.zh: 驗光師用球徑計測量鏡片的曲率。

w4269.en: The cactus was covered with sharp, **spinous** projections that deterred grazing animals.
w4269.zh: 那株仙人掌覆滿尖銳的棘狀突起，阻止了食草動物啃食。

w4270.en: In that era, an unmarried woman past thirty was unfairly labelled a **spinster**.
w4270.zh: 在那個年代，三十歲以上未婚的女子會被不公平地貼上老處女的標籤。

w4271.en: The crowd broke into **spontaneous** applause when the singer finished her song.
w4271.zh: 當歌手唱完她的歌時，群眾爆發出自發的掌聲。

w4272.en: Despite his age, the **sprightly** old gentleman danced energetically all evening.
w4272.zh: 儘管年事已高，那位老當益壯的老紳士整晚都精力充沛地跳舞。

w4273.en: The salesman made **spurious** claims about the product's miraculous health benefits.
w4273.zh: 那名推銷員對產品神奇的健康功效做出了虛假的宣稱。

w4274.en: The two children began to **squabble** noisily over who owned the toy.
w4274.zh: 兩個孩子開始為玩具歸誰所有而大聲爭吵。

w4275.en: The refugees were forced to live in **squalid**, overcrowded camps without clean water.
w4275.zh: 難民被迫住在骯髒、過度擁擠且沒有乾淨水的營地裡。

w4276.en: A **squatter** had moved into the abandoned house without paying any rent.
w4276.zh: 一名擅自佔屋者搬進了那間廢棄的房子，沒有付任何租金。

w4277.en: Mosquitoes bred quickly in the **stagnant** water of the neglected pond.
w4277.zh: 蚊子在那座無人照管的池塘裡不流動的死水中迅速繁殖。

w4278.en: Without fresh ideas, the company's sales began to **stagnate** for several years.
w4278.zh: 缺乏新的點子，公司的銷售額開始停滯了好幾年。

w4279.en: Economic **stagnation** left thousands of young graduates struggling to find work.
w4279.zh: 經濟蕭條使數以千計的年輕畢業生難以找到工作。

w4280.en: Her **stagy** gestures made the apology feel insincere and overly rehearsed.
w4280.zh: 她演戲似的誇張手勢使那道歉顯得不真誠且過度排練。

w4281.en: The **staid** professor never told jokes and disapproved of any classroom laughter.
w4281.zh: 那位古板的教授從不講笑話，並且不贊成課堂上有任何笑聲。

w4282.en: The farmer kept a powerful black **stallion** for breeding strong racehorses.
w4282.zh: 那名農夫養了一匹強壯的黑色種馬，用來培育優良的賽馬。

w4283.en: A sturdy metal **stanchion** supported the heavy beam above the doorway.
w4283.zh: 一根堅固的金屬支柱支撐著門口上方的沉重橫樑。

w4284.en: The poet read aloud the final **stanza** of his moving wartime poem.
w4284.zh: 詩人朗讀了他那首動人戰時詩作的最後一節。

w4285.en: The young leader showed remarkable **statecraft** during the difficult peace negotiations.
w4285.zh: 那位年輕領袖在艱難的和平談判中展現出非凡的治國才能。

w4286.en: The population in that remote village has remained **static** for over a decade.
w4286.zh: 那個偏遠村莊的人口十多年來一直保持靜止不變。

w4287.en: Engineering students study **statics** to understand how forces keep structures balanced.
w4287.zh: 工程系學生學習靜力學，以了解力如何使結構保持平衡。

w4288.en: The security camera detected no movement, since the figure stayed completely **stationary**.
w4288.zh: 監視器沒有偵測到任何移動，因為那個人影完全靜止不動。

w4289.en: The **statistician** analysed the survey data to reveal surprising patterns in consumer behaviour.
w4289.zh: 那位統計學家分析了調查數據，揭示出消費者行為中令人驚訝的模式。

w4290.en: The **statuesque** model stood tall and elegant under the bright runway lights.
w4290.zh: 那位身材高挑如雕像般的模特兒在明亮的伸展台燈光下顯得高挑優雅。

w4291.en: A small bronze **statuette** of a dancer stood on the writer's desk.
w4291.zh: 一尊舞者的小銅雕像立在作家的書桌上。

w4292.en: Years of groundbreaking research greatly increased the scientist's international **stature**.
w4292.zh: 多年的開創性研究大大提升了這位科學家的國際聲望。

w4293.en: Parliament passed a new **statute** to protect workers from unfair dismissal.
w4293.zh: 國會通過了一項新的成文法，以保護勞工免於不公平的解僱。

w4294.en: The thieves entered the museum by **stealth**, avoiding every security camera inside.
w4294.zh: 竊賊偷偷摸摸地進入博物館，避開了裡面每一台監視器。

w4295.en: The young actress gave a **stellar** performance that critics praised enthusiastically.
w4295.zh: 那位年輕女演員的演出極為出色，獲得評論家熱烈讚賞。

w4296.en: Herds of wild horses roamed freely across the vast Asian **steppe**.
w4296.zh: 成群的野馬在遼闊的亞洲乾草原上自由奔馳。

w4297.en: She wore a delicate bracelet made of pure **sterling** silver.
w4297.zh: 她戴著一只用標準純度純銀製成的精緻手鐲。

w4298.en: The thick smoke began to **stifle** the firefighters trapped inside the building.
w4298.zh: 濃煙開始使困在大樓內的消防員窒息。

w4299.en: Society still attaches an unfair **stigma** to people seeking help for mental illness.
w4299.zh: 社會仍對尋求精神疾病協助的人附加著不公平的恥辱。

w4300.en: She wore a pair of black **stiletto** heels to the formal dinner.
w4300.zh: 她穿著一雙黑色細高跟鞋出席那場正式晚宴。

w4301.en: Caffeine acts as a mild **stimulant** that helps many people stay awake.
w4301.zh: 咖啡因是一種溫和的刺激物，幫助許多人保持清醒。

w4302.en: Bright pictures and music can **stimulate** a young child's developing imagination.
w4302.zh: 鮮明的圖畫和音樂能激發幼兒正在發展的想像力。

w4303.en: Lower interest rates provided a strong **stimulus** to the struggling housing market.
w4303.zh: 較低的利率為陷入困境的房市提供了強而有力的刺激。

w4304.en: He was too **stingy** to leave even a small tip for the waiter.
w4304.zh: 他太吝嗇了，連給服務生一點小費都不肯。

w4305.en: The junior researcher received a modest monthly **stipend** to cover living costs.
w4305.zh: 那名初級研究員領取微薄的月薪以支付生活費用。

w4306.en: She bore her long illness with remarkable **stoicism** and quiet dignity.
w4306.zh: 她以非凡的堅忍和沉靜的尊嚴承受了長期的病痛。

w4307.en: The **stolid** guard showed no emotion even as the alarms blared loudly.
w4307.zh: 那名無動於衷的警衛即使警報大聲響起也面無表情。

w4308.en: A narrow **strait** separates the two islands by only three kilometres.
w4308.zh: 一條狹窄的海峽將這兩座島嶼隔開僅僅三公里。

w4309.en: The general devised a clever **stratagem** for capturing the enemy's supply line.
w4309.zh: 將軍想出了一個巧妙的計謀來奪取敵人的補給線。

w4310.en: Geologists examined each **stratum** of rock to date the ancient riverbed.
w4310.zh: 地質學家檢視每一層岩層，以推定這條古老河床的年代。

w4311.en: A clear **streamlet** trickled gently down the mossy slope into the valley.
w4311.zh: 一條清澈的小溪輕柔地沿著長滿青苔的斜坡流入山谷。

w4312.en: The **stringency** of the new safety rules surprised many factory managers.
w4312.zh: 新安全規定的嚴格程度令許多工廠經理感到意外。

w4313.en: The airline enforces **stringent** security checks before every international flight.
w4313.zh: 該航空公司在每趟國際航班前都實施嚴格的安全檢查。

w4314.en: The eager **stripling** begged the captain to let him join the crew.
w4314.zh: 那名熱切的年輕人懇求船長讓他加入船員行列。

w4315.en: The **studious** girl spent every evening quietly reviewing her chemistry notes.
w4315.zh: 那個勤奮好學的女孩每晚都安靜地複習她的化學筆記。

w4316.en: Endless paperwork can **stultify** even the most creative and energetic employees.
w4316.zh: 無止盡的文書工作甚至能使最有創意、最有活力的員工變得遲鈍無用。

w4317.en: From the summit, the climbers admired the **stupendous** view of the snowy peaks.
w4317.zh: 從山頂上，登山者讚嘆著雪峰那令人驚嘆的景色。

w4318.en: After the accident, the driver lay in a **stupor**, unable to speak clearly.
w4318.zh: 事故發生後，駕駛陷入恍惚昏迷的狀態，無法清楚說話。

w4319.en: Through gentle **suasion** rather than threats, she finally won the committee's support.
w4319.zh: 她憑藉溫和的說服而非威脅，最終贏得了委員會的支持。

w4320.en: The **suave** diplomat charmed everyone with his polished manners and easy smile.
w4320.zh: 那位文雅的外交官以優雅的舉止和輕鬆的微笑迷住了每一個人。

w4321.en: His **subacid** remarks about the plan hinted at quiet disapproval.
w4321.zh: 他對該計畫帶有諷刺意味的評論暗示著默默的不贊同。

w4322.en: Divers explored the **subaquatic** caves hidden beneath the rocky coastline.
w4322.zh: 潛水員探索著隱藏在岩石海岸下方的水下洞穴。

w4323.en: Many of our daily habits are driven by **subconscious** desires we rarely notice.
w4323.zh: 我們許多日常習慣是由我們鮮少察覺的潛意識慾望所驅動的。

w4324.en: Engineers tested the **subjacent** clay before laying the building's heavy foundations.
w4324.zh: 工程師在鋪設大樓沉重的地基之前，先檢測了下方的黏土層。

w4325.en: The complete **subjection** of the rebels took the empire nearly a decade.
w4325.zh: 完全征服叛軍花了帝國將近十年的時間。

w4326.en: The ancient empire sought to **subjugate** every neighbouring tribe through brutal warfare.
w4326.zh: 那個古老帝國企圖透過殘酷的戰爭征服每一個鄰近部落。

w4327.en: Advertisers sometimes use **subliminal** messages to influence shoppers without their awareness.
w4327.zh: 廣告商有時利用潛意識訊息在消費者不自覺的情況下影響他們。

w4328.en: The doctor placed a small **sublingual** tablet beneath the patient's tongue.
w4328.zh: 醫生將一顆小小的舌下錠放在病人的舌頭下方。

w4329.en: Researchers discovered a vast **submarine** valley far beneath the ocean's surface.
w4329.zh: 研究人員在海面下方深處發現了一座廣闊的海底山谷。

w4330.en: The heavy rains caused the river to **submerge** several low-lying villages.
w4330.zh: 豪雨使河水淹沒了好幾個地勢低窪的村莊。

w4331.en: The gradual **submergence** of the coastline alarmed local environmental scientists.
w4331.zh: 海岸線的逐漸沉沒使當地的環境科學家感到警覺。

w4332.en: The team used a small **submersible** to film creatures on the deep seabed.
w4332.zh: 研究團隊使用一艘小型潛水器拍攝深海海床上的生物。

w4333.en: Complete **submersion** in the language helped her learn to speak fluently.
w4333.zh: 完全沉浸在這個語言中幫助她學會了流利地說話。

w4334.en: The deadline for the grant **submission** is the last day of June.
w4334.zh: 補助申請提交的截止日期是六月的最後一天。

w4335.en: The committee reviewed every **submittal** before announcing the winning design.
w4335.zh: 委員會在宣布得獎設計之前審查了每一份提交的作品。

w4336.en: As a junior clerk, she felt her role was clearly **subordinate** to her manager's.
w4336.zh: 身為初級職員，她覺得自己的角色明顯從屬於她的經理。

w4337.en: The flooding occurred **subsequent** to several days of unusually heavy rainfall.
w4337.zh: 那場洪水發生在連續幾天異常豪雨之後。

w4338.en: His complete **subservience** to his boss earned him little respect from colleagues.
w4338.zh: 他對老闆的完全恭順屈從使他幾乎得不到同事的尊重。

w4339.en: The dictator surrounded himself with **subservient** officials who never questioned his orders.
w4339.zh: 那名獨裁者讓恭順屈從、從不質疑其命令的官員圍繞在身邊。

w4340.en: The doctor said the fever would gradually **subside** within a few days.
w4340.zh: 醫生說發燒會在幾天內逐漸平息。

w4341.en: During the expedition, the explorers had to **subsist** on dried meat and biscuits.
w4341.zh: 在探險期間，探險者必須靠乾肉和餅乾維持生計。

w4342.en: The poorest families struggled to earn enough for basic **subsistence**.
w4342.zh: 最貧窮的家庭難以賺取足夠的錢來維持基本生計。

w4343.en: The two governments finally held **substantive** talks on reducing nuclear weapons.
w4343.zh: 兩國政府最終就削減核武進行了實質性的會談。

w4344.en: In the diagram, the longest chord **subtends** the widest angle at the centre.
w4344.zh: 在圖中，最長的弦在圓心對向最大的角。

w4345.en: He gained entry to the building through a clever **subterfuge** involving fake documents.
w4345.zh: 他透過一個涉及偽造文件的巧妙花招進入了那棟大樓。

w4346.en: Workers discovered a vast **subterranean** chamber while digging the new tunnel.
w4346.zh: 工人在開鑿新隧道時發現了一個巨大的地下室。

w4347.en: There was a **subtle** difference in tone that only native speakers could detect.
w4347.zh: 語氣上有一種微妙的差異，只有母語人士才能察覺。

w4348.en: In the equation, the **subtrahend** is the number being taken away from another.
w4348.zh: 在這道算式中，減數是被從另一個數中減去的數。

w4349.en: The government accused the activists of plotting the **subversion** of the state.
w4349.zh: 政府指控那些行動者密謀顛覆國家。

w4350.en: Rebel leaders secretly worked to **subvert** the long-established military government.
w4350.zh: 叛軍領袖暗中設法顛覆那個建立已久的軍政府。

w4351.en: With enough practice and patience, most students eventually **succeed** in the final exam.
w4351.zh: 只要有足夠的練習和耐心，大多數學生最終都能在期末考中成功。

w4352.en: The film's surprising **success** turned its unknown director into a household name.
w4352.zh: 那部電影出乎意料的成功使其默默無聞的導演成為家喻戶曉的人物。

w4353.en: After years of effort, she launched a highly **successful** online clothing business.
w4353.zh: 經過多年努力，她創辦了一家非常成功的網路服飾事業。

w4354.en: The board chose an experienced engineer as the **successor** to the retiring chairman.
w4354.zh: 董事會選了一位經驗豐富的工程師作為即將退休主席的接替者。

w4355.en: The teacher praised her **succinct** summary that captured the essay's main argument.
w4355.zh: 老師稱讚她那簡明的摘要抓住了文章的主要論點。

w4356.en: The chef served tender, **succulent** roast lamb with fresh garden herbs.
w4356.zh: 主廚端上了搭配新鮮香草、鮮嫩多汁的烤羊肉。

w4357.en: Despite the team's resistance, they eventually **succumbed** to the pressure and resigned.
w4357.zh: 儘管團隊抗拒，他們最終還是屈服於壓力而辭職了。

w4358.en: The tenants remained in the flat only on the landlord's **sufferance**.
w4358.zh: 那些房客僅靠房東的容忍才得以留在公寓裡。

w4359.en: The charity ensured a **sufficiency** of food and blankets for every flood victim.
w4359.zh: 那個慈善機構確保每位水災受害者都有充足的食物和毛毯。

w4360.en: Women campaigned for decades before finally winning the right to **suffrage**.
w4360.zh: 婦女奮鬥了數十年才終於贏得選舉權。

w4361.en: At dawn, the sky was **suffused** with soft shades of pink and gold.
w4361.zh: 破曉時分，天空彌漫著柔和的粉紅與金色色調。

w4362.en: Young children are highly **suggestible** and may believe almost anything adults tell them.
w4362.zh: 幼兒極易受影響，可能相信大人告訴他們的幾乎任何事。

w4363.en: The film was banned in some countries for its **suggestive** scenes and dialogue.
w4363.zh: 那部電影因其性暗示的場景和對白在某些國家遭禁。

w4364.en: Please write a brief **summary** of the report's key findings for the meeting.
w4364.zh: 請為這場會議撰寫一份報告主要發現的簡短摘要。

w4365.en: The royal banquet was a **sumptuous** feast of rare dishes and fine wines.
w4365.zh: 那場皇家宴會是一頓由珍稀佳餚與美酒構成的奢華盛宴。

w4366.en: The market offered a **superabundance** of fresh fruit at remarkably low prices.
w4366.zh: 市場上以極低的價格供應著超級豐富的新鮮水果。

w4367.en: To the basic recipe, the chef chose to **superadd** a hint of chilli.
w4367.zh: 在基本食譜的基礎上，主廚選擇額外添加一絲辣椒味。

w4368.en: The company decided to **superannuate** its oldest and least efficient machinery.
w4368.zh: 公司決定淘汰其最老舊、效率最差的機器。

w4369.en: The orchestra gave a **superb** performance that left the audience deeply moved.
w4369.zh: 管弦樂團的演出極為出色，使觀眾深受感動。

w4370.en: The **supercilious** waiter barely glanced at the customers he clearly disliked.
w4370.zh: 那名高傲的服務生幾乎不正眼看他顯然不喜歡的顧客。

w4371.en: His knowledge of the subject was **superficial**, lacking any real depth or detail.
w4371.zh: 他對這個主題的了解流於表面，缺乏任何真正的深度或細節。

w4372.en: The minimalist designer removed every **superfluity** from the small apartment's interior.
w4372.zh: 那位極簡主義設計師移除了小公寓內部的每一件多餘之物。

w4373.en: The editor deleted several **superfluous** words to make the sentence clearer.
w4373.zh: 編輯刪除了幾個多餘的字，讓句子更清楚。

w4374.en: Engineers **superheat** the steam to drive the powerful turbines more efficiently.
w4374.zh: 工程師使蒸汽過熱，以更有效率地驅動強大的渦輪機。

w4375.en: An experienced foreman was hired to **superintend** the entire construction project.
w4375.zh: 公司聘請了一位經驗豐富的工頭來監督整個建築工程。

w4376.en: Under her careful **superintendence**, the renovation finished ahead of schedule.
w4376.zh: 在她細心的監督下，這項翻修工程提前完工。

w4377.en: The school **superintendent** visited each classroom to observe the new teaching methods.
w4377.zh: 學校的督學走訪每間教室，觀察新的教學方法。

w4378.en: Among all the candidates, she was the **superlative** choice for the leadership role.
w4378.zh: 在所有候選人中，她是領導職位最出色的人選。

w4379.en: The villagers believed a **supernatural** force protected their ancient sacred forest.
w4379.zh: 村民相信有一股超自然的力量守護著他們古老的聖林。

w4380.en: Several **supernumerary** actors were hired to fill the busy crowd scenes.
w4380.zh: 劇組僱用了幾名臨時小配角來填補熱鬧的群眾場面。

w4381.en: Smartphones have largely **superseded** older devices like digital cameras and music players.
w4381.zh: 智慧型手機已大致取代了數位相機和音樂播放器等舊裝置。

w4382.en: The patient lay in a **supine** position while the nurse prepared the scan.
w4382.zh: 病人仰臥著，護士則準備進行掃描。

w4383.en: A younger, cheaper rival soon began to **supplant** the established market leader.
w4383.zh: 一個更年輕、更便宜的競爭對手很快開始取代既有的市場領導者。

w4384.en: Years of yoga kept her body remarkably **supple** well into old age.
w4384.zh: 多年的瑜伽使她的身體直到老年仍保持極為柔軟。

w4385.en: Students may earn **supplementary** marks by completing the optional research project.
w4385.zh: 學生可透過完成選修的研究專題來獲得補充的分數。

w4386.en: The desperate **supplicant** knelt before the king and begged for mercy.
w4386.zh: 那名絕望的懇求者跪在國王面前乞求寬恕。

w4387.en: The villagers gathered at the temple to **supplicate** the gods for rain.
w4387.zh: 村民聚集在廟裡懇求眾神降雨。

w4388.en: The detective's theory rested on the **supposition** that the witness had lied.
w4388.zh: 偵探的理論建立在證人說謊這個假定之上。

w4389.en: The regime tried to **suppress** all newspapers that criticised its harsh policies.
w4389.zh: 該政權試圖查禁所有批評其嚴苛政策的報紙。

w4390.en: Engineers confirmed that the unexpected vibration was easily **suppressible** with better insulation.
w4390.zh: 工程師確認，那意外的震動透過更好的隔離很容易就能遏制。

w4391.en: The violent **suppression** of the peaceful protest drew international condemnation.
w4391.zh: 對和平抗議的暴力鎮壓引來了國際譴責。

w4392.en: The monk devoted his life to **supramundane** matters beyond ordinary worldly concerns.
w4392.zh: 那名僧侶將一生奉獻給超越尋常塵世憂慮的遠離塵世之事。

w4393.en: The airline added a fuel **surcharge** to every long-haul ticket this summer.
w4393.zh: 今年夏天該航空公司在每張長程機票上加收了燃油附加費。

w4394.en: Her wealthy uncle agreed to act as **surety** for the young man's loan.
w4394.zh: 她富有的叔叔同意擔任那名年輕人貸款的擔保人。

w4395.en: The endless rich desserts began to **surfeit** even the hungriest guests.
w4395.zh: 那源源不絕的豐盛甜點開始連最飢餓的客人也吃得發膩。

w4396.en: From the muddy footprints, the detective began to **surmise** how the thief escaped.
w4396.zh: 從泥濘的腳印，偵探開始推測竊賊是如何逃走的。

w4397.en: With determination and support, she managed to **surmount** every obstacle in her path.
w4397.zh: 憑藉決心與支持，她設法克服了路途中的每一個障礙。

w4398.en: He took a **surreptitious** glance at his phone during the long meeting.
w4398.zh: 在漫長的會議中，他偷偷瞄了一眼自己的手機。

w4399.en: For many lonely commuters, a pet became a comforting **surrogate** for companionship.
w4399.zh: 對許多孤獨的通勤者而言，寵物成了陪伴的一種慰藉替代品。

w4400.en: Tall hedges **surround** the quiet garden, shielding it from the busy street.
w4400.zh: 高大的樹籬環繞著這座寧靜的花園，將它與繁忙的街道隔開。

w4401.en: The land **surveyor** carefully measured the boundaries before construction could begin.
w4401.zh: 土地測量員在動工前仔細測量了邊界。

w4402.en: Her **susceptibility** to flattery often led her to trust the wrong people.
w4402.zh: 她易受奉承影響的特質常使她信任了錯的人。

w4403.en: Young children are especially **susceptible** to colds during the cold winter months.
w4403.zh: 幼兒在寒冷的冬季月份特別容易感冒。

w4404.en: The film kept the audience in **suspense** until its final shocking scene.
w4404.zh: 那部電影讓觀眾保持懸疑緊張，直到最後令人震驚的一幕。

w4405.en: The referee ordered the immediate **suspension** of the match because of the storm.
w4405.zh: 裁判因暴風雨下令立即中止比賽。

w4406.en: The guard grew **suspicious** of the stranger lingering near the locked gate.
w4406.zh: 警衛對那名在上鎖的大門附近徘徊的陌生人起了疑心。

w4407.en: The stranded hikers had barely enough **sustenance** to last another two days.
w4407.zh: 受困的健行者僅有勉強夠再撐兩天的食物。

w4408.en: Years of working outdoors had given the fisherman a deeply **swarthy** complexion.
w4408.zh: 多年的戶外工作使那名漁夫有了黝黑的膚色。

w4409.en: The wealthy **Sybarite** spent his fortune on lavish parties and exotic luxuries.
w4409.zh: 那名愛奢侈享樂的富人把財產花在奢華的派對和異國的奢侈品上。

w4410.en: The king was surrounded by flattering **sycophants** who praised his every decision.
w4410.zh: 國王身邊圍繞著對他每個決定都讚不絕口的阿諛奉承的馬屁精。

w4411.en: Linguists noted the **syllabic** stress patterns that distinguish the two dialects.
w4411.zh: 語言學家注意到區分這兩種方言的音節重音模式。

w4412.en: The dictionary uses dots to show the **syllabication** of each difficult word.
w4412.zh: 這本字典用圓點來標示每個難詞的音節區分。

w4413.en: The teacher asked the children to clap once for each **syllable** they heard.
w4413.zh: 老師要孩子們每聽到一個音節就拍一次手。

w4414.en: Students received the course **syllabus** outlining every topic and assignment deadline.
w4414.zh: 學生收到了課程大綱，概述了每個主題和作業截止日期。

w4415.en: The graceful dancer moved across the stage like a delicate **sylph**.
w4415.zh: 那位優雅的舞者像氣精般輕盈地掠過舞台。

w4416.en: The architect designed a perfectly **symmetrical** facade with matching windows on both sides.
w4416.zh: 建築師設計了一個完全對稱的正立面，兩側有相互對應的窗戶。

w4417.en: The butterfly's wings displayed a striking **symmetry** of colour and pattern.
w4417.zh: 那隻蝴蝶的翅膀展現出色彩與圖案引人注目的對稱。

w4418.en: The nurse was warm and **sympathetic** towards every anxious patient she met.
w4418.zh: 那名護士對她遇到的每位焦慮的病人都溫暖且富有同情心。

w4419.en: Many readers wrote letters to **sympathize** with the family after their tragic loss.
w4419.zh: 許多讀者寫信來對這個家庭的悲慘失去表達同情。

w4420.en: The composer transformed the simple folk tune into a grand **symphonic** work.
w4420.zh: 作曲家將那首簡單的民謠改編成一部宏大的交響樂作品。

w4421.en: The choir's voices blended into one **symphonious** and deeply moving harmony.
w4421.zh: 合唱團的歌聲融合成一種諧音且深深動人的和聲。

w4422.en: The orchestra performed Beethoven's famous ninth **symphony** to a packed concert hall.
w4422.zh: 管弦樂團在座無虛席的音樂廳演奏了貝多芬著名的第九號交響曲。

w4423.en: Historians study the **synchronism** of revolutions that erupted across Europe that year.
w4423.zh: 歷史學家研究那一年席捲歐洲各地的革命同時發生的現象。

w4424.en: A powerful publishing **syndicate** controlled most of the country's daily newspapers.
w4424.zh: 一個強大的出版財團控制了該國大部分的日報。

w4425.en: The chemist observed **syneresis** as the gel slowly released liquid from its surface.
w4425.zh: 化學家觀察到脫水收縮，凝膠緩慢地從表面釋出液體。

w4426.en: The bishops gathered for a **synod** to debate important matters of doctrine.
w4426.zh: 主教們聚集召開宗教會議，討論重要的教義事項。

w4427.en: The word "happy" is a common **synonym** for the adjective "glad".
w4427.zh: happy 這個字是形容詞 glad 常見的同義字。

w4428.en: The publisher requested a one-page **synopsis** of the novel's main plot.
w4428.zh: 出版商要求提供一頁概述小說主要情節的概要。

w4429.en: The researcher took a **systematic** approach, testing each variable one at a time.
w4429.zh: 研究人員採取有系統的方法，一次測試一個變項。

w4430.en: The festival opened with a colourful **tableau** depicting the village's ancient legend.
w4430.zh: 慶典以一幅描繪村莊古老傳說、色彩繽紛的動人場面揭開序幕。

w4431.en: There was a **tacit** agreement among the staff never to discuss salaries openly.
w4431.zh: 員工之間有一項默示的協議，絕不公開討論薪水。

w4432.en: The **taciturn** farmer rarely spoke, answering most questions with a single nod.
w4432.zh: 那名沉默寡言的農夫鮮少開口，大多數問題都只以點頭回應。

w4433.en: She used a small **tack** to pin the notice firmly to the board.
w4433.zh: 她用一枚小圖釘把通知牢牢地固定在布告欄上。

w4434.en: With great **tact**, the manager delivered the bad news without upsetting anyone.
w4434.zh: 經理以高度的圓融機智傳達了壞消息，卻沒有讓任何人不快。

w4435.en: A skilled **tactician**, the coach adjusted his plan brilliantly during halftime.
w4435.zh: 身為一名高明的戰術家，教練在中場休息時出色地調整了他的策略。

w4436.en: The general changed his **tactics** when the enemy advanced unexpectedly from the north.
w4436.zh: 當敵人意外地從北方推進時，將軍改變了他的戰術。

w4437.en: The geometry lesson explained the point of **tangency** between the circle and line.
w4437.zh: 那堂幾何課解釋了圓與直線之間的相切點。

w4438.en: The speaker went off on a **tangent**, drifting away from his main topic.
w4438.zh: 講者離了題，漸漸偏離了他的主要主題。

w4439.en: The new policy produced **tangible** benefits, including shorter waiting times for patients.
w4439.zh: 新政策帶來了實際的好處，包括病人等候時間縮短。

w4440.en: The smell from the old **tannery** drifted across the entire riverside town.
w4440.zh: 那座老製革廠的氣味飄散到整個河邊小鎮。

w4441.en: The chef would **tantalize** guests by describing each dish before it arrived.
w4441.zh: 主廚會在每道菜上桌前描述它，藉此吊客人的胃口。

w4442.en: Ignoring the safety warning was **tantamount** to risking everyone's life on board.
w4442.zh: 無視安全警告等同於拿船上每個人的性命冒險。

w4443.en: An ancient **tapestry** depicting a royal hunt hung in the castle's great hall.
w4443.zh: 一幅描繪皇室狩獵的古老掛毯懸掛在城堡的大廳裡。

w4444.en: The scandal threatened to **tarnish** the politician's once spotless reputation.
w4444.zh: 那樁醜聞可能玷汙這名政治人物曾經毫無瑕疵的名譽。

w4445.en: The climbers kept the rope **taut** to prevent any dangerous slack.
w4445.zh: 登山者把繩子保持繃緊，以防出現任何危險的鬆弛。

w4446.en: The government raised **taxation** on tobacco to discourage people from smoking.
w4446.zh: 政府提高了菸草的課稅，以勸阻人們吸菸。

w4447.en: The museum hired an expert in **taxidermy** to preserve the rare bird specimens.
w4447.zh: 博物館聘請了一位剝製術專家來保存那些稀有的鳥類標本。

w4448.en: The pianist demonstrated flawless **technic** while performing the demanding concerto.
w4448.zh: 那位鋼琴家在演奏這首高難度協奏曲時展現出無懈可擊的技巧。

w4449.en: The contract was rejected on a minor **technicality** that everyone had overlooked.
w4449.zh: 那份合約因一個眾人都忽略的小技術細節而遭駁回。

w4450.en: The artist developed a unique painting **technique** using layers of thin glaze.
w4450.zh: 那位藝術家發展出一種使用多層薄釉的獨特繪畫技法。

w4451.en: The engineer studied **technography** to document how the factory's machines were operated.
w4451.zh: 工程師研習技術工藝學，以記錄工廠機器的操作方式。

w4452.en: Modern medical **technology** has dramatically improved survival rates for many serious diseases.
w4452.zh: 現代醫療技術已大幅提升了許多重症的存活率。

w4453.en: Rain began to **teem** down, soaking the crowd within just a few minutes.
w4453.zh: 雨開始傾盆而下，短短幾分鐘內就把人群淋濕了。

w4454.en: The twins claimed to communicate through **telepathy**, sensing each other's thoughts instantly.
w4454.zh: 那對雙胞胎聲稱透過心靈感應溝通，能瞬間感知彼此的想法。

w4455.en: Advances in **telephony** allowed people to make clear calls across entire continents.
w4455.zh: 電話通訊技術的進步使人們能跨越整個大陸進行清晰的通話。

w4456.en: Authors sometimes **telescope** several years of events into a single short chapter.
w4456.zh: 作者有時會把好幾年的事件壓縮進單獨一個短章節裡。

w4457.en: The muddy boots were a **telltale** sign that someone had entered through the garden.
w4457.zh: 那雙泥濘的靴子是有人從花園進入、洩露真相的跡象。

w4458.en: He had the **temerity** to question the professor's findings in front of everyone.
w4458.zh: 他竟有膽量當著眾人的面質疑教授的研究結果。

w4459.en: Philosophers debated whether human achievements have any lasting or merely **temporal** value.
w4459.zh: 哲學家辯論人類的成就究竟具有持久的價值，還是僅是短暫的價值。

w4460.en: The company hired several **temporary** workers to handle the busy holiday season.
w4460.zh: 公司僱用了幾名臨時工來應付繁忙的假日季節。

w4461.en: Rather than decide at once, the cautious minister chose to **temporize** for weeks.
w4461.zh: 那位謹慎的部長沒有立刻決定，而是選擇拖延了好幾週。

w4462.en: The bright window displays were designed to **tempt** shoppers inside the store.
w4462.zh: 那些明亮的櫥窗陳列是設計來引誘購物者進入店裡的。

w4463.en: In the story, a cunning **tempter** lured the hero away from his sacred duty.
w4463.zh: 在故事中，一個狡猾的引誘者誘使英雄背離了他神聖的職責。

w4464.en: Her **tenacious** efforts finally persuaded the committee to fund her research.
w4464.zh: 她頑強不懈的努力終於說服了委員會資助她的研究。

w4465.en: The new **tenant** signed a one-year lease for the small downtown apartment.
w4465.zh: 新房客為那間市中心的小公寓簽了一年的租約。

w4466.en: Modern teenagers show a strong **tendency** to share every moment on social media.
w4466.zh: 現代青少年表現出強烈的傾向，要在社群媒體上分享每個時刻。

w4467.en: Honesty remained the central **tenet** of the organisation's founding philosophy.
w4467.zh: 誠實始終是該組織創立理念的核心信條。

w4468.en: The general **tenor** of the meeting was cautious but ultimately hopeful.
w4468.zh: 那場會議的整體基調是謹慎，但最終仍懷抱希望。

w4469.en: Her shoulders felt **tense** after a long day of stressful negotiations.
w4469.zh: 在漫長一天充滿壓力的談判後，她的肩膀感到緊繃。

w4470.en: The two sides reached a **tentative** agreement that still required formal approval.
w4470.zh: 雙方達成了一項仍需正式批准的試驗性協議。

w4471.en: The professor was granted permanent **tenure** after a decade of distinguished teaching.
w4471.zh: 那位教授在十年傑出的教學之後獲得了終身職。

w4472.en: The city held a grand **tercentenary** celebration marking three hundred years since its founding.
w4472.zh: 該市舉辦了盛大的三百週年慶典，紀念建城三百年。

w4473.en: The **termagant** landlady scolded her tenants loudly over the smallest complaints.
w4473.zh: 那名兇悍的女房東為了最小的抱怨就大聲斥責她的房客。

w4474.en: The doctor gently explained that the illness was, sadly, at a **terminal** stage.
w4474.zh: 醫生溫和地說明，遺憾的是，這個疾病已到了末期階段。

w4475.en: The company decided to **terminate** the contract after repeated delays in delivery.
w4475.zh: 在多次延誤交貨之後，公司決定終止合約。

w4476.en: The early **termination** of the lease left the tenants searching for new housing.
w4476.zh: 租約的提前終止使房客必須尋找新的住處。

w4477.en: The tired travellers finally reached the bus **terminus** at the edge of town.
w4477.zh: 疲憊的旅客終於抵達了位於城鎮邊緣的巴士總站。

w4478.en: Loud thunder and lightning can **terrify** young children during a violent storm.
w4478.zh: 猛烈的暴風雨中，響亮的雷聲和閃電會使幼兒感到害怕。

w4479.en: The two nations argued for years over a small but **territorial** dispute.
w4479.zh: 這兩個國家為一場規模雖小卻關乎領土的爭端爭論了多年。

w4480.en: The editor preferred a **terse** report over a long, rambling account.
w4480.zh: 編輯偏好一份精練的報告，而非冗長、雜亂無章的敘述。

w4481.en: In his **testament**, the old man left his entire estate to charity.
w4481.zh: 在他的遺囑中，那位老人把全部財產都留給了慈善機構。

w4482.en: The lawyer read the **testator's** wishes aloud to the assembled family members.
w4482.zh: 律師向聚集的家族成員大聲宣讀立遺囑者的遺願。

w4483.en: Several satisfied customers wrote a glowing **testimonial** praising the company's excellent service.
w4483.zh: 幾位滿意的顧客寫了一份讚不絕口的推薦書，稱讚公司優異的服務。

w4484.en: The ancient kingdom was ruled as a **thearchy**, with priests claiming divine authority.
w4484.zh: 那個古老王國以神權統治治理，祭司宣稱擁有神授的權威。

w4485.en: The philosophy lecture compared **theism** with various atheistic views of the universe.
w4485.zh: 那堂哲學課比較了有神論與各種對宇宙的無神論觀點。

w4486.en: In that ancient **theocracy**, religious leaders held complete control over the government.
w4486.zh: 在那個古老的神權國家，宗教領袖對政府握有完全的控制權。

w4487.en: The scholar studied an unusual **theocrasy** that blended several rival gods into one cult.
w4487.zh: 那位學者研究一種不尋常的諸神混合崇拜，將數個敵對的神融合成單一信仰。

w4488.en: The famous **theologian** spent decades writing about the nature of divine grace.
w4488.zh: 那位著名的神學家花了數十年撰寫關於神恩本質的著作。

w4489.en: The university offers a respected degree in **theological** studies and religious history.
w4489.zh: 那所大學提供一個備受尊敬的神學研究與宗教史學位。

w4490.en: She studied medieval **theology** to understand how religious ideas shaped daily life.
w4490.zh: 她研究中世紀神學，以理解宗教思想如何形塑日常生活。

w4491.en: The model works perfectly in **theoretical** terms but fails under real conditions.
w4491.zh: 這個模型在理論上運作完美，但在真實條件下卻失敗了。

w4492.en: A leading economic **theorist**, she proposed a bold new model of inflation.
w4492.zh: 身為一位頂尖的經濟理論家，她提出了一個大膽的通膨新模型。

w4493.en: Scientists continued to **theorize** about what might lie beyond the observable universe.
w4493.zh: 科學家持續對可觀測宇宙之外可能存在的事物建立理論。

w4494.en: The cottage stood by the river, or **thereabout**, hidden among tall trees.
w4494.zh: 那間小屋就坐落在河邊，或在那附近，隱身於高大的樹木之間。

w4495.en: The funds were misused, and the manager was held responsible **therefor**.
w4495.zh: 那筆款項遭到濫用，經理為此被追究責任。

w4496.en: The hikers wore **thermal** clothing to stay warm on the freezing mountain.
w4496.zh: 健行者穿著保暖的衣物，在冰寒的山上保持溫暖。

w4497.en: The device generates electricity using a **thermoelectric** effect from temperature differences.
w4497.zh: 這個裝置利用溫差產生的溫差電效應來發電。

w4498.en: Engineers harness **thermoelectricity** to convert wasted engine heat into useful power.
w4498.zh: 工程師利用熱電將引擎浪費的熱能轉換成有用的電力。

w4499.en: She spent two years researching and writing her doctoral **thesis** on climate policy.
w4499.zh: 她花了兩年研究並撰寫她關於氣候政策的博士論文。

w4500.en: The breeder sold a magnificent **thoroughbred** that had won several major races.
w4500.zh: 那名育馬者賣出了一匹曾贏得多場重大賽事的出色純種馬。

w4501.en: The hotel sits on a busy **thoroughfare** lined with shops and cafes.
w4501.zh: 這家旅館坐落在一條兩旁滿是商店和咖啡館的繁忙大道上。

w4502.en: For years he was held in **thrall** to ambition and could not stop working.
w4502.zh: 多年來他一直沉湎於野心而無法自拔，停不下工作。

w4503.en: Years of careful ploughing left the soil in excellent **tilth** for planting.
w4503.zh: 多年細心的犁耕使土壤達到了極佳的耕作狀態，適合種植。

w4504.en: The cello's warm **timbre** gave the piece a deeply moving quality.
w4504.zh: 大提琴溫暖的音色賦予這首樂曲深深動人的特質。

w4505.en: The **timorous** student rarely raised her hand, fearing she might be wrong.
w4505.zh: 那個膽怯的學生很少舉手，生怕自己會答錯。

w4506.en: The artist **tinctured** the white paint with a faint trace of blue.
w4506.zh: 這位畫家在白色顏料中染上了一絲淡淡的藍色。

w4507.en: Her voice carried a **tinge** of regret as she said goodbye.
w4507.zh: 她道別時，聲音中帶著一絲悔意。

w4508.en: After two glasses of wine he felt pleasantly **tipsy** and very talkative.
w4508.zh: 喝了兩杯酒後，他覺得有點微醺，而且非常健談。

w4509.en: The coach launched into a furious **tirade** about the team's poor effort.
w4509.zh: 教練開始對球隊不努力的表現發表了一通激烈的長篇抨擊。

w4510.en: The **tireless** volunteers worked through the night to help flood victims.
w4510.zh: 那些孜孜不倦的志工徹夜工作，幫助水災受災者。

w4511.en: The long, repetitive lecture grew **tiresome** before the first hour ended.
w4511.zh: 那場冗長又重複的講座還沒到一小時就變得令人厭倦。

w4512.en: Building the bridge was a **titanic** task requiring thousands of skilled workers.
w4512.zh: 建造這座橋是一項極其浩大的工程，需要數千名熟練工人。

w4513.en: Clearing the rocky field by hand was slow and **toilsome** labour.
w4513.zh: 用手清理這片多石的田地是緩慢而勞苦的工作。

w4514.en: The noise from the street was annoying but just about **tolerable**.
w4514.zh: 街上的噪音很惱人，但勉強還算可以忍受。

w4515.en: A good teacher shows great **tolerance** for students who learn slowly.
w4515.zh: 好老師對學得慢的學生會展現極大的寬容。

w4516.en: My grandmother is remarkably **tolerant** of noisy children at family gatherings.
w4516.zh: 我祖母對家庭聚會上吵鬧的孩子出奇地寬容。

w4517.en: I simply cannot **tolerate** rudeness from anyone, no matter their position.
w4517.zh: 無論對方地位如何，我就是無法容忍任何人的無禮。

w4518.en: Religious **toleration** allowed people of different faiths to live peacefully together.
w4518.zh: 宗教寬容讓不同信仰的人能夠和平共處。

w4519.en: The hikers studied the **topography** carefully before choosing their mountain route.
w4519.zh: 健行者在選擇登山路線前，仔細研究了當地的地形。

w4520.en: A heavy meal left him in a state of pleasant **torpor**.
w4520.zh: 一頓豐盛的大餐讓他陷入一種愉快的遲鈍懶散狀態。

w4521.en: The desert's **torrid** heat made walking at noon almost unbearable.
w4521.zh: 沙漠灼熱的高溫使得正午行走幾乎令人難以忍受。

w4522.en: The company faced **tortious** liability for the harm its product caused.
w4522.zh: 該公司因其產品造成的傷害而面臨民事侵權責任。

w4523.en: The **tortuous** mountain road twisted sharply around every steep cliff.
w4523.zh: 那條迂迴曲折的山路在每處陡峭的懸崖邊急轉。

w4524.en: The long wait for the test results was a **torturous** experience.
w4524.zh: 漫長地等待檢測結果是一段折磨人的經歷。

w4525.en: The young pony proved gentle and **tractable**, easy for beginners to ride.
w4525.zh: 那匹小馬被證明溫順又易於駕馭，適合初學者騎乘。

w4526.en: Patience is a valuable **trait** in anyone who teaches small children.
w4526.zh: 耐心對任何教導幼童的人來說都是一項可貴的特質。

w4527.en: Scientists calculated the **trajectory** of the rocket before it was launched.
w4527.zh: 科學家在火箭發射前計算了它的軌道。

w4528.en: She longed to escape the **trammels** of her dull office routine.
w4528.zh: 她渴望擺脫枯燥辦公室常規的束縛。

w4529.en: We spent a **tranquil** afternoon reading beside the calm mountain lake.
w4529.zh: 我們在寧靜的山中湖畔度過了一個平靜的下午閱讀。

w4530.en: The vet had to **tranquilize** the frightened horse before examining it.
w4530.zh: 獸醫必須先讓那匹受驚的馬鎮定下來，才能為牠檢查。

w4531.en: The garden offered a sense of **tranquility** far from the city's noise.
w4531.zh: 這座花園帶來一種遠離城市喧囂的寧靜感。

w4532.en: The **transalpine** regions lie just beyond the northern slopes of the Alps.
w4532.zh: 那些阿爾卑斯山另一側的地區就位於阿爾卑斯山北坡之外。

w4533.en: The two firms met privately to **transact** their important business deal.
w4533.zh: 兩家公司私下會面以辦理他們重要的商業交易。

w4534.en: The **transatlantic** flight from London to New York took about seven hours.
w4534.zh: 從倫敦到紐約的橫渡大西洋航班大約花了七個小時。

w4535.en: Her talent seemed to **transcend** anything her teachers had seen before.
w4535.zh: 她的才華似乎超越了她老師們以往見過的一切。

w4536.en: The pianist gave a **transcendent** performance that left the audience speechless.
w4536.zh: 這位鋼琴家獻上了一場卓越超凡的演出，讓觀眾啞口無言。

w4537.en: They planned a **transcontinental** journey by train across the whole country.
w4537.zh: 他們計劃乘火車進行一趟橫貫全國的大陸旅程。

w4538.en: It took hours to **transcribe** the entire recorded interview into clear text.
w4538.zh: 把整段錄製的訪談謄寫成清楚的文字花了好幾個小時。

w4539.en: The student requested an official **transcript** of all her university grades.
w4539.zh: 這名學生申請了一份她所有大學成績的正式成績單。

w4540.en: The company will **transfer** him to its branch office in Singapore.
w4540.zh: 公司將把他調到位於新加坡的分公司。

w4541.en: The skills you learn here are easily **transferable** to other careers.
w4541.zh: 你在這裡學到的技能很容易轉移到其他職業上。

w4542.en: As the **transferee**, she signed the documents accepting ownership of the property.
w4542.zh: 作為受讓人，她簽署了文件，接受該房產的所有權。

w4543.en: The **transference** of knowledge from teacher to student takes patience and time.
w4543.zh: 知識從老師到學生的傳遞需要耐心與時間。

w4544.en: As the **transferrer**, he was responsible for completing all the legal paperwork.
w4544.zh: 作為讓渡人，他負責完成所有的法律文件。

w4545.en: A fresh coat of paint can completely **transfigure** an old, tired room.
w4545.zh: 一層新漆能徹底改變一個老舊破敗的房間。

w4546.en: Doctors decided to **transfuse** blood into the patient after the accident.
w4546.zh: 醫生決定在事故後為病人輸血。

w4547.en: The donated plasma was carefully tested to confirm it was safely **transfusible**.
w4547.zh: 捐贈的血漿經過仔細檢驗，以確認它可以安全輸用。

w4548.en: The patient needed an urgent blood **transfusion** after losing so much blood.
w4548.zh: 病人在失血過多後需要緊急輸血。

w4549.en: Anyone who **transgresses** the safety rules will be removed from the site.
w4549.zh: 任何違反安全規則的人都將被逐出工地。

w4550.en: The **transience** of youth makes us value each passing year more.
w4550.zh: 青春的短暫使我們更加珍惜逝去的每一年。

w4551.en: The hostel mainly serves **transient** workers who stay only a few nights.
w4551.zh: 這家旅舍主要服務只住幾晚的過往工人。

w4552.en: The country's **transition** from dictatorship to democracy was slow but peaceful.
w4552.zh: 這個國家從獨裁到民主的過渡緩慢卻和平。

w4553.en: Fame can be **transitory**, fading almost as quickly as it arrives.
