w4553.zh: 名聲可能是短暫的，幾乎來得多快就消失得多快。

w4554.en: She was hired to **translate** the report from German into English.
w4554.zh: 她受僱將這份報告從德文翻譯成英文。

w4555.en: A skilled **translator** must understand both cultures, not just the two languages.
w4555.zh: 一位優秀的譯者必須了解兩種文化，而不只是兩種語言。

w4556.en: The **translucence** of the curtains let soft daylight fill the room.
w4556.zh: 窗簾的半透明特性讓柔和的日光灑滿整個房間。

w4557.en: The lampshade was made of thin, **translucent** paper that glowed softly.
w4557.zh: 燈罩是用薄薄的半透明紙做成的，散發著柔和的光。

w4558.en: The disease is highly **transmissible** through contaminated water and unwashed hands.
w4558.zh: 這種疾病透過受污染的水和未清洗的雙手極易傳播。

w4559.en: Vaccines greatly reduce the **transmission** of many dangerous infectious diseases.
w4559.zh: 疫苗大幅減少了許多危險傳染病的傳播。

w4560.en: The station can **transmit** signals to ships hundreds of miles away.
w4560.zh: 這座電台能將訊號發送給數百英里外的船隻。

w4561.en: Medieval alchemists hoped to **transmute** ordinary metals into pure gold.
w4561.zh: 中世紀的煉金術士希望把普通金屬變質成純金。

w4562.en: The lake water was so **transparent** that we could see the bottom.
w4562.zh: 湖水如此清澈透明，我們能看見湖底。

w4563.en: It later **transpired** that the manager had known about the problem all along.
w4563.zh: 後來人們才得知，那位經理一直都知道這個問題。

w4564.en: Surgeons can now **transplant** a healthy kidney from one person to another.
w4564.zh: 外科醫生現在能將健康的腎臟從一個人移植到另一個人身上。

w4565.en: A simple **transposition** of two letters changed the whole meaning of the word.
w4565.zh: 兩個字母的簡單調換改變了整個單字的意思。

w4566.en: A **transverse** beam runs across the ceiling to support the roof.
w4566.zh: 一根橫向的橫梁橫跨天花板以支撐屋頂。

w4567.en: After months of **travail**, the team finally completed the difficult project.
w4567.zh: 經過數月的艱苦勞動，團隊終於完成了這個困難的專案。

w4568.en: The film was a clumsy **travesty** of the much-loved original novel.
w4568.zh: 這部電影是對那本廣受喜愛的原著小說拙劣的滑稽模仿。

w4569.en: The melting ice made the path dangerously slippery and quite **treacherous**.
w4569.zh: 融化的冰使這條小徑危險地濕滑，相當凶險難測。

w4570.en: The general was executed for his **treachery** against the king.
w4570.zh: 這位將軍因背叛國王的變節行為而被處決。

w4571.en: Plotting against the government was considered a **treasonable** act punishable by death.
w4571.zh: 密謀反對政府被視為一種可處死刑的叛逆行為。

w4572.en: She wrote a long **treatise** on the economics of developing countries.
w4572.zh: 她撰寫了一篇關於開發中國家經濟學的長篇論文。

w4573.en: The company hopes to **treble** its profits within the next three years.
w4573.zh: 公司希望在未來三年內使利潤增為三倍。

w4574.en: The new factory made the firm's output rise **trebly** within one year.
w4574.zh: 新工廠使公司的產量在一年內增為三倍。

w4575.en: The new bridge was a **tremendous** achievement for the small engineering team.
w4575.zh: 這座新橋對這個小型工程團隊來說是一項巨大的成就。

w4576.en: A slight **tremor** in his hands showed how nervous he really was.
w4576.zh: 他雙手輕微的顫抖顯示出他內心有多緊張。

w4577.en: Her **tremulous** voice betrayed the fear she was trying hard to hide.
w4577.zh: 她顫抖的聲音洩露了她極力想隱藏的恐懼。

w4578.en: The reviewer made a **trenchant** criticism that exposed the book's main weakness.
w4578.zh: 這位評論者提出了一針見血的批評，揭露了該書的主要弱點。

w4579.en: She opened the exam envelope with considerable **trepidation** and trembling hands.
w4579.zh: 她懷著相當的不安和顫抖的雙手打開了考試信封。

w4580.en: The old railway crossed the valley on a tall wooden **trestle**.
w4580.zh: 那條舊鐵路藉由一座高大的木製棧橋跨越山谷。

w4581.en: The three songs form a **triad** united by a single shared theme.
w4581.zh: 這三首歌曲組成一個三件一套的組合，由單一共同主題串連。

w4582.en: In ancient Rome, a **tribune** was elected to protect ordinary citizens' rights.
w4582.zh: 在古羅馬，護民官是被選出來保護平民權利的。

w4583.en: The magician amazed the crowd with clever sleight of hand and **trickery**.
w4583.zh: 魔術師用巧妙的手法和騙術讓觀眾驚嘆不已。

w4584.en: The team marched proudly behind their bright **tricolor** flag.
w4584.zh: 球隊自豪地走在他們鮮豔的三色旗後面。

w4585.en: The toddler pedalled his little red **tricycle** around the quiet garden.
w4585.zh: 那個幼兒踩著他的小紅三輪車繞著安靜的花園。

w4586.en: In the painting, the sea god raised his three-pronged **trident** high.
w4586.zh: 在那幅畫中，海神高高舉起他的三叉戟。

w4587.en: The committee holds a **triennial** meeting once every three years.
w4587.zh: 委員會每三年舉行一次三年一度的會議。

w4588.en: The garden was admired for the **trimness** of its neatly clipped hedges.
w4588.zh: 這座花園因其修剪整齊的樹籬之整潔而備受讚賞。

w4589.en: The exhibition presented a **trinity** of paintings sharing one historical theme.
w4589.zh: 這場展覽呈現了三幅一組、共享同一歷史主題的畫作。

w4590.en: The jazz **trio** played softly in the corner of the small restaurant.
w4590.zh: 這個爵士三重奏在小餐廳的角落輕柔地演奏。

w4591.en: The recipe requires a **triple** layer of sponge cake and cream.
w4591.zh: 這道食譜需要三層的海綿蛋糕和奶油。

w4592.en: Please prepare the contract in **triplicate** so each party keeps a copy.
w4592.zh: 請將合約準備成一式三份，好讓每一方各保留一份。

w4593.en: The design's beauty lies in the **triplicity** of its three balanced parts.
w4593.zh: 這設計之美在於其三個平衡部分的三位一體。

w4594.en: The photographer steadied her camera on a sturdy three-legged **tripod**.
w4594.zh: 攝影師把相機穩穩地架在一個堅固的三腳架上。

w4595.en: Using only a compass, can you **trisect** this angle into three equal parts?
w4595.zh: 只用圓規，你能把這個角三等分成三個相等的部分嗎？

w4596.en: The speech was full of **trite** phrases that no longer impressed anyone.
w4596.zh: 這場演講充滿了陳腐的詞句，再也打動不了任何人。

w4597.en: Each **triumvir** shared power equally in governing the vast ancient empire.
w4597.zh: 每位三執政之一在統治這個龐大的古代帝國時平等地分享權力。

w4598.en: Please don't waste my time with such **trivial** and unimportant complaints.
w4598.zh: 請別用這種瑣碎又不重要的抱怨浪費我的時間。

w4599.en: The leaking pipe became a **troublesome** problem that no one could fix.
w4599.zh: 漏水的管子成了一個沒人能修好的棘手問題。

w4600.en: The dictator ruled with a **truculence** that frightened even his closest allies.
w4600.zh: 這名獨裁者以一種連最親近的盟友都感到害怕的殘暴進行統治。

w4601.en: The **truculent** customer shouted at the staff over a tiny mistake.
w4601.zh: 那名兇狠的顧客為了一個小錯誤對員工大吼大叫。

w4602.en: Saying that hard work brings success is something of a **truism**.
w4602.zh: 說努力工作會帶來成功，算是一種老生常談。

w4603.en: A **truthful** witness gave the court a clear and honest account.
w4603.zh: 一位誠實的證人向法庭提供了清楚而坦白的陳述。

w4604.en: The report was full of **turgid** language that obscured its simple message.
w4604.zh: 這份報告充滿了浮誇的語言，掩蓋了它簡單的訊息。

w4605.en: The judge condemned the crime as an act of shocking moral **turpitude**.
w4605.zh: 法官譴責這項罪行是一種駭人的道德墮落行為。

w4606.en: Under the **tutelage** of a master chef, she quickly improved her cooking.
w4606.zh: 在一位大廚的指導下，她的廚藝迅速進步。

w4607.en: The villagers prayed to their **tutelar** spirit for a good harvest.
w4607.zh: 村民們向他們的守護神祈求豐收。

w4608.en: She accepted a **tutorship** at the college, guiding first-year students closely.
w4608.zh: 她接受了該學院的導師職位，密切指導一年級學生。

w4609.en: He felt a sudden **twinge** of guilt for forgetting his friend's birthday.
w4609.zh: 他因忘了朋友的生日而突然感到一陣內疚。

w4610.en: Heavy rain in spring is **typical** weather for this coastal region.
w4610.zh: 春季的大雨是這個沿海地區典型的天氣。

w4611.en: This old farmhouse perfectly **typifies** the simple rural life of the past.
w4611.zh: 這座老農舍完美地代表了過去簡樸的鄉村生活。

w4612.en: The editor found several **typographical** errors in the printed first edition.
w4612.zh: 編輯在印刷的第一版中發現了幾處排印錯誤。

w4613.en: Modern **typography** lets designers choose from thousands of different printed fonts.
w4613.zh: 現代印刷排版讓設計師能從數千種不同的印刷字體中挑選。

w4614.en: The villagers suffered under the **tyrannical** rule of a cruel lord.
w4614.zh: 村民們在一位殘暴領主的暴虐統治下受苦。

w4615.en: The people finally rose up to overthrow years of brutal **tyranny**.
w4615.zh: 人民最終起身推翻了多年的殘暴暴政。

w4616.en: As a complete **tyro** at chess, he lost his first ten games.
w4616.zh: 身為一個徹底的西洋棋新手，他輸掉了頭十盤棋。

w4617.en: Mobile phones have become **ubiquitous**, appearing in almost every home today.
w4617.zh: 手機已變得無所不在，如今幾乎出現在每個家庭中。

w4618.en: I suspect he had some **ulterior** motive for offering to help us.
w4618.zh: 我懷疑他主動提議幫我們是別有用心。

w4619.en: The team's **ultimate** goal is to win the national championship next year.
w4619.zh: 這支球隊的最終目標是明年贏得全國冠軍。

w4620.en: The workers issued an **ultimatum** demanding higher pay or they would strike.
w4620.zh: 工人們發出最後通牒，要求加薪，否則就罷工。

w4621.en: Ancient myths often described gods living in some distant **ultramundane** realm.
w4621.zh: 古代神話常描述眾神住在某個遙遠的、世界以外的國度。

w4622.en: The **ultramontane** villages lie on the southern side of the great mountains.
w4622.zh: 那些山那邊的村莊位於這座大山的南側。

w4623.en: We rested in the cool **umbrage** of an ancient spreading oak.
w4623.zh: 我們在一棵古老而枝葉伸展的橡樹涼爽的樹蔭下休息。

w4624.en: For some **unaccountable** reason, the machine suddenly stopped working last night.
w4624.zh: 由於某種無法解釋的原因，那臺機器昨晚突然停止運轉。

w4625.en: Her warm, **unaffected** manner quickly put every nervous guest at ease.
w4625.zh: 她溫暖而不做作的態度很快讓每位緊張的客人放鬆下來。

w4626.en: The committee reached a **unanimous** decision to approve the new park.
w4626.zh: 委員會達成一致決定，批准興建這座新公園。

w4627.en: There was complete **unanimity** among the members about the proposed plan.
w4627.zh: 成員們對所提出的計劃完全一致同意。

w4628.en: With so much rain, some flooding in the valley was sadly **unavoidable**.
w4628.zh: 雨下得這麼大，山谷裡發生一些水患遺憾地無法避免。

w4629.en: The pain in his injured knee finally became almost **unbearable**.
w4629.zh: 他受傷膝蓋的疼痛最終變得幾乎令人無法忍受。

w4630.en: Such rude behaviour is quite **unbecoming** to a respected public official.
w4630.zh: 這種無禮的舉止對一位受人尊敬的公職人員來說相當不得體。

w4631.en: His complete **unbelief** in the rumour kept him calm during the panic.
w4631.zh: 他對那謠言的全然不信讓他在恐慌中保持鎮定。

w4632.en: A good judge must remain **unbiased** when weighing both sides' arguments.
w4632.zh: 一位好法官在權衡雙方論點時必須保持公正無偏見。

w4633.en: Years of **unbridled** spending finally pushed the company into serious debt.
w4633.zh: 多年來毫無節制的花費最終使公司陷入嚴重債務。

w4634.en: It is **uncommon** to see snow this far south in winter.
w4634.zh: 在冬天於這麼南邊的地方看到雪是不尋常的。

w4635.en: Charging such desperate families that price was simply **unconscionable** and unfair.
w4635.zh: 向那些走投無路的家庭收取那種價格根本是無理而不公的。

w4636.en: The driver was knocked **unconscious** when his car struck the wall.
w4636.zh: 當汽車撞上牆壁時，駕駛被撞得失去知覺。

w4637.en: The priest performed the **unction**, anointing the sick man's forehead with oil.
w4637.zh: 神父進行了塗油禮，在病人的額頭上塗抹聖油。

w4638.en: The thick cream felt smooth and **unctuous** between her fingers.
w4638.zh: 那濃稠的乳霜在她指間感覺光滑似油。

w4639.en: The honest report finally **undeceived** investors about the firm's true finances.
w4639.zh: 這份誠實的報告終於讓投資人不再受公司真實財務狀況的蒙蔽。

w4640.en: The shop accidentally **undercharged** me, so I went back to pay more.
w4640.zh: 那家店不小心對我收費過低，所以我回去多付了錢。

w4641.en: The **underexposed** photographs came out too dark to show any detail.
w4641.zh: 那些曝光不足的照片洗出來太暗，看不出任何細節。

w4642.en: In winter she wears a warm **undergarment** beneath her woollen sweater.
w4642.zh: 冬天她會在羊毛衣裡面穿一件保暖的內衣。

w4643.en: Budget cuts forced the hospital to **underman** its busy night shift.
w4643.zh: 預算削減迫使醫院的繁忙夜班人員配備不足。

w4644.en: The new store will **undersell** its rivals to attract more customers.
w4644.zh: 這家新店將以低於對手的價格廉售，以吸引更多顧客。

w4645.en: The fishermen returned the **undersized** fish to the sea to grow.
w4645.zh: 漁民們把尺寸過小的魚放回海裡讓牠們長大。

w4646.en: His **underhanded** scheme to steal the contract eventually came to light.
w4646.zh: 他企圖竊取合約的狡詐陰謀最終被揭發。

w4647.en: Deep distrust seemed to **underlie** every decision the two countries made.
w4647.zh: 深層的不信任似乎構成了兩國所做每個決定的基礎。

w4648.en: The proud manager treated his **underlings** as if they were invisible.
w4648.zh: 那位自負的經理對待他的下屬就好像他們是隱形人。

w4649.en: Constant criticism can slowly **undermine** a young child's confidence.
w4649.zh: 不斷的批評會慢慢削弱一個小孩的自信。

w4650.en: Never **underrate** your opponent, however weak they may appear at first.
w4650.zh: 絕不要低估你的對手，不管他們起初看起來多麼弱小。

w4651.en: He tends to **understate** his achievements, rarely mentioning the awards he won.
w4651.zh: 他傾向於對自己的成就輕描淡寫，很少提及他贏得的獎項。

w4652.en: Many people **undervalue** the importance of regular sleep for good health.
w4652.zh: 許多人低估了規律睡眠對健康的重要性。

w4653.en: The novel explores the criminal **underworld** of a busy port city.
w4653.zh: 這部小說探討了一座繁忙港口城市的犯罪黑社會。

w4654.en: The bank agreed to **underwrite** the cost of the risky new venture.
w4654.zh: 銀行同意為這項高風險新事業的成本提供財力支援並承擔責任。

w4655.en: The report criticised the manager for placing **undue** pressure on his staff.
w4655.zh: 這份報告批評那位經理對員工施加過度的壓力。

w4656.en: Golden wheat fields seemed to **undulate** gently in the warm summer breeze.
w4656.zh: 金黃的麥田在溫暖的夏日微風中似乎輕輕地起伏波動。

w4657.en: The dancer's flowing scarf made soft, **undulous** shapes through the air.
w4657.zh: 舞者飄逸的圍巾在空中劃出柔和的、如波浪般的形狀。

w4658.en: The poor weather proved **unfavorable** for the outdoor wedding ceremony.
w4658.zh: 惡劣的天氣對這場戶外婚禮儀式來說很不利。

w4659.en: The tall bird moved with an **ungainly**, awkward walk across the mud.
w4659.zh: 那隻高大的鳥以一種笨拙不優雅的步態走過泥地。

w4660.en: The nurse gently rubbed a soothing **unguent** onto the burned skin.
w4660.zh: 護士輕輕地把一種舒緩的藥膏抹在燒傷的皮膚上。

w4661.en: Under the microscope we examined a single **unicellular** organism in pond water.
w4661.zh: 在顯微鏡下，我們檢視了池水中的一個單細胞生物。

w4662.en: The biologist noted the **univalence** of that particular chromosome during the study.
w4662.zh: 生物學家在研究中記下了那條特定染色體的單一性。

w4663.en: The new leader hoped to **unify** the divided nation under one flag.
w4663.zh: 這位新領袖希望將分裂的國家統一在一面旗幟之下。

w4664.en: Each handmade vase is **unique**, with its own colour and shape.
w4664.zh: 每個手工製作的花瓶都是獨一無二的，有著自己的顏色和形狀。

w4665.en: The whole choir sang the final chorus in perfect **unison**.
w4665.zh: 整個合唱團以完美的齊唱唱出最後的合唱段。

w4666.en: The two bells were **unisonant**, ringing at exactly the same clear pitch.
w4666.zh: 那兩座鐘是同音的，以完全相同的清亮音高鳴響。

w4667.en: The **Unitarian** church welcomed people of many different beliefs and backgrounds.
w4667.zh: 那座一位論派教會歡迎許多不同信仰與背景的人。

w4668.en: Selling these goods without a licence is completely **unlawful** here.
w4668.zh: 在這裡沒有執照販售這些商品是完全不合法的。

w4669.en: The new phone plan offers **unlimited** calls and text messages each month.
w4669.zh: 這個新手機方案每月提供無限制的通話和簡訊。

w4670.en: Her stiff, **unnatural** smile made the photograph look rather forced.
w4670.zh: 她僵硬、不自然的微笑讓那張照片看起來相當勉強。

w4671.en: We removed all the **unnecessary** details to make the report shorter.
w4671.zh: 我們刪除了所有不必要的細節，好讓報告更簡短。

w4672.en: The strange noise at night began to **unsettle** the whole family.
w4672.zh: 夜裡那奇怪的聲音開始讓全家人心神不寧。

w4673.en: The visitor admired the **unsophisticated** charm of the small fishing village.
w4673.zh: 這位訪客欣賞這個小漁村純樸不矯飾的魅力。

w4674.en: The survivors described the disaster as an **unspeakable** and lasting horror.
w4674.zh: 倖存者形容那場災難是一種難以言喻且揮之不去的恐怖。

w4675.en: The poet's **untimely** death cut short a remarkably promising career.
w4675.zh: 這位詩人英年早逝，中斷了一段格外有前途的事業。

w4676.en: Nothing **untoward** happened during the long flight, and we landed safely.
w4676.zh: 漫長的飛行中沒有發生任何不幸的事，我們平安降落了。

w4677.en: Standing before the vast canyon, she felt an **unutterable** sense of awe.
w4677.zh: 站在廣闊的峽谷前，她感到一種難以言喻的敬畏。

w4678.en: The huge old wardrobe was too **unwieldy** to carry up the stairs.
w4678.zh: 那個巨大的舊衣櫃太笨重了，無法搬上樓。

w4679.en: It would be **unwise** to invest all your savings in one risky company.
w4679.zh: 把你所有的積蓄投資在一家高風險公司是不明智的。

w4680.en: At dusk the farmer would **unyoke** the tired oxen and let them rest.
w4680.zh: 黃昏時農夫會卸下疲憊的牛，讓牠們休息。

w4681.en: The annual **upkeep** of such a large old house is very expensive.
w4681.zh: 這麼一棟大型老房子每年的維修費非常昂貴。

w4682.en: The coach began to **upbraid** the players for their careless mistakes.
w4682.zh: 教練開始為球員們粗心的失誤而責罵他們。

w4683.en: Fresh air flowed steadily up the mine's main **upcast** shaft.
w4683.zh: 新鮮空氣穩定地沿著礦坑的主要上風井向上流動。

w4684.en: The election caused a major political **upheaval** across the entire country.
w4684.zh: 這場選舉在全國各地引起了重大的政治動盪。

w4685.en: Powerful forces deep underground slowly **upheaved** the rocks into tall mountains.
w4685.zh: 地底深處的強大力量緩慢地將岩石隆起成高山。

w4686.en: Safety remained **uppermost** in the captain's mind throughout the difficult voyage.
w4686.zh: 在整段艱難的航程中，安全始終是船長心中最重要的考量。

w4687.en: The comedian's **uproarious** jokes had the whole audience laughing helplessly.
w4687.zh: 這位喜劇演員令人捧腹大笑的笑話讓全場觀眾笑到不行。

w4688.en: The storm could **uproot** even the largest and oldest trees in the park.
w4688.zh: 這場暴風雨甚至能將公園裡最大最老的樹連根拔起。

w4689.en: Economists predict a sharp **upturn** in the economy later this year.
w4689.zh: 經濟學家預測今年稍晚經濟會大幅好轉。

w4690.en: He greeted his rivals with surprising **urbanity** and polished good manners.
w4690.zh: 他以令人意外的儒雅和優雅的良好禮貌迎接他的對手。

w4691.en: A ragged little **urchin** begged for coins outside the busy station.
w4691.zh: 一個衣衫襤褸的小頑童在繁忙的車站外乞討零錢。

w4692.en: The doctor stressed the **urgency** of treating the infection without delay.
w4692.zh: 醫生強調了刻不容緩治療這個感染的緊迫性。

w4693.en: This dictionary explains the correct **usage** of many difficult English words.
w4693.zh: 這本字典解釋了許多艱難英語單字的正確用法。

w4694.en: The poor borrowers suffered under the lender's cruel, **usurious** interest rates.
w4694.zh: 貧窮的借款人在放款人殘酷的高利貸利率下受苦。

w4695.en: The ambitious general tried to **usurp** the throne from the rightful king.
w4695.zh: 那位野心勃勃的將軍企圖從合法的國王手中篡奪王位。

w4696.en: In ancient times, charging excessive interest, or **usury**, was strictly forbidden.
w4696.zh: 在古代，收取過高的利息，也就是放高利貸，是被嚴格禁止的。

w4697.en: His political views were shaped by the practical principles of **utilitarianism**.
w4697.zh: 他的政治觀點是由功利主義的務實原則所塑造的。

w4698.en: The new road greatly increased the **utility** of the remote farmland.
w4698.zh: 這條新路大大增加了這片偏遠農地的實用性。

w4699.en: The rescue team did their **utmost** to reach the trapped climbers quickly.
w4699.zh: 救援隊竭盡全力盡快抵達受困的登山者那裡。

w4700.en: Tenants must **vacate** the apartment by noon on the last day.
w4700.zh: 房客必須在最後一天中午前騰空這間公寓。

w4701.en: Parents are urged to **vaccinate** their children against measles before school.
w4701.zh: 當局敦促家長在孩子入學前為他們接種麻疹疫苗。

w4702.en: She continued to **vacillate** between studying medicine and pursuing music professionally.
w4702.zh: 她在攻讀醫學和專業從事音樂之間搖擺不定。

w4703.en: His **vacuous** stare suggested he had not understood the question at all.
w4703.zh: 他空洞的眼神顯示他根本沒聽懂這個問題。

w4704.en: Sound cannot travel through the empty **vacuum** of outer space.
w4704.zh: 聲音無法穿越外太空的真空。

w4705.en: The old **vagabond** wandered from town to town with all his belongings.
w4705.zh: 這個老流浪漢帶著他所有的家當從一個城鎮漂泊到另一個城鎮。

w4706.en: The police moved a homeless **vagrant** along from the shop doorway.
w4706.zh: 警察把一個無家可歸的流浪漢從商店門口趕走。

w4707.en: His endless boasting revealed an empty **vainglory** that few people admired.
w4707.zh: 他無止盡的吹噓暴露出一種少有人欣賞的空洞虛榮。

w4708.en: A clear stream wound slowly through the peaceful green **vale**.
w4708.zh: 一條清澈的溪流緩緩蜿蜒穿過寧靜的綠色山谷。

w4709.en: The retiring teacher gave a moving **valediction** to her grateful students.
w4709.zh: 這位退休教師向她感激的學生們發表了一段動人的告別辭。

w4710.en: As class **valedictorian**, she delivered an inspiring speech at the graduation ceremony.
w4710.zh: 作為畢業生致辭代表，她在畢業典禮上發表了一場鼓舞人心的演講。

w4711.en: In his **valedictory** address, the principal thanked everyone for thirty wonderful years.
w4711.zh: 在他的告別演說中，校長感謝大家三十個美好的年頭。

w4712.en: The scientist presented a **valid** argument supported by years of careful research.
w4712.zh: 這位科學家提出了一個由多年細心研究所支持、令人信服的論點。

w4713.en: The **valorous** soldier returned to rescue his wounded comrades under heavy fire.
w4713.zh: 那位英勇的士兵在猛烈砲火下返回救援他受傷的同袍。

w4714.en: The film's dull plot and **vapid** dialogue bored the entire audience.
w4714.zh: 這部電影沉悶的情節和乏味的對白讓全場觀眾感到無聊。

w4715.en: She used a small **vaporizer** to release soothing scent into the room.
w4715.zh: 她用一個小型噴霧器在房間裡釋放舒緩的香氣。

w4716.en: The weather in this region is highly **variable**, changing several times daily.
w4716.zh: 這個地區的天氣非常多變，一天會變化好幾次。

w4717.en: There was a clear **variance** between the two witnesses' accounts of events.
w4717.zh: 兩位證人對事件的陳述之間存在明顯的分歧。

w4718.en: The shop sells a cheaper **variant** of the popular branded sports shoe.
w4718.zh: 這家店販售那款熱門品牌運動鞋的較便宜變體版本。

w4719.en: There is wide **variation** in temperature between the day and the night.
w4719.zh: 白天與夜晚之間的溫度有很大的變化。

w4720.en: The gardener chose to **variegate** the border with many brightly coloured flowers.
w4720.zh: 園丁選擇用許多色彩鮮豔的花朵讓花壇邊緣繽紛多彩。

w4721.en: In medieval times, each **vassal** owed loyalty and service to his lord.
w4721.zh: 在中世紀，每位封臣都對其領主負有忠誠與效勞的義務。

w4722.en: My grandparents loved the songs and dances of old-fashioned **vaudeville** shows.
w4722.zh: 我的祖父母喜愛舊式歌舞雜耍表演的歌曲與舞蹈。

w4723.en: The study focused on the **vegetal** growth observed in the shaded forest floor.
w4723.zh: 這項研究著重於在陰暗林地觀察到的植物生長。

w4724.en: As a strict **vegetarian**, she never eats meat, fish, or poultry.
w4724.zh: 身為一個嚴格的素食者，她從不吃肉、魚或家禽。

w4725.en: After losing his job, he began to **vegetate** at home all day.
w4725.zh: 失業後，他開始整天待在家裡過著呆板懶散的生活。

w4726.en: Thick green **vegetation** covered the hillside after months of heavy rain.
w4726.zh: 連續數月的大雨後，茂密的綠色植物覆蓋了山坡。

w4727.en: The mild climate provides ideal **vegetative** conditions for growing tropical fruit.
w4727.zh: 溫和的氣候為種植熱帶水果提供了理想的植物生長條件。

w4728.en: She gave a **vehement** denial of all the charges against her.
w4728.zh: 她對所有針對她的指控做出了強烈的否認。

w4729.en: Scientists measured the exact **velocity** of the ball as it fell.
w4729.zh: 科學家測量了球下落時的精確速度。

w4730.en: The rose petals felt soft and **velvety** against her cheek.
w4730.zh: 玫瑰花瓣貼著她的臉頰，感覺柔軟如天鵝絨。

w4731.en: The **venal** official accepted bribes in exchange for ignoring the law.
w4731.zh: 那名貪贓的官員收受賄賂，以換取對法律的視而不見。

w4732.en: The craftsman produced small wooden toys that were easily **vendible** at markets.
w4732.zh: 這位工匠製作的小木製玩具在市集上很容易銷售。

w4733.en: The contract clearly set out the terms governing the **vendition** of the goods.
w4733.zh: 合約清楚地載明了規範這些貨物販賣的條款。

w4734.en: A street **vendor** sold hot roasted chestnuts on the cold corner.
w4734.zh: 一名街頭小販在寒冷的街角賣著熱騰騰的烤栗子。

w4735.en: Beneath his polite **veneer** lay a cold and calculating personality.
w4735.zh: 在他禮貌的虛飾之下，藏著一種冷酷而精於算計的性格。

w4736.en: The **venerable** professor had taught at the university for over fifty years.
w4736.zh: 這位德高望重的教授在這所大學任教超過五十年。

w4737.en: Many cultures **venerate** their elders for the wisdom they have gathered.
w4737.zh: 許多文化敬重他們的長者，因為長者累積了智慧。

w4738.en: Doctors warned the public about the spread of **venereal** diseases.
w4738.zh: 醫生們警告大眾要注意性病的傳播。

w4739.en: Arriving a few minutes late was only a **venial** and forgivable error.
w4739.zh: 遲到幾分鐘只是個輕微而可原諒的過錯。

w4740.en: The restaurant serves roast **venison** with a rich berry sauce in autumn.
w4740.zh: 這家餐廳在秋天供應佐以濃郁莓果醬的烤鹿肉。

w4741.en: A single drop of the snake's **venom** can be extremely dangerous.
w4741.zh: 這條蛇的一滴毒液就可能極其危險。

w4742.en: The nurse drew a sample of dark **venous** blood from his arm.
w4742.zh: 護士從他的手臂抽取了一份暗紅色的靜脈血樣本。

w4743.en: A **veracious** account of the events helped clear the innocent man's name.
w4743.zh: 對事件的真實陳述幫助洗清了這位無辜者的名聲。

w4744.en: The judge questioned the **veracity** of the witness's surprising statement.
w4744.zh: 法官質疑這位證人那令人意外陳述的真實性。

w4745.en: The clerk copied the angry speech **verbatim** into the official record.
w4745.zh: 書記員把那段憤怒的演說一字不差地抄進官方記錄。

w4746.en: The report could be far shorter without so much unnecessary **verbiage**.
w4746.zh: 如果沒有這麼多不必要的冗詞，這份報告本可以短得多。

w4747.en: His **verbose** writing buried a simple idea under endless extra words.
w4747.zh: 他冗長囉嗦的寫作把一個簡單的概念埋在無盡的贅字之下。

w4748.en: After the spring rains, the **verdant** hills were covered in fresh grass.
w4748.zh: 春雨過後，蒼翠的山丘覆蓋著新生的青草。

w4749.en: The bank requires further **verification** of your address before opening an account.
w4749.zh: 銀行在開戶前要求對你的地址做進一步的證實。

w4750.en: Please **verify** the figures carefully before submitting the final report.
w4750.zh: 在提交最終報告前，請仔細核對這些數字。

w4751.en: He **verily** believed that hard work alone would secure his future success.
w4751.zh: 他真心相信光憑努力工作就能確保他未來的成功。

w4752.en: The documentary explores eternal **verities** about love, loss, and the human condition.
w4752.zh: 這部紀錄片探討關於愛、失去與人類處境的永恆真理。

w4753.en: Farmers spent the summer trying to keep **vermin** away from their stored grain.
w4753.zh: 農夫整個夏天都試圖讓害蟲遠離他們儲存的穀物。

w4754.en: The novel was written in the local **vernacular** rather than formal standard English.
w4754.zh: 這部小說是用當地方言而非正式標準英語寫成的。

w4755.en: The **vernal** equinox marks the moment when spring officially begins each year.
w4755.zh: 春分標誌著每年春天正式開始的時刻。

w4756.en: This **versatile** kitchen tool can chop, slice, and grate a wide range of foods.
w4756.zh: 這款多用途的廚房工具可以切碎、切片並研磨各種食物。

w4757.en: The latest **version** of the software fixes several bugs from earlier releases.
w4757.zh: 這款軟體的最新版本修正了先前版本中的數個錯誤。

w4758.en: Each **vertex** of the triangle was clearly labelled with a different letter.
w4758.zh: 三角形的每個頂點都清楚地標上了不同的字母。

w4759.en: The cliff rose in a sheer **vertical** wall above the crashing waves below.
w4759.zh: 懸崖在下方拍岸的海浪之上形成一道陡峭的垂直牆面。

w4760.en: Standing near the edge of the tower, she suddenly felt a wave of **vertigo**.
w4760.zh: 站在塔的邊緣附近，她突然感到一陣暈眩。

w4761.en: Archaeologists found only a faint **vestige** of the ancient city's outer walls.
w4761.zh: 考古學家只找到這座古城外牆殘餘的微弱遺跡。

w4762.en: The priest put on his ceremonial **vestments** before beginning the morning service.
w4762.zh: 神父在開始晨間禮拜之前穿上了他的祭袍。

w4763.en: The president decided to **veto** the bill despite strong support in parliament.
w4763.zh: 儘管該法案在議會獲得強力支持，總統仍決定否決它。

w4764.en: Many fans enjoy a **vicarious** thrill watching their favourite athletes win championships.
w4764.zh: 許多球迷在觀看他們最愛的運動員贏得冠軍時享受著感同身受的興奮。

w4765.en: The **viceroy** governed the distant colony on behalf of the absent monarch.
w4765.zh: 總督代表不在當地的君主治理這個遙遠的殖民地。

w4766.en: Through all the **vicissitudes** of life, she never lost her sense of humour.
w4766.zh: 歷經人生種種變化無常，她從未失去她的幽默感。

w4767.en: Several companies now **vie** with each other for the same shrinking market.
w4767.zh: 如今好幾家公司彼此競爭同一個不斷萎縮的市場。

w4768.en: The constant **vigilance** of the guards prevented any intruders from entering the compound.
w4768.zh: 警衛持續的警戒防止了任何入侵者進入這座院區。

w4769.en: Airport staff remained **vigilant** for any unattended bags throughout the busy holiday season.
w4769.zh: 在繁忙的假期期間，機場人員對任何無人看管的行李保持警覺。

w4770.en: The book opens with a charming **vignette** of village life in the 1950s.
w4770.zh: 這本書以一段描繪一九五零年代鄉村生活的迷人小插曲開場。

w4771.en: The general insisted that even the strongest enemy army was ultimately **vincible**.
w4771.zh: 將軍堅稱即使是最強大的敵軍最終也是可征服的。

w4772.en: New evidence finally helped **vindicate** the man who had been wrongly convicted.
w4772.zh: 新的證據最終幫助證明了這名遭錯誤定罪的男子是無辜的。

w4773.en: The court took a **vindicatory** approach, imposing harsh penalties on repeat offenders.
w4773.zh: 法院採取懲罰性的做法，對累犯施加嚴厲的刑罰。

w4774.en: Her lengthy **vindicative** essay sought to defend her mentor's controversial decisions.
w4774.zh: 她那篇冗長且起辯護作用的文章試圖為她導師具爭議的決定辯護。

w4775.en: They strolled through the sunny **vinery**, admiring rows of ripening purple grapes.
w4775.zh: 他們漫步穿過陽光普照的葡萄園，欣賞著一排排逐漸成熟的紫色葡萄。

w4776.en: The musician carefully tuned her antique **viol** before the early music concert.
w4776.zh: 這位音樂家在古樂音樂會前仔細為她的古董六弦提琴調音。

w4777.en: She plays the **viola**, whose warm tone sits between the violin and cello.
w4777.zh: 她演奏中提琴，其溫暖的音色介於小提琴與大提琴之間。

w4778.en: The court fined the repeat **violator** heavily for ignoring the safety regulations again.
w4778.zh: 法院對這名再次無視安全規定的累犯處以重罰。

w4779.en: Driving without a licence is a serious **violation** of the country's traffic laws.
w4779.zh: 無照駕駛是嚴重違反該國交通法規的行為。

w4780.en: He carried his **violoncello** carefully onto the stage before the evening recital.
w4780.zh: 在晚間獨奏會前，他小心地把大提琴搬上了舞台。

w4781.en: The play depicts a fearsome **virago** who terrorises everyone in the small town.
w4781.zh: 這齣戲描繪一名令小鎮上每個人都聞風喪膽的潑婦。

w4782.en: The statue portrays a **virile** young warrior holding a spear and shield.
w4782.zh: 這座雕像描繪一名手持長矛與盾牌、充滿男子氣概的年輕戰士。

w4783.en: The wealthy collector filled his mansion with rare **virtu** from across Europe.
w4783.zh: 這位富有的收藏家用來自全歐洲的稀有美術品填滿了他的宅邸。

w4784.en: Years of overspending left the company in a state of **virtual** bankruptcy.
w4784.zh: 多年的超支使這家公司陷入了實質上破產的狀態。

w4785.en: The young pianist is already regarded as a **virtuoso** by leading music critics.
w4785.zh: 這位年輕鋼琴家已被頂尖樂評人視為演奏名家。

w4786.en: Scientists studied the surprising **virulence** of the new strain spreading across the region.
w4786.zh: 科學家研究了在該地區蔓延的新病毒株出人意料的致命性。

w4787.en: Doctors warned that this **virulent** infection could spread rapidly without proper precautions.
w4787.zh: 醫生警告說，若無適當的預防措施，這種致命的感染可能迅速蔓延。

w4788.en: A look of deep sorrow passed across the old man's weathered **visage**.
w4788.zh: 一抹深沉的悲傷掠過這位老人飽經風霜的臉龐。

w4789.en: The estate had belonged to the same **viscount** for over three centuries.
w4789.zh: 這座莊園歸同一位子爵所有已超過三個世紀。

w4790.en: From the hilltop, visitors enjoy a breathtaking **vista** of the entire valley.
w4790.zh: 從山頂上，遊客可以欣賞到整個山谷令人屏息的壯觀景色。

w4791.en: The teacher used colourful charts as a **visual** aid during the science lesson.
w4791.zh: 老師在自然課中使用色彩繽紛的圖表作為視覺輔助材料。

w4792.en: Athletes often **visualize** themselves winning before they step onto the field.
w4792.zh: 運動員在踏上球場之前經常把自己獲勝的情景形象化。

w4793.en: Despite his age, the old gardener still works with remarkable energy and **vitality**.
w4793.zh: 儘管年事已高，這位老園丁工作起來仍展現出驚人的精力與活力。

w4794.en: A good leader can **vitalize** a struggling team and restore its confidence.
w4794.zh: 一位優秀的領導者能夠為陷入困境的團隊注入活力並恢復其信心。

w4795.en: One careless error can completely **vitiate** the results of a long experiment.
w4795.zh: 一個粗心的錯誤就可能完全損害一項長期實驗的結果。

w4796.en: The committee considered his careless behaviour thoroughly **vituperable** and worthy of public blame.
w4796.zh: 委員會認為他粗心的行為完全是罪有應得，理應受到公眾的指責。

w4797.en: Her natural **vivacity** made her the most popular guest at every party.
w4797.zh: 她天生的活潑使她成為每場派對上最受歡迎的賓客。

w4798.en: A skilful storyteller can **vivify** even the dullest historical events for young readers.
w4798.zh: 一位技巧高超的說書人能為年輕讀者把即使最枯燥的歷史事件變得生動。

w4799.en: Many activists strongly oppose the **vivisection** of animals for medical research.
w4799.zh: 許多行動人士強烈反對為了醫學研究而對動物進行活體解剖。

w4800.en: The dictionary lists each **vocable** along with its pronunciation and main meanings.
w4800.zh: 這本字典列出每個單字及其發音與主要意義。

w4801.en: In Latin, the **vocative** case is used when directly addressing a person.
w4801.zh: 在拉丁文中，呼格用於直接稱呼某人時。

w4802.en: The sudden **vociferance** of the crowd drowned out the speaker's quiet voice.
w4802.zh: 群眾突然的喧嚷淹沒了演講者輕柔的聲音。

w4803.en: The angry protesters began to **vociferate** their demands outside the government building.
w4803.zh: 憤怒的抗議者開始在政府大樓外吶喊著他們的要求。

w4804.en: A **vociferous** group of fans demanded that the manager be replaced immediately.
w4804.zh: 一群喧嚷的球迷要求立即撤換這位經理。

w4805.en: Tight jeans were briefly in **vogue** before looser styles became popular again.
w4805.zh: 緊身牛仔褲短暫流行過一陣子，之後較寬鬆的款式才再度受歡迎。

w4806.en: Ancient legends often describe **volant** creatures soaring high above the mountain peaks.
w4806.zh: 古老的傳說常描述能飛的生物高高翱翔於山峰之上。

w4807.en: Petrol is highly **volatile**, so it must be stored away from open flames.
w4807.zh: 汽油極易揮發，所以必須遠離明火儲存。

w4808.en: She left the company entirely of her own **volition**, without any outside pressure.
w4808.zh: 她完全是出於自己的意志離開了公司，沒有任何外來的壓力。

w4809.en: The teacher explained that certain **volitive** verb forms express a wish or request.
w4809.zh: 老師解釋說某些表示祈望的動詞形式用以表達願望或請求。

w4810.en: The **voluble** tour guide chatted happily about local history for the entire trip.
w4810.zh: 這位滔滔不絕的導遊在整趟旅程中愉快地談論著當地歷史。

w4811.en: The painting depicts a garden full of **voluptuous** flowers in rich, glowing colours.
w4811.zh: 這幅畫描繪一座滿是濃郁、鮮豔色彩、令人陶醉的花朵的花園。

w4812.en: As a child, she was a **voracious** reader who finished several books each week.
w4812.zh: 小時候，她是個如飢似渴的讀者，每週讀完好幾本書。

w4813.en: The small boat was nearly pulled under by a powerful **vortex** of swirling water.
w4813.zh: 那艘小船差點被一股強勁的漩渦捲入水下。

w4814.en: He became a devoted **votary** of the cause, attending every meeting and rally.
w4814.zh: 他成了這項志業的忠實信徒，參加每一場會議與集會。

w4815.en: Pilgrims lit **votive** candles at the shrine to express their heartfelt prayers.
w4815.zh: 朝聖者在聖壇前點燃許願蠟燭以表達他們衷心的祈禱。

w4816.en: The reviewer criticised the film for the **vulgarity** of its crude humour.
w4816.zh: 這名評論者批評這部電影粗俗的低級幽默。

w4817.en: Young children are especially **vulnerable** to infection during the cold winter months.
w4817.zh: 幼童在寒冷的冬季月份特別容易受到感染。

w4818.en: The charity provides shelter and food for any homeless **waif** found on the streets.
w4818.zh: 這家慈善機構為街頭上發現的任何無家可歸的流浪兒提供庇護與食物。

w4819.en: He wore a smart **waistcoat** beneath his jacket for the formal dinner.
w4819.zh: 他在正式晚宴上於外套底下穿了一件雅致的背心。

w4820.en: The bank agreed to **waive** the monthly fee for the first three months.
w4820.zh: 銀行同意在前三個月免除每月手續費。

w4821.en: The early settlers sometimes traded goods for strings of **wampum** with local tribes.
w4821.zh: 早期的拓荒者有時會用貨物與當地部落交換一串串的貝殼串。

w4822.en: Public interest in the scandal began to **wane** after only a few weeks.
w4822.zh: 大眾對這樁醜聞的興趣僅僅幾週後就開始減退。

w4823.en: The teacher could not tolerate the careless **wantonness** of the unruly students.
w4823.zh: 老師無法容忍那些不守規矩學生那種輕率的放縱。

w4824.en: The ancient tribe was famous for its fierce and **warlike** reputation.
w4824.zh: 這個古老的部落以其凶猛好戰的名聲而聞名。

w4825.en: A gentle breeze sent tiny **wavelets** rippling across the calm surface of the lake.
w4825.zh: 一陣微風使細小的漣漪蕩漾過平靜的湖面。

w4826.en: His **weak-kneed** response to the crisis disappointed everyone who had trusted his leadership.
w4826.zh: 他對這場危機軟弱的回應令所有信任他領導能力的人感到失望。

w4827.en: The fall left an angry red **weal** across the back of his hand.
w4827.zh: 那一摔在他的手背上留下了一道紅腫的傷痕。

w4828.en: Most farmers **wean** their lambs from the ewe at around three months old.
w4828.zh: 大多數農夫在羔羊約三個月大時讓牠們從母羊那裡斷奶。

w4829.en: The long lecture grew increasingly **wearisome** as the afternoon slowly dragged on.
w4829.zh: 隨著下午緩慢地過去，這場冗長的演講變得越來越令人厭倦。

w4830.en: They paused for just a **wee** moment before continuing their long mountain hike.
w4830.zh: 他們只停下來極短的一會兒，便繼續他們漫長的登山健行。

w4831.en: Her **well-bred** manners impressed everyone she met at the formal reception.
w4831.zh: 她有教養的舉止令她在正式招待會上遇到的每個人都印象深刻。

w4832.en: The village honoured the quiet **well-doer** who had helped so many needy families.
w4832.zh: 村莊表揚了這位幫助過許多貧困家庭、默默做好事的人。

w4833.en: Their **well-to-do** neighbours owned two large houses and several expensive cars.
w4833.zh: 他們那些富有的鄰居擁有兩棟大房子與好幾輛昂貴的汽車。

w4834.en: Police are still trying to discover the **whereabouts** of the missing witness.
w4834.zh: 警方仍在試圖查明這名失蹤證人的下落。

w4835.en: The bridge suddenly collapsed, **whereupon** the entire village rushed to help the trapped drivers.
w4835.zh: 橋樑突然倒塌，於是整個村莊的人都趕去幫助受困的駕駛人。

w4836.en: The loyal dog follows its owner **wherever** she goes around the busy farm.
w4836.zh: 這隻忠心的狗無論主人在這座繁忙的農場走到哪裡都跟著她。

w4837.en: The poor villagers lacked the tools **wherewith** to rebuild their flooded homes.
w4837.zh: 貧窮的村民缺乏用以重建被洪水淹沒家園的工具。

w4838.en: The delicious smell of roast beef began to **whet** everyone's appetite for dinner.
w4838.zh: 烤牛肉的美味香氣開始勾起每個人對晚餐的食欲。

w4839.en: The artist is known for her **whimsical** drawings of dancing animals and flying houses.
w4839.zh: 這位藝術家以她那些跳舞的動物與飛翔的房屋的異想天開畫作而聞名。

w4840.en: The frightened puppy began to **whine** softly when its owner left the room.
w4840.zh: 當主人離開房間時，受驚的小狗開始輕聲地哀鳴。

w4841.en: The success of the project depends **wholly** on careful planning and steady teamwork.
w4841.zh: 這項專案的成功完全取決於審慎的規劃與穩定的團隊合作。

w4842.en: As chief executive, she **wields** enormous influence over the company's future direction.
w4842.zh: 身為執行長，她對公司未來的方向行使著巨大的影響力。

w4843.en: The clever fox used every **wile** it knew to escape the hunters.
w4843.zh: 那隻聰明的狐狸用盡牠所知的每一個詭計來逃離獵人。

w4844.en: The child gave the visitors a **winsome** smile that instantly melted their hearts.
w4844.zh: 那孩子給了訪客一個迷人的微笑，瞬間融化了他們的心。

w4845.en: They huddled indoors during the long, **wintry** evenings of late December.
w4845.zh: 在十二月下旬漫長、寒冷如冬的夜晚裡，他們擠在室內取暖。

w4846.en: Despite his slim build, the climber proved surprisingly strong and **wiry**.
w4846.zh: 儘管身材瘦削，這名登山者卻出人意料地強壯而堅韌。

w4847.en: In the old folktale, the villagers accused the strange woman of practising **witchcraft**.
w4847.zh: 在這則古老的民間故事中，村民指控那名奇怪的女子施行巫術。

w4848.en: His **witless** comment during the meeting embarrassed both himself and his colleagues.
w4848.zh: 他在會議中那句愚笨的評論讓他自己和同事都感到尷尬。

w4849.en: The clever debater easily silenced the boastful **witling** with a single sharp remark.
w4849.zh: 這位聰明的辯論者用一句尖銳的話便輕易讓那個假作聰明的人啞口無言。

w4850.en: The host kept the audience laughing with one clever **witticism** after another.
w4850.zh: 主持人用一句接一句巧妙的妙語逗得觀眾笑個不停。

w4851.en: He **wittingly** ignored the warning signs and continued hiking into the storm.
w4851.zh: 他有意地無視警告標誌，繼續往暴風雨中健行。

w4852.en: The hot summer sun can quickly **wizen** fruit left out in the open.
w4852.zh: 炎熱的夏日陽光能很快讓暴露在外的水果乾癟。

w4853.en: A thin, **wizen-faced** old man sold newspapers on the corner every morning.
w4853.zh: 一個瘦削、面孔乾瘦的老人每天早上在街角賣報紙。

w4854.en: Every honest **working-man** in the town deserves a fair and decent wage.
w4854.zh: 鎮上每一位誠實、有工作的人都應得到公平而體面的薪資。

w4855.en: The carpenter finished the cabinet in a neat and thoroughly **workmanlike** manner.
w4855.zh: 這位木匠以俐落且技術純熟的方式完成了這個櫥櫃。

w4856.en: The antique table was admired for its fine craftsmanship and delicate **workmanship**.
w4856.zh: 這張古董桌子因其精緻的工藝與細膩的手藝而備受讚賞。

w4857.en: The two departments continued to **wrangle** over the limited annual budget for months.
w4857.zh: 這兩個部門就有限的年度預算持續爭論了好幾個月。

w4858.en: The powerful storm threatened to **wreak** havoc on the small coastal village.
w4858.zh: 這場強烈的風暴可能對這個沿海小村莊造成嚴重的破壞。

w4859.en: The rebels fought hard to **wrest** control of the city from the army.
w4859.zh: 叛軍奮力從軍隊手中奪取這座城市的控制權。

w4860.en: The film captures the **wretchedness** of families living in the overcrowded slums.
w4860.zh: 這部電影捕捉了住在過度擁擠貧民窟中的家庭的不幸。

w4861.en: The injured player began to **writhe** in pain on the muddy pitch.
w4861.zh: 受傷的球員開始在泥濘的球場上痛苦地扭動。

w4862.en: Her elegant **writing** filled every page of the old leather-bound diary.
w4862.zh: 她優雅的筆跡填滿了那本舊皮革裝訂日記的每一頁。

w4863.en: He gave a **wry** smile when asked how his disastrous interview had gone.
w4863.zh: 當被問到他那場災難般的面試進行得如何時，他露出一抹解嘲的微笑。

w4864.en: The farmer sold a healthy **yearling** at the regional livestock market last week.
w4864.zh: 這位農夫上週在區域牲畜市場賣掉了一頭健康的一歲幼畜。

w4865.en: The political **zealot** refused to listen to any opinion that differed from his own.
w4865.zh: 這名政治狂熱者拒絕聆聽任何與他自己不同的意見。

w4866.en: The exhibition perfectly captures the rebellious **zeitgeist** of the late nineteen-sixties.
w4866.zh: 這場展覽完美捕捉了一九六零年代末叛逆的時代精神。

w4867.en: The author reached the **zenith** of her career when she won the international prize.
w4867.zh: 當這位作家贏得國際大獎時，她達到了職業生涯的頂峰。

w4868.en: A gentle **zephyr** drifted through the orchard, carrying the scent of ripe apples.
w4868.zh: 一陣輕柔的微風飄過果園，帶來成熟蘋果的香氣。

w4869.en: Ancient astronomers divided the sky into the twelve signs of the **zodiac**.
w4869.zh: 古代天文學家將天空劃分為黃道十二宮的十二個星座。
